**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores). Fiscal year ends March 31.

## 3. Solvency Usability Check

| Requirement | Available? (Y/N) | Source | Why It Matters |
|---|---|---|---|
| Balance sheet (recent) | **Y** | Consolidated audited Balance Sheet as at Mar 31, 2026 — Preliminary Interim Report (May-13-2026); cross-checked against Capital IQ Balance Sheet tab. Total borrowings ₹4,817 cr; total equity ₹12,734 cr; total assets ₹52,309 cr. | Debt, cash, equity base |
| Debt note (amounts by type) | **Partial** | MD&A table in Annual Report (Jun-06-2026) shows short-term debt ₹678 cr, current portion of long-term debt ₹2,795 cr, long-term debt ₹1,344 cr, total ₹4,817 cr. NCDs aggregating ₹2,300 cr are noted. Breakdown by individual instrument (coupon, security, seniority per NCD) is referenced to the CG Report but the full debenture schedule is inside the annual report PDF. Balance Sheet shows lease liabilities (current ₹195 cr + non-current ₹603 cr = ₹798 cr). Capital IQ Capital Structure Summary tab returned "No Data Available." | The debt stack and seniority |
| Maturity schedule | **Y** | Annual Report (Jun-06-2026), MD&A p.220 — explicit maturity profile: within 1 yr ₹3,657 cr; 1–2 yr ₹1,357 cr; 2–5 yr ₹11 cr; above 5 yr ₹243 cr; total ₹5,269 cr (including accrued interest on current maturities). Also: USD 127 mn 5.875% Senior Notes matured and repaid in May 2025. | The maturity wall and refinancing exposure |
| Cash flow statement | **Y** | Preliminary Interim Report (May-13-2026), p.3 — full consolidated cash flow statement; CFO ₹14,981 cr, capex ₹2,248 cr, financing outflow ₹5,223 cr (including debt repayment). Interest paid ₹884 cr disclosed as a supplemental item. Capital IQ Cash Flow tab confirms figures. | CFO/FCF for runway and coverage |
| Committed / undrawn facility detail | **Y** | Annual Report (Jun-06-2026), MD&A p.220: fund-based working capital limit ₹4,000 cr of which ₹3,750 cr unutilized; non-fund-based limit ₹4,500 cr. Both are secured by hypothecation of current assets. Iveco acquisition: committed loan facility of EUR 3.8 bn arranged at TMLCVB (Netherlands subsidiary), backed by ₹1.9 bn corporate guarantee + ₹1.9 bn letter of comfort from TML, with a 12-month availability period and 1-year post-drawdown tenor. This Iveco facility has NOT yet been drawn as of Mar 31, 2026. | True liquidity beyond cash |
| Interest expense detail | **Y** | P&L shows finance costs ₹874 cr for FY26. Cash flow supplemental shows interest paid ₹884 cr. Annual Report MD&A confirms reduction from ₹1,079 cr (FY25 9M) to ₹874 cr. Coverage ratios are calculable. Capital IQ Ratios tab: EBIT/Interest = 9.18x; EBITDA/Interest = 11.40x; (EBITDA-capex)/Interest = 9.89x. | Coverage ratios |
| Covenant disclosure | **Partial** | Annual Report (Jun-06-2026), MD&A Loan Covenants section (pp.220–221): states that certain financing arrangements include financial covenants to "maintain certain net-worth, liability and debt related ratios." Management states compliance with all covenants as of FY26. Company monitors compliance on an ongoing basis. However, the actual covenant thresholds (specific net-worth floor, leverage cap, coverage floor) are NOT disclosed in the data pool — they are described qualitatively only. | Headroom to a breach |
| Lease detail (operating/finance) | **Partial** | Balance sheet shows lease liabilities: non-current ₹603 cr, current ₹195 cr, total ₹798 cr. Right-of-use assets ₹812 cr. Cash flow shows lease charges ₹65 cr and amortisation of ROU assets embedded in D&A. No separate schedule of operating vs finance lease split or individual maturity breakdown is available in the extracted text. Under Ind AS 116 (IFRS 16-equivalent), all significant leases are capitalized. | Debt-like obligations |
| Pension / OPEB funded status | **N** | Capital IQ Pension/OPEB tab: "No Data Available." Annual Report does not surface a defined benefit pension obligation in the extracted sections. TMCV is a commercial vehicle manufacturer with a workforce in India; gratuity and provident fund provisions are standard under Indian law but their funded status and any defined-benefit shortfall are not extractable from the available data pool. | Off-balance-sheet obligation |
| Commitments & contingencies note | **Partial** | Annual Report (Jun-06-2026): (1) Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations for end-of-life vehicles (effective Apr 1, 2025) — cost cannot be reliably estimated, no provision made; (2) Stamp duty ₹962 cr on demerger land transfers recognized as exceptional items; (3) Lease liabilities ₹798 cr; (4) Iveco corporate guarantee EUR 1.9 bn + letter of comfort EUR 1.9 bn given by TML. Full contingencies note (disputed tax, litigation, guarantees by amount) is referenced in the Annual Report but the specific contingent-liability amounts table is not separately confirmed in the extracted text — it exists in the filing but was not surfaced in the extracted pages reviewed. | Guarantees, LCs, litigation, tax claims |
| Credit ratings | **Y** | Annual Report (Jun-06-2026), Board's Report / Finance & Credit Rating (p.117–118): CRISIL AA+ Stable, ICRA AA+ Stable, CARE AA+ Stable (all three leading India agencies); S&P BBB Stable (investment grade, covers TML CV Holdings Pte. and TML CV Holdings B.V. in Singapore/Netherlands). Rating agencies noted the company's stronger business risk profile, solid financial risk profile, and robust FCF. S&P explicitly noted the proposed Iveco acquisition could be "neutral" for the credit rating. | Refinancing access and cost |
| EBITDA base (for stress test) | **Y** | Underlying EBITDA ₹10,314 cr (12.3% margin) for FY26 per MD&A. Capital IQ reports EBITDA ₹9,965 cr (slightly different — includes items that management excludes). Both measures available; stress test can be built on either. Earnings module (01_historical-financials.md) confirms the EBITDA base and its derivation. | Required for the survival stress test |
| Business type (operating / bank / insurer / REIT / HoldCo-OpCo) | **Y** | TMCV is an operating company — India's largest commercial vehicle manufacturer. Not a bank, insurer, or REIT. However, a HoldCo/OpCo element exists: TML CV Holdings Pte. Ltd. (Singapore) and TML CV Holdings B.V. (Netherlands) are holding entities that have arranged the EUR 3.8 bn Iveco acquisition facility at the subsidiary level, backed by a corporate guarantee and letter of comfort from the parent TML. The Iveco deal (if completed) introduces structural subordination considerations. Pre-Iveco, this is a straightforward operating company. | Selects the correct framework |
| Revolver terms + availability / borrowing base | **Y** | Fund-based working capital limits: ₹4,000 cr (consortium of banks), ₹3,750 cr unutilized as of Mar 31, 2026. Secured by hypothecation of entire current assets (inventory, receivables, book debts). Renewed annually. Non-fund-based (LCs, guarantees): ₹4,500 cr. No borrowing-base formula is disclosed — the facility appears to be a standard working capital line, not a borrowing-base revolver. | Determines usable liquidity and springing covenants |
| Covenant EBITDA definition (addbacks / caps) | **N** | Annual Report (Jun-06-2026) Loan Covenants section states only that financial covenants include "net-worth, liability and debt related ratios" — no EBITDA-based covenant is explicitly named, no addback definition is disclosed, and no specific threshold or headroom figure is provided. It is not determinable whether any covenant uses a management-adjusted EBITDA definition. | Prevents "fake headroom" |
| HoldCo / OpCo structure disclosure | **Partial** | Annual Report confirms TML CV Holdings Pte. Ltd. (Singapore, 100% subsidiary) and TML CV Holdings B.V. (Netherlands, step-down) are the acquisition vehicles for Iveco. The EUR 3.8 bn committed loan is arranged at TMLCVB and backed by a corporate guarantee (EUR 1.9 bn) and letter of comfort (EUR 1.9 bn) from TML. TMF Holdings Limited (TMFHL) is a wholly-owned subsidiary being merged into TML (scheme ongoing). Material structural subordination from these entities is pre-Iveco limited; post-Iveco closure it becomes significant. | Structural subordination and upstreaming |
| Hedging / swaps disclosure | **Partial** | Annual Report references commodity and FX hedges in the context of management's Underlying EBITDA definition (MTM gains/losses on hedges are excluded from underlying). Finance costs of ₹874 cr are net of any interest rate benefit. No specific interest rate swap schedule, notional amounts, or fixed-rate vs floating-rate composition of existing debt is available in the extracted data pool. | Floating-rate exposure net of hedges |
| Change-of-control / cross-default / rating triggers | **Partial** | Annual Report Loan Covenants section (p.220) states that certain financing arrangements require "prior lender consent for undertaking new projects, issuing new securities, changes in management, mergers, sales of undertakings, material impairments and investments in subsidiaries." The Iveco merger (still awaiting French and Spanish regulatory approvals) has required approvals from lenders — the filing states "all approvals required so far have been timely received." No explicit cross-default or rating-trigger provisions are disclosed. | Hidden accelerants to distress |

## 4A. Jurisdiction & Reporting Regime

| Item | Detected Value | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Primary listing country | India | Listed on NSE (NSEI: TMCV) and BSE effective November 12, 2025 [FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report p.117] |
| Exchange | National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) and BSE Limited | Annual Report, Corporate Information; ticker NSEI:TMCV throughout all Capital IQ files |
| Filing regime | India / SEBI-LODR (SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements Regulations, 2015) | Board's Report cites Regulation 33 for quarterly results; Regulation 34(1) for the Annual Report; filed to both BSE and NSE [Annual Report cover letter, Jun 6, 2026] |
| Reporting standard | Ind AS (Indian Accounting Standards — effectively IFRS-converged) | Annual Report states "prepared in compliance with the applicable provisions of the Act and as stipulated under Regulation 33 of SEBI Listing Regulations as well as in accordance with the Indian Accounting Standards" [Board's Report, p.118]; Ind AS 116 (leases), Ind AS 37 (provisions) apply |
| Reporting currency | INR (Indian Rupees, crores) | All filings report in ₹ crores; Capital IQ balance sheet tab confirms "Currency: INR" |

Downstream agents must cite local-equivalent documents by their actual names (e.g., "SEBI LODR Reg 33 Preliminary Interim Report, year ended Mar 31, 2026, filed May 13, 2026, p.3" not "10-K" or "10-Q"). Credit ratings are CRISIL/ICRA/CARE/India Ratings (domestic), not only Moody's/S&P/Fitch. Borrowings notes use Ind AS nomenclature; operating-lease liabilities are on-balance-sheet under Ind AS 116. All figures carry INR denomination. Any reference to EUR amounts (Iveco deal) must include an FX rate.

---

## 5. Partial-Data Flags

| Missing Data | Applies? (Y/N) | Affected Agents | Cap Applied |
|---|---|---|---|
| No debt maturity schedule | **N** | 02, 06 | Not applicable — maturity schedule is available from the Annual Report MD&A (within 1 yr ₹3,657 cr; 1–2 yr ₹1,357 cr; 2–5 yr ₹11 cr; >5 yr ₹243 cr) |
| No covenant disclosure | **Y** | 04, 06 | Covenants exist and compliance is stated (FY26: all covenants met), but exact thresholds for net-worth, liability, and debt ratios are not disclosed. Covenant headroom = "Not assessable at precise threshold level." Overall usefulness capped at max 75. Agents 04 and 06 must use typical-market-covenant assumptions labeled as such. |
| No cash flow statement | **N** | 03, 04, 06 | Not applicable — full CFO, investing, and financing cash flow statements are available for FY26 |
| No undrawn-facility disclosure | **N** | 03 | Not applicable — ₹3,750 cr unutilized fund-based working capital confirmed; ₹4,500 cr non-fund-based also disclosed |
| No interest-expense detail | **N** | 04 | Not applicable — finance costs ₹874 cr disclosed in P&L; interest paid ₹884 cr in cash flow; coverage ratios directly computable |
| No EBITDA base | **N** | 06 | Not applicable — Underlying EBITDA ₹10,314 cr and reported EBITDA ₹9,965 cr both available; stress test is runnable |

One additional partial flag not listed in the standard table:

| Covenant EBITDA definition not disclosed | **Y** | 04, 06 | Covenant headroom quality cannot be assessed; agents must flag "addback illusion" risk as uncertain; covenant confidence capped at max 60. |

---

## 6. Sufficiency Verdict

- **Verdict:** Sufficient

- **Reason:** TMCV has a recent audited consolidated balance sheet (Mar 31, 2026), a disclosed debt maturity schedule, a full cash flow statement, committed liquidity details, credit ratings from all three Indian agencies plus S&P investment-grade, and an EBITDA base — all the primary inputs for leverage, liquidity, coverage, and a downside stress test are present.

- **Sections that can run:** All six: (1) capital structure and leverage, (2) maturity wall and refinancing, (3) liquidity runway, (4) coverage and covenants, (5) off-balance-sheet and contingencies, (6) downside stress test.

- **Active partial-data caps:**
  - Covenant headroom: "Not assessable at precise threshold level" — exact covenant thresholds are not disclosed; agents 04 and 06 must proxy with typical market covenants for India AA-rated issuers, labeled as assumptions; overall usefulness capped at max 75.
  - Covenant EBITDA definition: not disclosed; "addback illusion" risk is uncertain; covenant confidence capped at max 60.
  - Pension/OPEB: not available from data pool — solvency agents should note this as a gap; quantitative impact on leverage is unknown (likely not material for a manufacturing company with a net-cash balance sheet, but cannot be confirmed).
  - HoldCo/OpCo post-Iveco: the EUR 3.8 bn acquisition facility (not yet drawn) introduces structural subordination risk that will need explicit treatment in the capital structure agent; the current (pre-drawdown) analysis must flag the contingent nature of this liability.

- **Critical missing items:**
  - Exact financial covenant thresholds (net-worth floor, leverage cap, coverage floor) — the single highest-value missing document is the loan covenant schedule / term sheet.
  - Pension/OPEB funded status for Indian employees (gratuity, PF).
  - Specific instrument-level details for the ₹2,300 cr NCDs (coupon rates, individual maturity dates, security) — the CG Report reference implies this exists in the full annual report but was not surfaced in the extracted pages.

- **Single highest-value missing document:** Loan covenant schedule (actual covenant thresholds and headroom as at Mar 31, 2026) — without this, precise covenant-breach risk cannot be quantified.

---

**Note on the Iveco acquisition (material forward risk):** The committed EUR 3.8 bn loan facility at TMLCVB (backed by a corporate guarantee EUR 1.9 bn + letter of comfort EUR 1.9 bn from TML) does NOT appear on TMCV's balance sheet as at Mar 31, 2026 because the acquisition has not closed. If and when it closes (targeted Q2 FY27), it will materially change the capital structure — converting a net-cash ₹13,713 cr entity into a significantly net-debt entity. Agents 01, 02, 03, and 06 must build a pre-Iveco and post-Iveco scenario and must flag this deal as the dominant solvency variable for forward analysis. The corporate guarantee and letter of comfort ARE disclosed off-balance-sheet obligations that must be captured by agent 05.


---
## balance-sheet-survival/01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md

# Capital Structure & Leverage — TMCV

**Company:** Tata Motors Limited (formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited), NSEI: TMCV
**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR crore.
**Fiscal year:** April–March. FY26 = April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026.
**Entity note:** TMCV was incorporated June 23, 2024 and became a standalone listed entity via composite scheme of arrangement effective October 1, 2025. FY25 comparatives (9 months, unaudited, re-presented) exist but are not directly comparable to the full FY26 year. This is the only complete audited fiscal year available for TMCV as a standalone consolidated entity.

---

## 1. Debt Stack

All figures as at March 31, 2026.

| Instrument | Amount (₹ cr) | Entity | Secured? | Seniority | Collateral | Maturity | Rate | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Short-term debt / working capital lines | 678 | TMCV consolidated | Secured | Senior | Current assets (standard WC security) | < 1 year | Floating (bank rate-linked) | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet; Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 |
| Current portion of long-term debt (NCDs + other) | 2,795 | TMCV consolidated | Not disclosed at NCD level | Senior unsecured (NCDs) | Not disclosed | Due within 12 months of Mar 31, 2026 | Fixed (NCD coupon not individually disclosed) | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet; Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 |
| Long-term debt, net of current portion | 1,344 | TMCV consolidated | Not disclosed | Not disclosed | Not disclosed | 1–5+ years | Not disclosed | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| Revolver (drawn) | Nil | — | — | — | — | — | — | Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220: ₹3,750 cr of ₹4,000 cr fund-based limit unutilized |
| Finance / capital leases (Ind AS 116) | 798 | TMCV consolidated | Secured (right-of-use assets) | Junior to financial creditors | Leased assets (ROU) | Various | Implicit lease rate | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet (current ₹195 cr + non-current ₹603 cr) |
| **Total gross debt (borrowings only)** | **4,817** | | | | | | | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet; Key Financial Ratios row (a) |
| **Total gross debt including lease liabilities** | **5,615** | | | | | | | Derived: ₹4,817 + ₹798 |

**Reporting currency: INR crore.**

**NCD detail:** ₹2,300 crore of non-convertible debentures (NCDs) were transferred to TMCV under the demerger scheme. The Board's Report (Annual Report FY26, p.118) confirms ₹2,300 crore NCDs and credit ratings (CRISIL AA+ Stable, ICRA AA+ Stable, CARE AA+ Stable, S&P BBB Stable). Individual NCD series (coupon, ISIN, exact maturity dates) are referenced to the Corporate Governance Report and the NCD trustee record — not fully extracted in this data pool. The maturity profile (see Section 4) gives the time-bucket breakdown but not individual instrument terms.

**Seniority/security disclosure:** Instrument-level seniority and collateral details beyond "working capital lines secured by current assets" and "NCDs" are not disclosed in the Preliminary Interim Report or the extracted MD&A pages. Fields marked "Not disclosed" reflect the partial-data constraint — not an error.

---

## 2. Other Debt-Like Obligations

| Obligation | Amount (₹ cr) | Treatment | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Operating leases — Ind AS 116 (IFRS 16-equivalent) | 798 (on-balance-sheet lease liabilities) | All significant leases capitalized on balance sheet under Ind AS 116. The ₹798 cr is already included in the lease liability line above (Section 1). No material "off-balance-sheet" operating lease exposure remains under Ind AS 116. Short-term and low-value leases are expensed; amount not separately quantified in extracted pages. | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| Pension / OPEB underfunding | Not determinable from available data | Capital IQ pension tab returned no extractable data. Annual Report pages extracted do not surface a defined-benefit obligation note. Partial-data flag applied — a gratuity / defined-benefit obligation likely exists for TMCV employees but its funded status is not determinable from this data pool. | Capital IQ (not parseable); Annual Report FY26 (not surfaced in extracted pages) |
| Preferred equity | Nil | No preference share capital is shown on the balance sheet. | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligation | Not quantified (contingent) | EPR for end-of-life vehicles notified January 2025 (effective April 1, 2025). Company states cost cannot be reliably estimated; no provision made as of March 31, 2026. Contingent liability of uncertain but potentially material size — not on balance sheet. | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Note 10 |
| Iveco acquisition facility (off-balance-sheet at Mar 31, 2026) | EUR 3.8 bn (~₹41,691 cr at prevailing rate) arranged but not drawn | Loan facility arranged via TML CV Holdings Pte. Ltd. (Singapore) and TML CV Holdings B.V. (Netherlands). Corporate guarantee EUR 1.9 bn + letter of comfort EUR 1.9 bn provided by Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Limited (TMPV / former TML) on behalf of TMCV. Not on TMCV's balance sheet as at March 31, 2026; deal not closed. If closed (Q2 FY27 expected), this converts TMCV from a net-cash entity to a heavily leveraged one. | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Note 7; Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.221; FQ4 FY26 Earnings Call Transcript, May 13, 2026 |

---

## 3. Cash & Liquid Assets

All figures as at March 31, 2026.

| Item | Amount (₹ cr) | Restricted? | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Cash and cash equivalents | 6,899 | Not restricted (per classification) | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet; Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 |
| Short-term bank deposits (liquid, available) | 3,593 | Not flagged as restricted | Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 Liquidity table |
| Mutual fund investments (current, liquid) | 2,556 | Not restricted | Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 Liquidity table |
| **Total liquid assets (company-defined pool)** | **13,048** | | Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 |
| Bank balances other than cash (current) | 877 | Partially restricted — likely includes margin/fixed deposits held as security for guarantees and non-fund facilities. The balance is classified separately from cash; full restriction detail not disclosed. | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| Non-current investments (includes Tata Capital stake and other strategic holdings) | 6,547 | Not liquid — strategic investments, not available for near-term debt service | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |

**Restricted cash note:** The ₹877 crore "bank balances other than cash" is held separately from cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet, which typically signals some restriction or earmarking (margin deposits, escrow, lien-marked fixed deposits). This amount is NOT included in the liquid assets pool above. If partially or fully restricted, the liquid pool remains ₹13,048 crore. No formal disclosure of the restriction quantum is available from this data pool.

**Company-defined net cash of ₹13,713 crore** (as stated in the FQ4 FY26 transcript by management) uses a broader definition that likely includes Tata Capital and other strategic investments as well as lease liability netting. The agent uses the conservative ₹13,048 crore (cash + short-term deposits + mutual funds) as the auditable liquid pool for leverage purposes.

---

## 4. Gross & Net Debt

| Metric | Value (₹ cr) | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Gross debt (borrowings only) | 4,817 | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| Gross debt including lease liabilities | 5,615 | Derived |
| − Cash and cash equivalents only | (6,899) | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| **Net cash (cash only vs. borrowings)** | **2,082 net cash** | Derived |
| − Full liquid asset pool (cash + deposits + mutual funds) | (13,048) | Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 |
| **Net cash (full liquid pool vs. borrowings)** | **8,231 net cash** | Derived |
| Management-stated net cash | 13,713 | FQ4 FY26 Earnings Call Transcript, May 13, 2026; Annual Report FY26 Key Performance Highlights |

**TMCV is a net-cash entity as of March 31, 2026 on all three measures.** The minimum net cash position (cash only versus borrowings) is ₹2,082 crore; the auditable liquid-pool measure is ₹8,231 crore net cash. Net cash funds counter-cyclical action — including the ability to withstand a volume trough without refinancing pressure — and removes near-term solvency dependence. This is a positive strategic-flexibility signal, not a signal of an under-deployed balance sheet.

**Forward warning:** The Iveco acquisition (EUR 3.8 bn, ~₹41,691 crore) is pending closure. If completed, the entire liquid pool would be consumed and substantial new debt drawn, converting TMCV from a net-cash entity into a heavily net-debt entity. That scenario is out-of-scope for this agent's balance-sheet snapshot (it is for the stress-test agent) but is flagged here as the dominant forward solvency variable.

---

## 5. Leverage Ratios

**EBITDA bases used:**

- Reported EBITDA (FY26): approximately ₹8,641 crore. Derived as: Revenue from operations ₹90,439 cr (per Preliminary Interim Report income statement) × reported EBITDA margin of approximately 9.6% as implied by the Key Financial Ratios table. Note: the Preliminary Interim Report discloses EBIT-level ratios; reported EBITDA is estimated by adding back depreciation and amortisation of ₹1,945 crore to reported EBIT of approximately ₹6,696 crore. This is an estimate; the filing does not state reported EBITDA explicitly.
- Underlying EBITDA (management-adjusted, FY26): ₹10,314 crore. Stated explicitly in earnings/01_historical-financials.md, sourced from FQ4 FY26 Earnings Call Transcript and Annual Report KPIs. Underlying EBITDA excludes exceptional items (stamp duty ₹962 crore, Iveco acquisition costs ₹87 crore, PLI reversal, and other one-off items). Underlying EBITDA margin = 11.4% of revenue.

| Ratio | On Reported EBITDA (~₹8,641 cr) | On Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr) | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Gross debt / EBITDA (borrowings only) | 0.56x | 0.47x | Derived |
| Gross debt / EBITDA (incl. leases) | 0.65x | 0.54x | Derived |
| Net debt / EBITDA | N/A — net cash | N/A — net cash | |
| Debt / capital (borrowings / [borrowings + equity]) | 27.4% | (n/a — same numerator) | Derived: ₹4,817 / (₹4,817 + ₹12,734) |
| Debt / equity | 0.38x | (n/a) | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Key Financial Ratios row (a) |
| Interest coverage (EBIT / finance costs) | 10.65x | — | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Key Financial Ratios row (c) |
| Debt service coverage ratio | 1.05x | — | Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Key Financial Ratios row (i) |

**Notes:**
1. The GAAP-based gross leverage ratio (0.56x on reported EBITDA) is stated alongside the adjusted figure (0.47x on underlying EBITDA). The adjustment is material: exceptional items of approximately ₹1,673 crore reduced reported EBITDA versus underlying EBITDA.
2. Net leverage is not computable in the conventional sense because the company is net-cash across all three cash measures. A net leverage ratio would be negative (i.e., favorable), which carries no analytical content for a net-cash entity — so it is left as "N/A — net cash."
3. The debt service coverage ratio of 1.05x on reported earnings is notably lower than the interest coverage of 10.65x. This implies that scheduled principal repayments (₹3,657 crore within one year) dominate the denominator of the debt service ratio, not the interest charge. This reflects a front-loaded maturity profile, not a structural coverage weakness.

---

## 6. Leverage Trend

| Metric | FY24 (est.) | FY25 (9M, unaudited, re-presented) | FY26 (full year, audited) | Direction |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Gross debt — borrowings (₹ cr) | Not available (pre-demerger) | ~9,156 | 4,817 | Sharply falling |
| Cash and cash equivalents (₹ cr) | Not available | ~1,033 | 6,899 | Rising rapidly |
| Net debt / net cash (basic, ₹ cr) | Not available | ~8,123 net debt | 2,082 net cash | Positive inflection |
| Debt / equity | Not available | 0.87x | 0.38x | Sharply falling |
| Gross debt / Underlying EBITDA | Not available | Not computable (9M) | 0.47x | — |

**FY23 and FY24 figures for TMCV as a standalone consolidated entity are not available.** TMCV did not exist as an independent legal entity before June 2024, and the demerger became effective October 2025. The FY25 comparatives are a 9-month re-presentation (unaudited) of the CV segment carved out of the former Tata Motors Limited. Extending the trend to FY23/FY24 would require a CV-segment carve-out from the former parent — data not available in this pool. The two-point trend (FY25 to FY26) is therefore the only auditable consolidated comparison.

**What drove the change:** Between FY25 (9M) and FY26 (full year), gross borrowings fell by approximately ₹4,339 crore (from ₹9,156 crore to ₹4,817 crore). The driver is operating cash generation: CFO was ₹14,981 crore in FY26 — an exceptionally strong year driven by the negative 31-day working capital cycle, 11.4% underlying EBITDA margins, and ₹690 crore inventory unwind. Net long-term debt repayments were ₹3,413 crore; short-term lines fell by approximately ₹963 crore. Cash simultaneously rose from approximately ₹1,033 crore to ₹6,899 crore because FCF materially exceeded the debt repayments and capex combined. No asset sales are disclosed as driving the deleveraging — it is entirely operating-cash-driven. This is a genuine balance-sheet improvement, not accounting-driven.

---

## 6A. HoldCo / OpCo & Structural Subordination

| Item | Evidence | Why It Matters |
|---|---|---|
| Where debt sits | ₹4,817 crore borrowings sit at the TMCV consolidated level (operating company). The FY26 Preliminary Interim Report is the consolidated statement of TMCV and its subsidiaries. The Board's Report confirms NCDs ₹2,300 crore were transferred directly to TMCV (the OpCo/ListCo) under the demerger scheme — not to a HoldCo. | Financial creditors of TMCV have a direct claim on the operating entity — no structural subordination risk for existing debt. |
| Iveco acquisition HoldCo vehicles | Two special-purpose entities have been disclosed: TML CV Holdings Pte. Ltd. (Singapore) and TML CV Holdings B.V. (Netherlands). These are the acquisition vehicles for the Iveco tender offer. The EUR 3.8 bn loan facility is being arranged at this HoldCo level. TMCV (the listed Indian OpCo) has provided a corporate guarantee for EUR 1.9 bn and TMPV (former TML) has provided a letter of comfort for EUR 1.9 bn. | If the Iveco deal closes, TMCV will have a direct guarantee obligation of EUR 1.9 bn (~₹20,846 crore) even before any cash is drawn through to the Indian entity. The HoldCo acquisition debt will be serviced using cash flows / dividends from the Iveco operating business — introducing structural subordination within the new combined group. This is a material future structural risk not yet reflected in the FY26 balance sheet. |
| Upstreaming constraints | No dividend blocker covenants or regulatory restrictions on upstreaming cash from TMCV to its parent (Tata Sons / Tata Motors Group) are disclosed. TMCV declared its first dividend (₹4/share, ~₹1,500 crore) in FY26. | Not a current concern for TMCV's own debt service. |
| Restricted / trapped cash | ₹877 crore "bank balances other than cash" may be partially restricted (margin deposits, guarantee cover). The exact restriction level is not disclosed. This amount is excluded from the liquid pool in Section 3. | Liquid pool of ₹13,048 crore may be overstated by up to ₹877 crore if the entire balance is restricted. |

---

## 7. Leverage Anchor Summary (canonical numbers for downstream agents)

These are the numbers every other solvency agent in this module should use. Do not re-derive independently — use these.

| Item | Value | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| **Gross debt (borrowings only)** | **₹4,817 crore** | Audited; Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| **Gross debt including lease liabilities** | **₹5,615 crore** | Derived: ₹4,817 + ₹798 |
| **Cash and cash equivalents** | **₹6,899 crore** | Audited; Preliminary Interim Report FY26, Balance Sheet |
| **Full liquid asset pool** | **₹13,048 crore** | Auditable: cash ₹6,899 + short-term deposits ₹3,593 + mutual funds ₹2,556; Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 |
| **Net position (basic)** | **₹2,082 crore net cash** | Derived: ₹6,899 − ₹4,817 |
| **Net position (full liquid pool)** | **₹8,231 crore net cash** | Derived: ₹13,048 − ₹4,817 |
| **EBITDA base — reported** | **~₹8,641 crore** | Estimated; reported EBIT + D&A from Preliminary Interim Report FY26 |
| **EBITDA base — underlying (management-adjusted)** | **₹10,314 crore** | Stated; FQ4 FY26 Earnings Call Transcript; earnings/01_historical-financials.md |
| **Gross debt / Reported EBITDA** | **0.56x** | On reported basis |
| **Gross debt / Underlying EBITDA** | **0.47x** | On management-adjusted basis — use this figure alongside the reported figure |
| **Net debt / EBITDA** | **N/A — net cash on both EBITDA bases** | |
| **Reporting currency** | **INR crore** | Ind AS consolidated |

**TMCV is net-cash positive on all measures as at March 31, 2026.** Gross leverage is low (under 0.6x on any EBITDA basis). The net-cash position funds counter-cyclical action — a volume or margin downturn does not create refinancing pressure at current debt levels.

**Critical forward caveat for all downstream agents:** The Iveco acquisition (EUR 3.8 bn, ~₹41,691 crore all-cash) is pending closure (Q2 FY27 expected). If completed at disclosed terms, TMCV's net-cash position reverses to a significantly net-debt position. The EUR 1.9 bn corporate guarantee from TMCV is already an off-balance-sheet contingent obligation. Downstream agents assessing covenant headroom, liquidity runway, and stress scenarios must treat the Iveco close as the primary binary scenario — the current balance sheet does not reflect it.

**Partial data applied:**
- Pension/OPEB: underfunding not determinable from available data pool — flagged in Section 2; not included in gross debt.
- Individual NCD terms (coupon, series, ISIN-level maturities): not individually disclosed in extracted pages — time-bucket maturity profile is available (for maturity-wall agent).
- Covenant thresholds: compliance confirmed but specific financial maintenance thresholds not disclosed — "Not assessable at precise threshold level" applies in the covenant agent.
- Restricted cash quantum within ₹877 crore bank balance: not precisely disclosed — conservatively excluded from liquid pool.


---
## balance-sheet-survival/02_maturity-wall-and-refinancing.md

# Maturity Wall & Refinancing — TMCV (Tata Motors Limited / TML Commercial Vehicles)

**Reporting currency:** INR (₹ crore). **Reporting standard:** Ind AS. **Fiscal year:** April–March. **Balance-sheet date:** March 31, 2026.

**Sources used:** FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS) — MD&A p.220 (maturity table), Notes 22, 23, 23B, 23D, 23E (borrowing details); FY26 Preliminary Interim Report (income statement, cash flow); FQ4 FY26 earnings call transcript (CFO comments on Iveco financing); `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md` (gross debt anchor, ratings, liquidity pool); web-sourced benchmark rate labeled throughout.

---

## 1. Maturity Schedule

The maturity table below is sourced directly from the MD&A, p.220 (FY26 Annual Report, Ind AS). It covers "long-term debt obligations including current maturities of long-term borrowings and accrued interest as of March 31, 2026." The schedule total (₹5,269 crore) exceeds the balance-sheet gross borrowings (₹4,817 crore) by ₹452 crore, which is a reconciling item comprising accrued interest (₹42 crore per Note 25a) and carrying-value vs face-value differences (fair-value adjustments, debt-issue costs, and FX premiums on the residual foreign-currency tranche). This is not a data gap — both figures are from audited statements.

The NCD ISIN-level schedule (Note 23D, FY26 Annual Report) provides the year-by-year instrument breakdown for the fixed-rate bond portion.

| Period | Amount Due (₹ cr) | % of Total (₹5,269 cr) | Instrument(s) | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| Within 12 months (by Mar 2027) | 3,657 | 69.4% | 6.60% NCDs ₹500 cr (matured May 2026); 8.50% zero-coupon bonds ₹801 cr (Sep 2026); 8.50% NCDs ₹250 cr (Dec 2026); 8.50% NCDs ₹250 cr (Jan 2027); 7.65% NCDs ₹500 cr (Mar 2027); short-term bank loans ₹428 cr; WC demand loans ₹250 cr; perpetuals ₹350 cr (current maturities); other ₹328 cr | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220; Note 23D, 23E |
| Year 2 (Apr 2027–Mar 2028) | 1,357 | 25.8% | 7.65% NCDs ₹800 cr (Mar 2028); residual bank/lease obligations ₹557 cr | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220; Note 23D |
| Year 3–5 (Apr 2028–Mar 2031) | 11 | 0.2% | Government concessional loans (repayable 2030-2040, 0.1% p.a.) — small portion | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220; Note 22 |
| After 5 years (post Mar 2031) | 243 | 4.6% | Government concessional loans (bulk); perpetual debenture principal (₹444 cr LT; call/put option year 4–5 from issuance, already past in most tranches) | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220; Note 22, 23E |
| **Total (incl. accrued interest)** | **5,269** | **100%** | | |
| *Memo: Balance-sheet gross borrowings* | *4,817* | | *Reconciling gap ₹452 cr = accrued interest + carrying-value adjustments* | *Note 22+23, FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS)* |

**Note on the within-12-month bucket:** The ₹3,657 crore figure as of March 31, 2026 includes the 6.60% NCD (₹500 cr) that already matured May 29, 2026 — i.e., it has been paid or is being paid at the time of this analysis. The next large single event is the Zero Coupon bonds (₹801 cr) due September 1, 2026.

---

## 2. Maturity Profile Metrics

WAM computed using bucket midpoints: Within 12m = 0.5 yr; Year 2 = 1.5 yr; Year 3–5 = 3.5 yr (midpoint of 3-yr range); Beyond 5 yr = 7.0 yr (proxy). Weights are % of total (₹5,269 cr schedule).

WAM = (0.5 × 69.4%) + (1.5 × 25.8%) + (3.5 × 0.2%) + (7.0 × 4.6%) = 0.347 + 0.387 + 0.007 + 0.322 = **1.06 years**

| Metric | Value |
|---|---:|
| Weighted-average maturity (years) | ~1.1 years |
| % due within 12 months | 69.4% |
| % due within 24 months | 95.2% (= 69.4% + 25.8%) |
| % due within 36 months | 95.4% (= 95.2% + 0.2%) |
| Largest single maturity year (and amount) | FY27 (within 12 months from Mar 2026), ₹3,657 crore |

The WAM of ~1.1 years is exceptionally short — reflecting a deliberate deleveraging posture. TMCV repaid USD 5.875% Senior Notes (~USD 127 mn), unsecured bank term loans (₹567 cr), and Smart City project loans (₹953 cr) in FY26, all of which compressed the outstanding balance and shortened the remaining duration. What remains is predominantly short-dated.

---

## 3. Rate Exposure

**Fixed vs floating identification:** Notes 22, 23, 23B, 23D, 23E (FY26 Annual Report, Ind AS). All NCDs are privately placed, unsecured, fixed-rate instruments. Perpetual debentures are fixed (7.30%–8.75%). Government concessional loans are fixed at 0.1% p.a. Short-term bank loans (₹428 cr) carry fixed rates 1.11%–6.16%. Only the secured working-capital demand loans (₹250 cr) are floating, linked to the bank rate.

**Weighted-average coupon (WAC) computation:** Using face-value weights on the disclosed NCD schedule and known rates for other tranches. NCD schedule (Note 23D): 6.60% × ₹500 cr + 8.50% × ₹1,051 cr (ZC equivalent coupon on ₹801 cr ZC bonds, using their implicit yield ~8.53% as stated) + 8.50% × ₹250 cr + 7.65% × ₹500 cr + 7.65% × ₹800 cr = weighted NCD coupon ~7.8% on ₹3,101 cr. Perpetuals (₹794 cr total, midpoint rate 8.1%); government loans (₹102 cr, 0.1%); short-term bank loans (₹428 cr, ~4.0% midpoint); WC loans (₹250 cr, ~7.5% bank-rate proxy); other/residual. Blended across ₹4,817 cr → WAC approximately **7.3–7.5%**.

**Current market refi rate benchmark:** India 5-year G-sec ~6.66% as of May 7, 2026 (web-sourced: [Trading Economics, India 5-Year Note Yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/5-year-note-yield), indicative, unverified). India 10-year G-sec ~6.9% in late May 2026 (web-sourced: same source). For a 2–3 year NCD, the relevant benchmark is the 3-year G-sec, estimated ~6.40–6.60%. CRISIL/ICRA/CARE AA+ spread over G-sec for Indian corporates at this rating: approximately 50–80 bps based on PSU bond market evidence (AAA spreads ~40–60 bps; AA+ one notch wider). Current market refi rate for TMCV-quality 2–3 year NCD: **~7.0–7.4%** (web-sourced, indicative, unverified). The top end of this range overlaps with TMCV's current WAC, meaning the step-up on new issuance at current rates is minimal to negligible for most tranches. The cheapest legacy tranche (6.60% NCD, ₹500 cr, matured May 2026) would have seen a ~50–80 bps step-up on replacement.

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Fixed-rate share | ~94.8% (₹4,567 cr of ₹4,817 cr) | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), Notes 22, 23, 23B, 23D, 23E |
| Floating-rate share | ~5.2% (₹250 cr WC demand loans) | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), Note 23B |
| Weighted-average coupon (WAC) | ~7.3–7.5% | Computed from Note 23D, 23E, 22, 23B — see above |
| Current market refi rate (3–5yr AA+ NCD) | ~7.0–7.4% | Web-sourced: Trading Economics India bond yields, May–Jun 2026; FIMMDA spread proxies — indicative, unverified |
| Estimated refi cost step-up (bps) | ~0–30 bps on blended portfolio; ~50–80 bps on cheapest legacy tranche (6.60% NCD, already matured) | Computed — see above |

**Floating-rate sensitivity:** Only ₹250 crore (5.2% of gross debt) reprices with the bank rate. A 100 bps move in the RBI repo rate (and hence bank rate) changes annual interest costs by approximately ₹2.5 crore — negligible against ₹874 crore total finance costs (FY26 Preliminary Interim Report).

---

## 4. Refinancing Exposure

### Refi Funding Plan — within-24-month maturities (Apr 2026–Mar 2028, ~₹5,014 cr per schedule)

| Source of repayment for next-24m maturities | Amount (₹ cr) | Evidence |
|---|---:|---|
| Cash on hand (as of Mar 31, 2026) | 5,217 | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), balance sheet — cash and cash equivalents + bank deposits |
| Mutual funds / treasury investments | 7,831 | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), balance sheet — current investments; total liquid pool ₹13,048 cr per `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md` |
| FY26 FCF (run-rate, company-defined) | ~12,438 | FY26 Preliminary Interim Report — FCF ₹12,438 cr (CFO − capex definition); FY27 run-rate unverified |
| Revolver availability | 3,750 | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220 — ₹4,000 cr fund-based limit, ₹3,750 cr unutilized |
| New unsecured NCD issuance | Unknown — not committed or announced | No commitment disclosed; historically accessed NCD market for routine roll-overs |
| Asset-sale proceeds | None announced | No asset sale announced for debt repayment as of FY26 filing |

**Sufficiency assessment.** The liquid pool alone (₹13,048 crore) covers the full 24-month schedule (₹5,014 crore) by 2.6x without any new FCF generation, asset sales, or new debt issuance. Annual FCF of ~₹12,438 crore would, by itself, cover the entire 24-month obligation in less than 6 months of cash generation. The revolver (₹3,750 cr unutilized) provides additional backup that is not needed under the base case.

The near-term wall (12–24 months) is fully covered by cash and FCF without any market access. The company has CRISIL AA+ / ICRA AA+ / CARE AA+ / S&P BBB Stable ratings, so routine NCD roll-overs at market rates are available for the portion that management chooses to refinance rather than retire. FY26 saw deliberate active debt repayment: USD Senior Notes, ₹567 cr bank term loans, ₹953 cr Smart City loans, and ₹700 cr TMFHL perpetuals. This confirms a repay-from-cash posture rather than a roll-and-grow posture.

The 5.2% floating-rate share means that market rate movements barely affect interest cost — there is no meaningful floating-rate repricing risk on the current book.

**Verdict:** Self-funded / low refi risk (current balance sheet).

**Critical forward caveat — Iveco Group acquisition:** TMCV has agreed to acquire Iveco Group's commercial vehicle businesses (Iveco Bus, Iveco Defence, and Iveco Off-Road) for EUR 3.8 billion (~₹41,691 crore at spot). EUR 1.9 billion corporate guarantee has already been provided. CFO G.V. Ramanan confirmed (FQ4 FY26 transcript): "Our initial plan for financing is going to be through a bridge loan and the subsequent refinancing — at this point of time, all options are open. So we've kept both equity and debt still open." Closure is expected Q2 FY27 (pending France and Spain financial regulatory approvals). If completed, Iveco would transform the balance sheet from net-cash (₹8,231 cr) to significantly net-debt (~₹33,000–41,000 cr depending on financing mix). This is a binary forward event that the current maturity wall analysis does not — and cannot — reflect. The refinancing risk for the Iveco acquisition bridge loan is a separate and much larger question; it is addressed in the stress-test module (06).

---

## 5. Refinancing Read

TMCV's current debt book is an accelerating paydown — ₹3,657 crore comes due within 12 months and ₹5,014 crore within 24 months, representing 95% of total debt. The WAM of ~1.1 years is unusually short, but this is not a distress signal: the liquid pool of ₹13,048 crore and annual FCF of ~₹12,438 crore cover the full schedule multiple times over without any market access needed. Refinancing cost is near-neutral — the current WAC of ~7.3–7.5% sits at or within 30 bps of where a fresh AA+ NCD would price today, so there is no material cost step-up on roll-overs.

The single biggest refinancing risk on the current balance sheet is not refinancing at all — it is the Iveco bridge loan commitment. If TMCV closes the EUR 3.8 billion deal via a bridge, it goes from net-cash of ₹8,231 crore to net-debt of an order of magnitude larger, and the subsequent refinancing (equity or permanent debt) would need to be executed in what is currently an open but not guaranteed capital market. That binary event, not the current NCD roll-overs, is the real watch item.

**12-month market closure survival (current balance sheet):** Yes. Cash and treasury investments (₹13,048 crore) alone exceed the entire gross debt (₹4,817 crore). Even if no new unsecured issuance were available for 12 months, all within-12-month maturities (₹3,657 crore) would be met from existing liquid assets with ₹9,391 crore remaining. The revolver (₹3,750 crore unutilized) provides a further cushion. This assessment applies only to the current balance sheet; a post-Iveco balance sheet would require fresh analysis.

---

## Self-Check

- [x] Maturity schedule total ₹5,269 cr vs gross debt ₹4,817 cr — reconciling gap of ₹452 cr (accrued interest + carrying-value adjustments) explicitly stated.
- [x] WAM of ~1.1 years computed from bucket midpoints and disclosed amounts, not described vaguely.
- [x] 12/24/36-month shares computed: 69.4% / 95.2% / 95.4%.
- [x] Fixed share ~94.8%, floating share ~5.2% (₹250 cr WC loans only); floating-rate sensitivity quantified (~₹2.5 cr per 100 bps).
- [x] Refi cost step-up: WAC ~7.3–7.5% vs market ~7.0–7.4% = ~0–30 bps step-up; web-sourced benchmark labeled dated and unverified.
- [x] Refinancing risk tied to FCF/liquidity (covers 2.6x) and market access (AA+ ratings, active NCD market), not asserted.
- [x] Partial-data cap not applied — full 5-bucket maturity schedule disclosed in audited filing.
- [x] No banned phrases used.


---
## balance-sheet-survival/03_liquidity-runway.md

# Liquidity Runway — TMCV

**Company:** Tata Motors Limited (formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited), NSEI: TMCV
**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR crore.
**Fiscal year:** April–March. FY26 = April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026.
**Upstream note:** `02_maturity-wall-and-refinancing.md` has not yet run. The 12-month maturity figure is sourced directly from the Annual Report FY26, MD&A p.220 maturity profile table — the same primary source that Agent 02 will use. All other upstream inputs (`01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md`, `earnings/01_historical-financials.md`, `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md`) are present and complete.

---

## 1. Liquidity Sources (committed only)

*All figures as at March 31, 2026. Currency: INR crore.*

| Source | Amount (₹ cr) | Usable? | Notes | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Cash and cash equivalents | 6,899 | Y | Classified as unrestricted on the balance sheet. Not flagged as restricted. | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, May 13, 2026, Balance Sheet |
| Short-term bank deposits | 3,593 | Y | Disclosed in the liquidity table as liquid and available; not restricted. | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220, Liquidity table |
| Mutual fund investments (current) | 2,556 | Y | Current, liquid. Not flagged as restricted. | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220, Liquidity table |
| **Subtotal — cash and liquid investments** | **13,048** | | | |
| Bank balances other than cash | 877 | N — excluded | Likely includes margin deposits and lien-marked fixed deposits held against guarantees and non-fund facilities. Restriction quantum not disclosed. Conservatively excluded entirely. | SEBI LODR Consolidated Balance Sheet, May 13, 2026 |
| Fund-based WC facility (total commitment) | 4,000 | Partial | Consortium of banks; secured by hypothecation of current assets; renewed annually. Availability figure confirmed. | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220 |
| **Fund-based WC facility — undrawn (committed)** | **3,750** | **Y** | ₹250 cr drawn; ₹3,750 cr confirmed undrawn as at Mar 31, 2026. Secured by hypothecation of entire current assets. No borrowing-base formula disclosed — treated as a standard working capital line and counted at confirmed availability. | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220 |
| Non-fund-based limits (LCs, bank guarantees) | 4,500 | N — excluded | These are guarantee facilities, not cash-drawdown facilities. They do not fund debt service. Excluded from liquidity. | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220 |
| Uncommitted lines | Not disclosed | N — excluded | None separately disclosed. Even if such lines exist, they are excluded under the committed-only rule. | MODULE_RULES.md §4 |
| **Total usable liquidity** | **16,798** | | Cash + liquid investments (₹13,048 cr) + committed undrawn revolver (₹3,750 cr) | |

**Restricted cash flag:** The ₹877 crore "bank balances other than cash" line is excluded from usable liquidity because it is classified separately from cash on the balance sheet — the standard signal for margin deposits, escrow accounts, or lien-marked balances. The exact restriction quantum is not disclosed. Usable liquidity of ₹16,798 crore may therefore be understated by up to ₹877 crore if any portion of this balance is actually unrestricted.

**Non-fund limits flag:** The ₹4,500 crore non-fund-based facility (letters of credit, bank guarantees) is listed separately and excluded. It does not provide cash liquidity.

**Revolver annual renewal risk:** The fund-based working capital facility is renewed annually. If renewal is refused (Remote — given CRISIL/ICRA/CARE AA+ Stable ratings), availability drops to zero. This is noted but is not treated as a base-case constraint.

---

## 2. Near-Term Uses (next 12 months)

*"Next 12 months" means April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2027. All figures in INR crore.*

| Use | Amount (₹ cr) | Notes | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Debt maturities — within 1 year | 3,657 | Per the maturity profile table in the Annual Report, the "within 1 year" bucket totals ₹3,657 cr. This figure includes accrued interest embedded in the current maturities line; the filing does not separately strip out the interest component. The figure covers current portion of long-term debt (₹2,795 cr) plus short-term working capital lines (₹678 cr) plus accrued interest on NCDs (~₹184 cr implied). | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220, Maturity Profile |
| Cash interest (full-year basis) | 884 | Interest paid per the FY26 cash flow statement. This figure likely partially overlaps with the accrued interest embedded in the ₹3,657 cr maturity figure above; treated separately for conservatism and to avoid understating total cash outflow. If the overlap is material, the true combined total of maturities-plus-interest is closer to ₹4,100–₹4,200 cr. Both figures are retained for transparency. | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, May 13, 2026, Cash Flow Statement (supplemental interest paid line) |
| Maintenance capex | 2,248 | The maintenance vs. growth capex split is not separately disclosed. Total capex (PPE ₹1,321 cr + intangible/product development ₹927 cr = ₹2,248 cr) is used as a conservative proxy. Inference: a portion — possibly ₹800–₹1,000 cr — is growth or platform-launch related (17 next-generation models in FY26 pipeline), meaning true maintenance capex is lower. The total figure is retained here to avoid understating the use. | SEBI LODR Consolidated Cash Flow Statement, May 13, 2026; FY26 Annual Report MD&A p.218 |
| Committed dividends and buybacks | 1,500 | TMCV declared its first standalone dividend of ₹4 per share in FY26 on approximately 3,682 million shares = approximately ₹1,473 cr (~₹1,500 cr rounded). A recurring dividend at this level is treated as a near-term committed use. No buyback program has been announced. | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), Board's Report p.117 |
| **Total near-term uses** | **8,289** | Sum of the four lines above. Note the conservative double-count of interest (embedded in maturities + stated separately); the true non-overlapping total is approximately ₹7,900–₹8,000 cr. | |

**Overlap note:** The ₹3,657 cr maturity figure in the Annual Report is described as including accrued interest on current maturities. If approximately ₹184 cr of interest is already embedded in that figure, the non-overlapping total of maturities plus separate interest paid is approximately ₹4,357 cr (₹3,473 cr principal-equivalent + ₹884 cr). The ₹8,289 cr total above retains the overlap for conservatism.

---

## 3. Runway

| Metric | Value |
|---|---:|
| Total committed liquidity (₹ cr) | 16,798 |
| Annual FCF — CFO minus total capex (₹ cr) | 12,733 |
| Near-term uses over next 12 months (₹ cr) | 8,289 |
| **FCF surplus over near-term uses (₹ cr)** | **4,444** |
| FCF as % of near-term uses | 154% |

**Formula:** Annual FCF (₹12,733 cr) minus near-term uses (₹8,289 cr) = annual surplus of ₹4,444 crore.

FCF covers all near-term uses by a factor of 1.54×. There is no finite runway to compute — FCF generation alone exceeds all obligations due in the next 12 months, without drawing a single rupee from the cash or revolver. The liquidity pool of ₹16,798 crore is an additional layer of protection on top of the FCF surplus.

**FCF quality note:** The FY26 FCF (₹12,733 cr computed, ₹12,438 cr company-defined) was verified against the cash flow statement (SEBI LODR, May 13, 2026). The CFO-to-Underlying-EBITDA ratio was 145% in FY26 — significantly above 100% — driven by the deeply negative working capital cycle (−54 days). The earnings-quality agent (analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/06_earnings-quality.md) rated earnings quality at 72/100 and found no accrual concerns. The ₹6,657 cr working capital inflow in FY26 was the largest component inflating CFO above EBITDA; this includes ₹3,265 cr of customer advances (contract liabilities) that partially reverse as deliveries occur. The normalized FCF — stripping out the non-recurring portion of the customer-advance build — is approximately ₹10,000–₹11,000 cr. Even at this lower level, FCF still covers all near-term uses comfortably.

**Company-defined FCF cross-check:** The company's own FCF definition (₹12,438 cr) adds interest received (₹506 cr) and investee dividends (₹95 cr), and deducts equity investments in core auto entities (₹138 cr), relative to our (CFO − total capex) measure of ₹12,733 cr. The two figures are close; both are used here interchangeably as the FCF measure, with the more conservative (company-defined ₹12,438 cr) used where a single number is required.

### Seasonality / Peak Liquidity Need (Hard Check)

The FY26 quarterly revenue distribution was approximately Q1 ~18%, Q2 ~23%, Q3 ~26%, Q4 ~31% — heavily H2-weighted, with Q4 accounting for nearly one-third of full-year revenue. This pattern is consistent with India's infrastructure-spending and freight-cycle seasonality. However, only one full fiscal year of audited standalone TMCV data exists. One year is insufficient to confirm this as a structural seasonal pattern rather than a cycle-specific effect (the FY26 H2 was boosted by a recovery from H1 supply-chain disruptions per the earnings call).

The potential peak working capital need — the maximum intra-year cash outflow from working capital build before H2 receipts peak — is not separately quantified in the available filings. Given the negative cash conversion cycle (−54 days), TMCV collects cash from customers before paying suppliers, which limits the severity of a seasonal cash trough relative to a positive-WC business.

**Ruling:** Peak working-capital need not separately disclosed. Given the negative cash conversion cycle and the ₹16,798 cr committed liquidity pool — more than twice total annual near-term obligations — a peak trough that materially threatens liquidity is not plausible on any reasonable assumption. However, this cannot be formally stress-tested until multiple years of intra-year cash-flow data are available.

---

## 4. Sources and Uses Bridge

As of March 31, 2026, TMCV's internal sources — operating cash flow alone — cover all near-term obligations with ₹4,444 crore to spare. The business generates approximately ₹35–₹41 crore of CFO per day (₹14,981 crore CFO over 365 days). Near-term debt maturities of ₹3,657 crore represent roughly 88–104 days of operating cash inflow. No external access to refinancing markets, asset sales, or revolver drawings is required to meet the next 12 months of obligations under the current structure.

Of the ₹16,798 crore usable committed liquidity pool, ₹13,048 crore is already in hand (cash and liquid investments), and ₹3,750 crore is the undrawn committed revolver. The ₹13,048 crore in-hand liquidity alone exceeds the ₹8,289 crore of near-term uses — meaning that even in a scenario where FCF fell to zero, the existing cash and liquid investments would cover all uses for the next 12 months with ₹4,759 crore remaining.

The split: approximately 57% of liquidity coverage comes from already-in-hand cash (₹13,048 cr ÷ ₹8,289 cr = 1.57× coverage from cash alone). The remaining FCF is an additional layer that does not need to materialize for the near-term picture to be safe.

**Critical forward warning:** This analysis is a snapshot of the pre-Iveco balance sheet. The EUR 3.8 billion (~₹41,691 crore) all-cash Iveco acquisition (targeted Q2 FY27 close) will, if completed, consume the entire liquid pool and draw the committed acquisition facility, converting TMCV from a net-cash entity into a significantly net-debt entity. TMCV's CRISIL/ICRA/CARE AA+ Stable ratings have been maintained on the current structure; S&P has noted the deal could be credit-neutral if integration proceeds to plan. The liquidity runway analysis for FY27 post-Iveco will be a completely different picture — that scenario belongs to the stress-test agent (06).

---

## 5. Liquidity Read

TMCV's liquidity runway in the current (pre-Iveco) structure is not a finite number of months — annual FCF of approximately ₹12,400–₹12,700 crore covers all near-term uses of ₹8,289 crore with a ₹4,400+ crore surplus, and the existing cash pool of ₹13,048 crore would cover all near-term uses for roughly 19 months even if FCF fell to zero. The single biggest near-term liquidity risk is not the existing debt structure but the Iveco acquisition: a EUR 3.8 billion all-cash deal that, if closed in Q2 FY27, will erase the net-cash position and the liquidity surplus at a stroke, leaving TMCV dependent on the FCF of the combined business (including Iveco's European operations) to service a substantially larger debt load. Until that transaction closes or is withdrawn, the current liquidity position is straightforwardly strong — but the runway clock starts fresh the moment the deal clears.

---

## Self-Check

- [x] Liquidity uses committed facilities only; uncommitted lines are excluded and noted (non-fund-based ₹4,500 cr excluded; no uncommitted lines disclosed).
- [x] Restricted cash is flagged (₹877 cr bank balances excluded; restriction quantum not disclosed).
- [x] Near-term uses pull the 12-month maturity figure directly from the Annual Report MD&A p.220 maturity table (₹3,657 cr); Agent 02 not yet available — same primary source used.
- [x] FCF surplus stated (₹4,444 cr annual surplus) in place of a finite runway because FCF more than covers obligations.
- [x] Split between in-hand liquidity (₹13,048 cr already in hand, 1.57× near-term uses) and must-materialize FCF (additional layer, not required for near-term safety) is stated.
- [x] No partial-data caps triggered for this agent: cash flow statement available, committed-facility availability confirmed, maturity schedule confirmed.
- [x] No banned phrases (no "adequate liquidity" without a number; runway expressed in months or surplus; surplus expressed in crore with formula shown).

---

## Liquidity Runway Score

**Liquidity runway: 88 / 100** (Strong / very low risk)

The score reflects: committed liquidity (₹16,798 cr) that is 2.0× near-term uses (₹8,289 cr); a FCF surplus of ₹4,444 cr; in-hand cash that alone covers uses 1.57×; and investment-grade ratings across all four active rating agencies. The deduction from the top band reflects: (1) the annual revolver renewal risk (the fund-based line is not multi-year committed); (2) the FY26 CFO included a large non-recurring component of the working capital inflow (customer advances); and (3) the Iveco transaction, if closed, will require the entire liquidity analysis to be rebuilt at a materially weaker starting position.


---
## balance-sheet-survival/04_coverage-and-covenants.md

# Coverage & Covenants — TMCV

**Company:** Tata Motors Limited (formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited), NSEI: TMCV
**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR crore.
**Fiscal year:** April–March. FY26 = full year ended March 31, 2026 (first complete audited year as a standalone consolidated entity).
**Upstream inputs used:** `01_capital-structure-and-leverage.md` (debt stack, EBITDA bases, net-cash position); `earnings/01_historical-financials.md` (EBIT, EBITDA, capex, interest); `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md` (cash conversion quality of EBITDA).

---

## 1. Coverage Ratios

**EBITDA basis:** Two bases are used throughout. Reported EBITDA (~₹8,641 crore) is computed from the P&L (operating profit before exceptional items ₹5,922 cr + D&A ₹1,945 cr + finance costs ₹874 cr − FX gain ₹100 cr). Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 crore, 12.3% margin) is management's primary disclosed metric — it strips out the FVTPL fair-value loss on subsidiary equity investments (₹2,418 cr, non-cash, non-deductible) and MTM hedges but retains SBC. Both are shown. The Underlying EBITDA basis is more representative of operating cash generation; the reported basis is lower because of the recurring but volatile FVTPL item.

**Interest basis:** Gross finance costs of ₹874 crore as disclosed on the P&L (SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, year ended March 31, 2026, filed May 13, 2026). Cash interest paid was ₹884 crore (Cash Flow Statement, same filing) — the small difference reflects discount amortization on lease liabilities. All ratios use gross finance costs of ₹874 crore unless stated.

**Capex:** Total gross capex ₹2,248 crore (PPE ₹1,321 cr + intangibles ₹927 cr) from the Cash Flow Statement. Maintenance vs. growth split is not disclosed; total capex is used, which makes the (EBITDA − capex) / interest ratio conservative.

**Formulas:**
- EBITDA / interest = EBITDA ÷ ₹874 cr
- EBIT / interest = EBIT ÷ ₹874 cr
- (EBITDA − capex) / interest = (EBITDA − ₹2,248 cr) ÷ ₹874 cr
- Fixed-charge coverage = (EBITDA − capex) ÷ (finance costs + scheduled principal repayments + lease payments). Lease payments (principal + interest) approximated from Cash Flow Statement: lease liability repayment ₹226 cr (financing activities); interest component included in finance costs ₹874 cr. Scheduled principal repayment due within one year: ₹3,657 cr (MD&A maturity table, includes NCDs and term loans due within 12 months of March 31, 2026).

| Ratio | On Reported EBITDA (~₹8,641 cr) | On Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr) | Formula | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| EBITDA / interest (interest coverage) | 9.9x | 11.8x | EBITDA ÷ ₹874 cr | SEBI LODR results filing, May 13, 2026 — P&L and Cash Flow; FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.215, p.217 |
| EBIT / interest | 7.0x | 9.8x | EBIT ÷ ₹874 cr (Reported EBIT ~₹6,091 cr; Underlying EBIT ₹8,538 cr) | Same; company also discloses 10.65x interest service coverage ratio using a slightly different EBIT definition per SEBI LODR Key Financial Ratios row (c) — see note below |
| (EBITDA − capex) / interest | 7.3x | 9.2x | (EBITDA − ₹2,248 cr) ÷ ₹874 cr | Capex from SEBI LODR Cash Flow Statement, May 13, 2026 |
| Fixed-charge coverage | 1.2x | 1.4x | (EBITDA − ₹2,248 cr) ÷ (₹874 cr finance costs + ₹3,657 cr due within 1 year + ₹226 cr lease repayment) = (EBITDA − ₹2,248 cr) ÷ ₹4,757 cr | MD&A p.220 maturity table; SEBI LODR Cash Flow Statement |

**Note on disclosed interest coverage ratio:** The SEBI LODR Key Financial Ratios (row c) states interest service coverage of 10.65x for FY26, computed as "(Profit/loss from ordinary activities before tax + Interest on borrowings) / Interest on borrowings." This is essentially (PBT before exceptional items + finance costs) / finance costs = (₹6,091 cr + ₹874 cr) / ₹874 cr = 7.97x on reported numbers, but the disclosed ratio uses a definition that includes profit from equity-accounted investees. The 10.65x figure as disclosed is used for reference in Section 2 but the agent's own computation (EBIT/interest) above is the primary measure.

**EBITDA cash quality caveat:** Per `earnings/06_earnings-quality.md`, CFO/Underlying EBITDA was 145% in FY26, meaning operating cash generation materially exceeds EBITDA. The EBITDA used for coverage is therefore cash-backed — in fact, the EBITDA measure understates actual cash generation. The ₹6,657 crore working capital inflow in FY26 (of which ₹4,088 crore was from other liabilities including customer advances) is partly timing-driven and may not recur at the same scale in FY27. Even adjusting for a normalised working capital build, CFO/Underlying EBITDA of 100–110% is structurally supported by the negative 54-day cash conversion cycle. Coverage ratios using EBITDA are therefore conservative, not optimistic, relative to actual cash generation. No caveat on coverage quality is required on this ground — the opposite is true.

**Key finding:** At 9.9x–11.8x EBITDA interest coverage and 7.0x–9.8x EBIT interest coverage, earnings carry the interest charge by a large margin. The fixed-charge coverage of 1.2x–1.4x is low on the surface but reflects the front-loaded repayment of ₹3,657 crore of debt due within 12 months — this is a maturity concentration issue, not a structural cash-flow gap. With ₹13,048 crore of liquid assets versus ₹4,817 crore of total borrowings, TMCV can repay all existing debt outright and still have net cash of ₹8,231 crore.

---

## 2. Covenant Inventory

### Covenant Disclosure Quality

The FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.221, "Loan Covenants" section states:

> "Some of our existing financing arrangements require prior lender consent beyond specified thresholds for, among other things, undertaking new projects, issuing new securities, changes in management, mergers, sales of undertakings, material impairments and investments in subsidiaries. In addition, certain negative covenants may limit our ability to borrow additional funds or to incur additional liens, and/or provide for increased costs in case of breach. Certain financing arrangements also include financial covenants to maintain certain net-worth, liability and debt related ratios. We monitor compliance with our financial covenants on an ongoing basis. We also review our refinancing strategy and continue to plan for deployment of long-term funds to address any potential non-compliance and seek any waivers, if required. **For FY26, the Company is in compliance with all the covenants.**"

This discloses: (a) the existence of financial maintenance covenants on net-worth, liability, and debt ratios; (b) compliance as of FY26. It does NOT disclose: the specific thresholds, the actual measured metrics, or individual covenant headroom. The partial-data rule from MODULE_RULES.md therefore applies: actual headroom is not assessable from the data pool.

**Labeled assumption approach:** For a large Indian investment-grade corporate (AA+/BBB rated, operating company debt at low leverage), typical financial maintenance covenants in bilateral bank facilities and NCD trust deeds include:
- Minimum net-worth covenant: typically set at a floor of ~50–70% of the net-worth at facility inception (here, the Company had equity of ~₹10,533 crore at FY25 and ₹12,734 crore at FY26 — a floor of ~₹6,000–7,000 crore is a labeled assumption for indicative purposes).
- Maximum total debt / net-worth or debt / EBITDA: typically 2.0–3.0x debt/equity or 3.0–4.0x debt/EBITDA for an investment-grade CV OEM borrower.
- Minimum interest coverage (EBIT / interest): typically 2.0–3.0x for bank facilities.
These are LABELED ASSUMPTIONS, not disclosed thresholds. They are used only to give an indicative sense of the distance from breach.

| Covenant | Threshold | Current Actual | Headroom | Basis |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Max total debt / net-worth | Not disclosed. Assumed ≤ 2.0x for investment-grade (labeled assumption) | 0.38x (FY26 Key Financial Ratios row a: debt/equity = ₹4,817 cr / ₹12,734 cr) | ~81% indicative headroom vs. 2.0x threshold (actual 0.38x vs. assumed max 2.0x) | Partial-data assumption; actual threshold not disclosed. FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.221; Key Financial Ratios row (a) |
| Min interest service coverage | Not disclosed. Assumed ≥ 2.0x–3.0x for bank facilities (labeled assumption) | 10.65x (SEBI LODR Key Financial Ratios row c, FY26) | If threshold 2.0x: ~81% headroom. If threshold 3.0x: ~72% headroom | Partial-data assumption; actual threshold not disclosed. SEBI LODR Key Financial Ratios, May 13, 2026 |
| Min net-worth | Not disclosed. Assumed floor ~₹6,000–7,000 crore (labeled assumption) | ₹12,734 crore (FY26 balance sheet equity) | ~45–53% headroom vs. assumed floor | Partial-data assumption; actual floor not disclosed. SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, May 13, 2026 |
| Springing covenant (revolver utilisation trigger) | Fund-based limit ₹4,000 crore; ₹3,750 crore unutilised. No springing covenant disclosed. | N/A — revolver is largely undrawn | N/A | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.220–221 |
| Equity cure rights | Not disclosed. Standard Indian NCD trust deeds typically do not include equity cure provisions; bank facilities may. | N/A — no breach; cure rights academic at current metrics | Not assessable | Not disclosed in data pool |
| Negative covenants (incurrence-style) | Prior lender consent required for: new projects, issuing securities, management changes, mergers, sales of undertakings | Compliance confirmed for FY26 | Qualitative — no metric | FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.221 |

**Partial-data flag:** Covenant headroom is marked "Not assessable" for scoring purposes because specific financial thresholds are not disclosed. The indicative headroom numbers above are computed against labeled assumptions and must not be relied upon as factual outputs. The actual headrooms could be wider or narrower depending on the true threshold.

**Change-of-control / cross-default / rating-trigger scan:** Not individually disclosed in the data pool. The Board's Report (FY26 Annual Report, p.118) notes that the Iveco acquisition required and obtained lender approvals ("All approvals required so far have been timely received"), which implies change-of-control or material transaction consent requirements exist in at least some facilities. No rating-trigger pricing step-ups are disclosed.

### Covenant EBITDA Definition & Quality (required since headroom is computed on labeled assumptions)

| Item | Value / Description | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Covenant EBITDA definition | Not disclosed. Indian NCD trust deeds for AA+ issuers typically use EBITDA as defined in the Ind AS financial statements, without addbacks for exceptional items or FVTPL losses. Working capital limit lenders typically use reported EBIT / EBITDA. | Not disclosed in data pool. |
| Addbacks permitted (types) | Not disclosed. Based on typical Indian bank facility practice, exceptional items (e.g. stamp duty ₹962 cr) may or may not be added back depending on the facility agreement. FVTPL losses (₹2,418 cr) are very unlikely to be added back in standard Indian facilities. | Not proven from available data. Labeled assumption. |
| Addback caps / limits | Not disclosed. | Not disclosed in data pool. |
| Is covenant EBITDA materially above reported EBITDA? | Risk: if covenant definition follows reported Ind AS EBITDA (~₹8,641 cr) rather than Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr), then the relevant denominator for any coverage covenant is ~16% lower. At current metrics this makes no material difference to compliance, but it narrows the stated headroom gap somewhat. | Labeled assumption — actual definition unknown. Flag: "addback illusion" risk cannot be ruled out, but is low at current leverage levels. |

**Conclusion on EBITDA quality for covenants:** Even on the more conservative reported EBITDA basis of ~₹8,641 crore, TMCV's coverage ratios are so far above plausible minimum thresholds that the difference between reported and Underlying EBITDA is immaterial to covenant compliance. This changes materially if the Iveco acquisition closes and introduces EUR 3.8 billion of debt — at that point the EBITDA definition becomes critical.

---

## 3. Headroom & Breach Proximity

**Current balance sheet (pre-Iveco):**

| Metric | Value |
|---|---:|
| Tightest covenant (on labeled assumptions) | Min interest coverage — most likely to be a binding maintenance test in standard Indian facilities |
| Current interest coverage (EBIT/interest, disclosed basis) | 10.65x |
| Labeled minimum threshold (assumed) | 2.0–3.0x |
| Headroom on tightest covenant — indicative only, against assumed threshold (not assessable from filings) | ~72–81% indicative |
| EBITDA decline that would breach min coverage (2.0x threshold, Underlying EBITDA) | EBITDA would need to fall from ₹10,314 cr to ≤₹1,748 cr — a ~83% collapse — before breaching a 2.0x interest coverage floor. At a 3.0x threshold: to ≤₹2,622 cr — a ~75% collapse. Both are remote under any realistic operating scenario. |
| EBITDA decline that would breach min coverage (2.0x threshold, Reported EBITDA) | Reported EBITDA ₹8,641 cr would need to fall to ≤₹1,748 cr — an ~80% collapse. |
| Debt increase that would breach max leverage (2.0x debt/equity threshold, labeled) | Total debt would need to increase from ₹4,817 cr to ₹25,468 cr (2.0x × equity ₹12,734 cr) — additional debt of ~₹20,651 cr — before breaching the assumed threshold. |
| Iveco scenario — the dominant binary event | If the EUR 3.8 bn acquisition (~₹41,691 cr) closes, TMCV would draw the committed EUR 3.8 bn loan (arranged at HoldCo level). TMCV has provided a EUR 1.9 bn (~₹20,846 cr) corporate guarantee. The impact on TMCV's standalone consolidated ratios depends on the ultimate debt allocation and consolidation treatment — this cannot be computed without the final deal structure. However, the guarantee alone would consume all existing net-cash headroom and potentially trigger lender consent requirements. Management confirmed (FQ4 FY26 Earnings Call, May 13, 2026) that all required approvals have so far been obtained. |

**Note on debt service coverage ratio of 1.05x:** The SEBI LODR Key Financial Ratios (row b) discloses a debt service coverage ratio of 1.05x for FY26. This is computed including scheduled principal repayments of ₹3,657 crore due within one year (predominantly NCD maturities). The low ratio reflects a maturity concentration in FY27, not a structural inability to service the debt — the full ₹13,048 crore liquid pool is available to repay these obligations without any refinancing.

**Forward covenant risk — Iveco scenario:** This is the material covenant risk. The transaction would convert TMCV from a net-cash entity into a heavily net-debt entity. Lender consent rights on the acquisition have been exercised — approvals are being obtained. Post-closing, new covenant thresholds in the EUR 3.8 bn acquisition facility (arranged at HoldCo level via TMLCV Holdings B.V.) will apply. Those covenant terms are not disclosed. This agent marks forward covenant risk as significant and not assessable from the current data pool.

---

## 4. Coverage / Covenant Read

TMCV's earnings carry the interest charge by a very large margin on the current balance sheet. At 10.65x interest service coverage (as disclosed in the SEBI LODR Key Financial Ratios) and an EBITDA base that is 145% cash-backed, there is no coverage concern for the existing ₹4,817 crore of borrowings. The fixed-charge coverage ratio of 1.2x–1.4x looks tight but is shaped by a ₹3,657 crore debt maturity due within 12 months of March 31, 2026 — an obligation that is more than covered by the ₹13,048 crore liquid pool without needing to refinance or draw on operating cash flow.

Specific financial covenant thresholds are not disclosed in the Annual Report, so precise headroom is not assessable. The company confirms compliance with all covenants for FY26, and at current leverage (0.38x debt/equity, net-cash position), breach of any plausible minimum coverage or maximum leverage threshold would require an EBITDA collapse of 75–83% or a debt increase of ~5x from current levels — neither of which is remotely credible on a standalone basis.

The one situation that changes this picture entirely is the pending Iveco acquisition (EUR 3.8 bn, ~₹41,691 crore). Closing this deal would erase the net-cash position and introduce substantial new debt at the HoldCo level, with TMCV guaranteeing EUR 1.9 bn. At that point, coverage ratios tighten materially and covenant terms in the new acquisition facility — not yet disclosed — become the binding constraint. The current coverage read is therefore valid only for the pre-Iveco balance sheet; forward coverage assessment requires the deal terms.

---

## Partial Data Applied

- **No specific covenant thresholds disclosed:** The Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.221 confirms the existence of financial maintenance covenants on net-worth, liability, and debt ratios and confirms compliance for FY26, but does not state specific thresholds. Covenant headroom is "Not assessable" for scoring purposes. Labeled assumptions (max debt/equity ≤ 2.0x, min interest coverage ≥ 2.0–3.0x, min net-worth floor ~₹6,000–7,000 crore) are used for indicative breach-distance calculation only and are clearly marked as assumptions throughout.
- **No covenant EBITDA definition disclosed:** The addback policy and definition used in covenant calculations are unknown. The "addback illusion" risk is flagged but assessed as low at current leverage.
- **Pension / OPEB underfunding:** Not determinable from the data pool; not included in fixed-charge denominator.
