## 5D. Analyst Follow-Up Questions

**For each Red / Amber finding, the analyst must answer:**

1. **Iveco acquisition (RF-CAP-001, RF-CAP-004):** Will management disclose (a) a minimum post-deal return on invested capital threshold, (b) a synergy quantum with timeline, (c) a post-deal net-debt-to-EBITDA ceiling, and (d) the bridge loan lender and covenants? Is the Iveco rationale a one-off opportunistic deal or the start of a serial-acquirer pattern? Does the deal size (3.3x book equity) imply management believes TMCV is undervalued relative to Iveco — and if so, why not pursue buybacks instead?

2. **TMF Holdings amalgamation (RF-RPT-002, RF-OWN-004, RF-DISC-001):** What is the net financial impact on TMCV standalone (assets, liabilities, PAT, book value per share) if TMF Holdings and TMF Business Services merge in? Will minority shareholders have a quantified economics disclosure before the June 29, 2026 AGM vote? What percentage of FII/MF shareholders are expected to vote against? If institutional opposition blocks the scheme (needs 75%; promoter at 42.56% cannot carry it alone), what is the alternate structure?

3. **Incentive misalignment (RF-MGT-003):** Will the NRC introduce an explicit ROIC or TSR hurdle into the FY27 Balanced Scorecard, in light of the Iveco capital allocation decision? What are the specific PSU vesting criteria under the TML SLTI Scheme — are there TSR, EPS, or ROIC performance conditions, or is vesting purely time-based?

4. **RPT intensity (RF-RPT-001, RF-RPT-003):** Is the ₹2,925 crore in ICDs to subsidiaries callable on demand or term-locked? What interest rate is applied (₹58 crore interest income disclosed — implies ~2% yield on ₹2,774 crore, which may be below market)? What is the trend in total RPT outflows — is 8.2% of revenue a structural floor given Tata Cummins engine dependency, or is it expected to change?

5. **FVTPL adjustment (RF-DISC-002):** What is the FY27 expected FVTPL impact from TMF Holdings' equity investment portfolio? Will the portfolio be restructured or disclosed in detail as the amalgamation proceeds? Will management include a reconciliation of reported-to-normalised EPS in prepared remarks rather than relying on analyst computation?

6. **Noncyclical KPI (RF-DISC-001):** When will the noncyclical vs cyclical revenue split be disclosed in audited segment reporting, making the 2.7x CAGR claim independently verifiable? What is the definition of "noncyclical" for this purpose — is it aftermarket, telematics, spares, services only, or does it include defence?

7. **Company Secretary change:** Was the CS change from Sudipto Kumar Das to Ranjan Kumar voluntary or company-initiated? Is Ranjan Kumar a transfer from within the Tata Group, and does he have prior SEBI LODR compliance experience as Company Secretary of a listed entity?

8. **Chandrasekaran attendance:** Will the Non-Executive Chairman improve his board attendance from 44% (4/9 meetings in FY26) in FY27? Does the board have a minimum attendance expectation for the Chair, and was the 44% attendance rate the subject of NRC discussion?

---

## 6. What Would Change The Stewardship Verdict?

| Current Verdict | What Would Strengthen It | What Would Weaken It | Data Needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Standard / mixed | (1) Iveco deal closes with disclosed ROIC targets and post-deal leverage ceilings met; (2) NRC introduces ROIC or TSR metric into the FY27 Balanced Scorecard and discloses PSU vesting criteria; (3) TMF Holdings merger fails at 2nd AGM vote (June 29, 2026) or is restructured to protect minority holders; (4) Two–three more years of operating delivery matching promises builds a genuine track record | (1) Iveco deal closes with no disclosed return targets and subsequently destroys per-share value; (2) TMF Holdings merger passes despite institutional opposition and materialises as an ongoing PAT drag > ₹2,000 crore p.a.; (3) RPT intensity increases above 10% (Critical threshold); (4) FVTPL item becomes permanently large and management continues to lead with Underlying EBITDA without reporting the reported-EPS gap; (5) New management departure (CEO or CFO) without explanation | (1) Post-Iveco deal performance (FY28 onwards): delivered ROIC vs cost of capital; (2) 2nd AGM vote outcome and proxy-advisor recommendation on TMF merger; (3) FY27 Balanced Scorecard disclosures (are returns metrics added?); (4) FY27 audited segment results with noncyclical/cyclical split; (5) SEBI PIT/SAST individual KMP transaction archives |

---

## 7. Note To The Final Synthesizer

- **Stewardship verdict is "Standard / mixed."** The evidence for this is: genuine operating delivery in FY26 (promises kept on net cash, EBITDA margin, H2 recovery, FCF) set against a large, narrative-only capital allocation decision (Iveco at 3.3x book equity, zero financial targets) and a pending intra-group restructuring (TMF Holdings merger) that transfers NBFC losses into the listed entity without quantified minority-shareholder disclosure.

- **Capital allocation record is mixed, not value-creative on a full-picture basis.** The underlying CV business earns exceptional returns (auto ROCE 72.3%, negative working capital, FCF ₹12,438 crore in FY26). In FY26 itself, cash was used well: debt repaid, first dividend paid, capex measured. But the committed Iveco acquisition would consume the entire net cash pile (₹13,713 crore), require a bridge loan for the remainder (total €3.8 billion), and reverse three years of deliberate deleveraging — all with no ROIC target or synergy commitment. Per-share FCF of ₹3.38 in FY26 would compress to ~₹2.38–₹2.70 post-Iveco interest cost (inference, not from filings). History of completed M&A with measurable returns does not exist for this entity as a standalone issuer.

- **Incentives and ownership do not align management with minority holders on per-share value.** The Balanced Scorecard rewards volumes and absolute EBITDA (size metrics); no ROIC, EPS, FCF/share, or TSR metric is named. PSU performance criteria are undisclosed. CEO personal ownership is 0.006% (demerger-allotted, not market-purchased). Tata Sons' 42.56% stake is structural, not evidence of current-price conviction. This structure does not discourage empire-building.

- **Biggest governance risk is the pending Iveco acquisition (RF-CAP-001, RF-CAP-004) combined with the TMF Holdings merger (RF-RPT-002, RF-OWN-004).** Together, these two decisions commit over ₹44,000 crore of capital without disclosed return commitments and with the controlling shareholder directing NBFC liabilities into the listed entity without minority consent. Severity: 9 High red flags, 0 Critical. Governance risk score is 60 (floor applied from §24 Filter 4).

- **No hard disqualifier was flagged by `business-model/01_disqualifier-scan`.** All 8 disqualifiers are clear. No audit qualification, no promoter pledging, no RPT above 25% of revenue, no restatement, no enforcement action, no going concern.

- **§24 rejector filters tripped:**
  - *Filter 4 (serial-acquirer):* Iveco deal at 3.3x book equity with bare strategic-fit language. Cap applied: CapAlloc max 50; GovRisk floor 60.
  - *Filter 6 (unaligned owner — borderline):* TMF Holdings merger is a concrete intra-group liability transfer. Cap applied: ShFriendliness max 55; GovRisk floor 55 (superseded by Filter 4's 60 floor).
  - Filters 1 (integrity), 2 (turnaround), 3 (high debt on current balance sheet), and 5 (fast-changing industry) did not trip on current evidence.

- **Partial-data caps applied:** (a) Shareholder friendliness capped at 55 (most restrictive of partial-insider-data cap at 60 and Filter 6 borderline cap at 55); (b) Disclosure candor capped at 65 (Q1/Q2 FY26 transcripts not in pool); (c) Capital allocation capped at 50 (Filter 4, most restrictive vs single-period cap at 65). These limit the conviction of the incentive, ownership, and capital-allocation reads.

- **Biggest missing data point:** SEBI PIT/SAST individual insider-transaction disclosures (director and KMP buy/sell) since November 2025 listing — would confirm whether any insider bought TMCV shares at market since listing, which would be the single strongest alignment signal absent from the current read.

- **Explicit handoff:** This module supersedes the `business-model/11_capital-allocation-governance` quick-read. The master synthesizer should treat this module's governance verdict (Standard / mixed), Governance Score (51), Confidence-Adjusted Score (33), and Governance Rating (Weak) as the primary governance read. The business-model/11 quick-read's Iveco severity score (50/100) and its capital-allocation score are subsumed by this module's more detailed analysis.

---

## 8. Simple Summary

- Management delivered on operating promises in FY26: net cash achieved, margin expanded, two consensus beats — the short-track record within management's control is clean.
- The Iveco acquisition (€3.8 billion, 3.3x book equity) commits over 3 years of accumulated free cash flow to a deal with no disclosed ROIC target, no synergy figure, no leverage ceiling, and a sub-50% historical base rate for value creation; the balance sheet TMCV spent three years building is now committed away on a narrative.
- Pay rewards volumes and absolute EBITDA — size metrics — with no ROIC or TSR hurdle; the incentive structure structurally does not discourage the Iveco deal.
- Tata Sons controls 42.56% via demerger allotment, no pledging, clean; CEO personal stake is 0.006% (demerger-allotted, not purchased); no open-market buying by any insider is disclosed.
- Minority-shareholder protection is formally adequate (62.5% independent board, zero dual-class, no staggered board, no poison pill) but the pending TMF Holdings merger — which transfers NBFC losses of ₹2,478 crore per year into the listed CV entity — is a concrete alignment conflict that the 2nd AGM vote will resolve or confirm.
- Management is honest when asked in Q&A (commodity costs quantified, Iveco delay named, margin guidance corrected downward) but does not proactively disclose negatives — warranty KAM, CARO gap, TMF amalgamation financial impact, Iveco return targets were all left for filings or analyst questions to surface.
- No hard disqualifier was flagged: clean audit opinion, no promoter pledging, RPTs below 25% threshold, no restatement, no regulatory enforcement.
- This module is useful for the master synthesizer: primary governance sources are fresh (FY26 Annual Report, dated June 6, 2026), scores are grounded in evidence, and the Iveco + TMF governance events are actively developing and material to the investment case.

---
## memo.md

# TMCV — Colleague Memo
## Tata Motors Limited (Commercial Vehicles) | NSE: TMCV | INR | 2026-06-07

**Current price:** ₹369.15 (S&P Capital IQ Key Stats export, 2026-06-07)

**Decision: Watchlist — a genuinely good CV franchise trading at modest value is blocked from a Buy rating by an unresolved binary risk (the Iveco acquisition) that could destroy ~80% of equity value, and by governance that has already transferred group costs to minority holders twice in a single year.**

---

## 1. The Decision at a Glance

| Item | Answer |
|---|---|
| Rating | Watchlist |
| Suggested action | No position now; re-rate when Iveco financing structure is confirmed and TMF Holdings AGM vote outcome is known |
| Position stance | Zero — not even a starter |
| Time horizon | 12 months |
| Expected return (probability-weighted) | −4.4% |
| Downside risk | −19% to −81% depending on Iveco scenario |
| Risk/reward | −0.23 (negative; expected outcome is a loss at current price) |
| Confidence /100 | 47 |
| Data sufficiency /100 | 68 |
| Thesis type | Sector-cycle + Company-specific (conditional on Iveco outcome) |
| Rating cap | Filter 4 (serial-acquirer/very-large-deal) + Filter 6 (unaligned owner — borderline): maximum Watchlist until Iveco financing is resolved and minority-holder protection evidence exists |
| §24 Avoid-Big-Risks filters tripped | Filter 4 (serial-acquirer/very-large-deal) TRIPPED; Filter 6 (unaligned owner) TRIPPED — borderline |

*Risk/reward is inverted from the usual convention when the expected return is negative: a score of −0.23 means the probability-weighted loss exceeds the probability-weighted gain at current prices.*

---

## 2. What the Company Does

TMCV makes and sells trucks, buses, and light commercial vehicles — the heavy metal that moves freight and people. It was demerged from the old Tata Motors group and listed separately on the NSE in November 2025. It is now India's largest pure-play commercial vehicle (CV) maker.

**Revenue model in plain terms:**

> Revenue = (vehicles sold) × (average price per vehicle) + (spare parts and service revenue)

The business earned ₹83,855 crore in revenue in FY26 (the financial year ended March 2026). India accounted for 88% of that. The company sells in 52 countries, but the India freight and infrastructure cycle is what drives results. Material costs — steel, aluminium, copper — eat 67.7 paise of every rupee of revenue, so commodity prices are the biggest variable the company cannot control.

---

## 3. The Variant Perception

**What everyone already knows.** TMCV is India's CV market leader (35.7% overall share, 55% share in heavy commercial vehicles), recently listed as a pure-play, generating genuine cash returns — free cash flow (FCF; cash left after running the business and paying for capital equipment) of ₹12,438 crore, return on capital employed (ROCE; the profit earned on each ₹100 of capital in the auto business) of 72%, and three years of margin improvement. [FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), FY26; SEBI LODR Preliminary Results, May 13, 2026]

**What is probably priced in.** The Iveco acquisition risk and its financing uncertainty. A reverse-DCF (working backwards from today's price to find what growth rate the market must be assuming) at a 13% cost of capital (what that money costs to raise) shows the current price of ₹369.15 implies only ~1.7% free-cash-flow growth per year over ten years — roughly 8–9 percentage points below India's nominal GDP growth. The market is already applying a large discount for the binary deal risk. [Valuation module, `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/valuation/99_valuation-synthesis.md`]

**What the engine thinks may be missed.** The compounding effect of the TMF Holdings merger on reported earnings quality. The FVTPL loss (a non-cash accounting mark-to-market charge on financial investments — explained in the glossary) of ₹2,418 crore already cut reported profit after tax (PAT) by 80% in FY26. If both the Iveco bridge loan and the TMF merger close in FY27, the hit to reported earnings will be severe and will likely force analysts to reset forecasts sharply downward — a risk that may not be in current models. [SEBI LODR Note 8, May 13, 2026; FY26 Annual Report, MD&A, p.215]

**What evidence would prove the engine is actually different.** Nothing. The Iveco risk, the governance concerns, and the earnings noise are all visible in public filings. There is no proprietary data point here.

**There is no proven variant perception.** The confidence cap of 60 for no proven edge applies and drives the final confidence score to 47.

---

## 4. Why It Could Work — Bull Case

**Driver 1: Iveco is blocked by regulators or financed with equity, not debt.**
French and Spanish regulators have not yet approved the deal. If they block it, or if management funds the acquisition primarily with an equity raise (~€1 billion is discussed in web sources, unverified) or a Tata Capital stake sale, the balance sheet stays in net-cash territory. The Iveco overhang — the biggest reason the stock is cheap — lifts. Bull-case price target: ₹530, implying +43% from ₹369.15. [FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.116; SEBI LODR Note 7, May 13, 2026]

**Driver 2: The underlying business is genuinely cheap on EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation — the operating profit before non-cash and financing items) multiples.**
The stock trades at 12.5× FY26 Underlying EBITDA of ₹10,314 crore. The six-month average since listing has been ~14.9×. A re-rating back to the mean implies ₹423–465 per share — a 15–26% return, with no improvement in business performance required, just a removal of the Iveco discount. [S&P Capital IQ Multiples export, June 2026; Valuation module]

**Driver 3: Market leadership is real and growing.**
TMCV holds 35.7% overall CV market share and 55% in heavy commercial vehicles, with 314 dealer touchpoints and a negative working-capital cycle (−31 days — the company collects cash from dealers faster than it pays suppliers, meaning customers partly fund the business). The gross margin of 36.8% is 820 basis points above Ashok Leyland, its closest rival. [FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS), FY26; SEBI LODR Preliminary Results, May 13, 2026]

**Driver 4: Non-cyclical revenues are growing and add a floor.**
The parts and service business, which is less sensitive to new-vehicle demand cycles, grew 18.2% in FY26. This narrows (though does not eliminate) the earnings swings through the freight cycle. [FY26 Annual Report, MD&A, p.215]

**Driver 5: India infrastructure spending supports medium-term CV demand.**
The government's capital-expenditure programme in roads and logistics is a structural demand driver for heavy trucks. If festive-season freight demand picks up in H2 FY27 (October–March 2027), full-year earnings could partially rescue the soft first half. [Earnings module, `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/99_earnings-synthesis.md`]

---

## 5. Why It Could Fail — Bear Case and the Killer Risk

**Risk 1: Iveco closes fully debt-funded — the killer risk.**
If TMCV pays the full ~₹41,691 crore (€3.8 billion) acquisition price using the bridge loan already in place (at approximately Euribor + 102.5 basis points — web-sourced, unverified), the balance sheet transforms from net cash of ₹8,231 crore to net debt of ~₹33,000 crore in one step. Annual interest jumps by ₹3,000–4,000 crore. The intrinsic value of equity on this scenario is approximately ₹66 per share — versus the current price of ₹369.15. That is an 82% decline. [FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.116; SEBI LODR Note 7, May 13, 2026]

**Risk 2: Commodity cost inflation hits harder in Q1 FY27 than already guided.**
The CFO stated on the May 13, 2026 earnings call that Q1 FY27 commodity costs are "significantly higher" than Q4's already-material 100 basis-point (one basis point = 0.01%) impact. The company has no contractual cost pass-through mechanism — it cannot automatically raise prices when steel, aluminium, or copper costs jump. A margin print below 11% in Q1 FY27 would tell the market the FY23–FY26 margin expansion is partly reversing. [FQ4 FY26 Earnings Transcript, Q&A, May 13, 2026]

**Risk 3: The TMF Holdings merger passes at the June 29, 2026 AGM.**
TMF Holdings is a financial-services subsidiary with ₹2,478 crore of negative PAT contribution in FY26. The promoter (Tata Sons, 42.56% stake) wants to merge it into TMCV. This requires 75% shareholder approval — so the promoter cannot carry it alone and needs minority holders to back it. If it passes, an NBFC (non-banking financial company) that is currently losing money gets folded into the listed CV entity, with no quantified economic terms disclosed to minority holders before the vote. [FY26 Annual Report, Note 45; Note 11; SEBI LODR Note 11, May 13, 2026]

**Risk 4: FY27 consensus is stale and overstates the earnings bar.**
The only consensus available is a snapshot from April 27, 2026, showing analysts expecting ₹19.17 normalised EPS (earnings per share — profit divided by the number of shares) for FY27, up 20.5% from FY26. That model does not include Iveco bridge-loan interest and was built before Q4 FY26 actuals were reported. Normalised EPS in FY26 missed the April 2026 consensus by −10.7%. The bar will almost certainly be revised downward. [S&P Capital IQ Key Stats export, April 27, 2026 snapshot]

**Risk 5: Governance structure structurally disadvantages minority holders.**
The management bonus scorecard rewards total volumes and absolute EBITDA — size metrics, not per-share returns. CEO Girish Wagh owns 0.006% of the company (demerger-allotted, not market-purchased). These incentives do not discourage deals that grow the company at the cost of per-share value. [FY26 Annual Report, CG Report, p.233; Board's Report, p.121]

---

## 6. Avoid-Big-Risks Check

**Filter 4 — Serial-acquirer / very-large-deal: TRIPPED.**
The Iveco acquisition at ₹41,691 crore (3.3 times TMCV's own book equity — the net assets on the balance sheet) meets the "near or above the company's own value" test. Management has provided zero return-on-capital target, no synergy number, and no leverage ceiling. The public justification is "complementary portfolio" — that is bare strategic-fit language with no financial substance. Cap applied: capital allocation maximum 50/100; conviction maximum Watchlist. [FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.116; FQ4 FY26 Transcript, Q&A, p.9]

**Filter 6 — Unaligned owner — borderline: TRIPPED.**
The TMF Holdings merger transfers NBFC losses (₹2,478 crore negative PAT, ₹2,418 crore FVTPL loss) into the listed entity at the promoter's direction, without quantified minority-shareholder economics disclosed before the shareholder vote. This is a concrete transfer of financial harm from the parent group to minority holders. Cap applied: shareholder-friendliness maximum 55/100; conviction maximum Watchlist. [FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.117; Note 45; Q3 FY26 press release, Jan 29, 2026]

**Current survival read (pre-Iveco).** Net cash ₹8,231 crore. Gross debt ₹4,817 crore. Gross leverage 0.47× EBITDA. Investment-grade ratings: CRISIL/ICRA/CARE AA+ Stable; S&P BBB Stable. No stress scenario from −30% to −60% EBITDA breaks this structure. No covenant breach. No going-concern risk on the pre-deal balance sheet. [SEBI LODR Preliminary Results, May 13, 2026; FY26 Annual Report (Ind AS)]

**Filters 1, 2, 3, and 5 (partial).** Filter 1 (crooks/integrity): not tripped — clean disqualifier scan, unqualified audit opinion, no enforcement. Filter 2 (turnaround): not tripped — market leader with positive operating cash flow. Filter 3 (high debt): not tripped on the current balance sheet — will trip if Iveco closes fully debt-funded. Filter 5 (fast-changing industry): tripped — EV transition creates technology-cycle risk; thesis flagged as a sector/technology-cycle bet, not a durable compounder.

---

## 7. Valuation and Fair Value

**Method:** Sum-of-the-parts (SOTP — valuing the CV business and the listed Tata Capital stake separately, then adding them up), collapsed to a single-segment EV/EBITDA multiple at 12×–14× FY26 Underlying EBITDA of ₹10,314 crore. Anchored to Ashok Leyland as the pure-play peer. Cross-checked against own-history mean reversion. [Valuation module synthesis, `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/valuation/99_valuation-synthesis.md`]

**EV** (enterprise value — market cap plus net debt, or the total cost to buy the whole business) **bridge:** Market cap ₹1,35,933 crore minus net cash ₹7,435 crore (broad definition) = EV ₹1,28,498 crore. EV/EBITDA at current price: 12.68×.

| Scenario | Fair Value (₹/share) | Return from ₹369.15 |
|---|---:|---:|
| Bull (Iveco blocked or equity-funded) | ₹520–530 | +41–43% |
| Base (Iveco closes with partial equity) | ₹380–430 | +3–17% |
| Bear (Iveco closes full bridge debt) | ₹300–346 | −6–19% |
| Event-risk floor (full debt + margin collapse) | ~₹66–70 | ~−81% |

**What the price implies.** The reverse-DCF at 13% cost of capital requires only ~1.7% FCF growth per year over ten years. The company has demonstrated 5–7% revenue growth and 550 basis points of EBITDA margin expansion over FY23–FY26. The market is pricing in a permanent slowdown — a conservative but understandable stance given the Iveco binary.

**Margin of safety** (the gap between current price and the point at which an investor begins losing money in the bear case): Narrow — the bear case is 6–19% below today's price. This is not enough cushion when the worst-case scenario is an 82% decline.

**Why the stock looks cheap but may not be.** The apparent 47% EV/EBITDA discount versus Ashok Leyland is mostly a capital-structure artefact — Ashok Leyland's EV is inflated by its finance arm's ₹46,856 crore of net debt. On a debt-neutral comparison, TMCV trades at roughly an 11% discount, which is genuine but modest. The more likely explanation for the discount is rational pricing of the Iveco binary, not market mispricing.

**Cross-method disagreement.** The standalone normalised DCF (discounted cash flow — valuing a business by estimating all future cash flows and discounting them back to today) produced ₹147–190 per share, far below the SOTP range. The thesis treats this as a floor, not a central estimate, because the WACC (weighted average cost of capital — the blended cost of equity and debt funding) used is likely overstated given the six-month listed beta. The SOTP and own-history mean-reversion methods are the primary anchors.

---

## 8. Catalysts

**June 29, 2026 — 2nd AGM (PROVEN, DATED).**
Two votes: the ₹4/share dividend (₹1,473 crore total, positive but small) and the TMF Holdings amalgamation special resolution (requires 75% majority). Bullish trigger: dividend passes, TMF merger rejected or deferred. Bearish trigger: both pass — confirms TMCV will absorb NBFC liabilities permanently. This is the single most actionable near-term event. [FY26 Annual Report, Note 45; AGM Notice]

**September 1, 2026 — 8.50% NCD maturity ₹801 crore (PROVEN, DATED).**
First large post-demerger bond repayment. Watch whether it is retired from cash (confirms self-funding) or rolled into new debt (implies cash is being preserved for the Iveco bridge). [Balance-sheet-survival module]

**Q2 FY27 July–September 2026 — Iveco regulatory close (VAGUE, BINARY).**
The single most consequential financial event. French and Spanish approvals are pending. An activist hedge fund holds 6–7% of Iveco and may complicate the process. Bullish trigger: closes with material equity component, management discloses ROIC targets (return on capital — profit earned on each unit of capital invested) and synergies. Bearish trigger: closes on full bridge-debt terms; annual interest ₹3,000–4,000 crore hits the P&L immediately. This is a §24 Filter 4 event — it must NOT be read as a conviction-lifting catalyst. It is a binary risk event. [SEBI LODR Note 7, May 13, 2026; web-sourced bridge-loan details, unverified]

**Late July–early August 2026 — Q1 FY27 results (VAGUE).**
First real test of whether commodity inflation is contained. Management has already guided it will be "significantly higher" than Q4's 100 basis-point impact. Bullish trigger: Underlying EBITDA margin ≥12%; management upgrades full-year tone. Bearish trigger: margin falls below 11.5%; EPS consensus cut. [FQ4 FY26 Earnings Transcript, May 13, 2026]

**December 2026–March 2027 — NCD maturities totalling ₹1,000 crore (PROVEN, DATED).**
First post-Iveco-close refinancing signals. If rolled at 100+ basis points above the existing weighted average cost of ~7.3–7.5%, it is the first tangible evidence that post-deal leverage has hurt TMCV's credit access. [Balance-sheet-survival module]

**Catalyst verdict: the calendar skews bearish-to-neutral.** The only unambiguously positive event is the ₹4/share dividend, which is small relative to the risks above it. The dominant event (Iveco close) is vague and binary. Catalyst timing score: 52/100.

---

## 9. Scenario Model

Current price: ₹369.15 | Shares outstanding: 3,682 million

| Scenario | Probability | Return | Price Target | What Must Happen |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Bull — Iveco blocked / equity-funded | 20% | +43% | ₹530 | Regulators block the deal OR Tata Capital stake sale + rights issue funds it entirely; no new net debt; overhang lifts; EV/EBITDA re-rates to ~15×; commodity costs ease in H2 FY27 |
| Base — Iveco closes with partial equity | 35% | +14% | ₹420 | Iveco closes with ~€1 bn equity and €2.875 bn bridge (web-sourced, unverified); net debt rises to ~₹24,700 crore (~2.4× EBITDA); earnings drag partially offset by Iveco EBITDA; consensus reset −10 to −15% |
| Bear — Iveco closes full bridge debt | 30% | −19% | ₹300 | Full €3.875 bn bridge, no equity, ~₹33,000 crore net debt (~3.2× EBITDA); annual interest ₹3,000–4,000 crore; FY27 consensus cut 20–30%; TMF merger also passes at AGM |
| Event-risk floor — full debt + margin collapse | 15% | −81% | ₹70 | Full-debt Iveco + commodity-driven EBITDA margin below 9% + TMF merger passes; consensus slashed; multiple collapses to distressed-CV-cycle levels |

**Probability check:** 20% + 35% + 30% + 15% = 100% ✓

**Probability-weighted expected return:**
(0.20 × +43%) + (0.35 × +14%) + (0.30 × −19%) + (0.15 × −81%)
= +8.6% + 4.9% − 5.7% − 12.15% = **−4.4%**

**Probability-weighted price target:**
(0.20 × ₹530) + (0.35 × ₹420) + (0.30 × ₹300) + (0.15 × ₹70) = **₹353.5**

Cross-check: (₹353.5 − ₹369.15) / ₹369.15 = −4.2% ≈ −4.4% ✓ (small rounding from integer price targets)

**The expected outcome is a small loss, not a gain. This confirms the Watchlist rating.** The scenario model is highly sensitive to the event-risk-floor probability: a 5-percentage-point shift in that probability changes the expected return by approximately ±4%.

---

## 10. What Would Change Our Mind

Five specific, observable kill criteria — events that would force a downgrade or exit:

**1. Iveco closes fully debt-funded with no equity component.**
This is the primary kill trigger. Equity value falls from ~₹380–430 (base case) to ~₹66 per share on the intrinsic model. Net debt rises to ~₹33,000 crore. Annual interest of ₹3,000–4,000 crore eliminates FCF that currently covers shareholders. Monitor: NSE/BSE exchange intimations on deal close and financing terms; SEBI LODR Note updates on the bridge loan.

**2. TMF Holdings merger passes at the AGM (June 29, 2026) AND FVTPL losses repeat at ₹2,000+ crore in FY27.**
This confirms the governance discount is structural, not temporary. The reported PAT noise becomes permanent. Monitor: scrutinizer report from the AGM published on NSE/BSE; FY27 quarterly reported vs normalised PAT gap.

**3. Q1 FY27 Underlying EBITDA margin falls below 11%.**
This tells us the three-year margin expansion is reversing, not simply pausing. The full-year consensus EPS of ₹19.17 becomes unreachable even without Iveco. Monitor: Q1 FY27 results press release (late July/early August 2026).

**4. India CV total industry volume (TIV — total new vehicle sales to the market) contracts more than 10% in FY27.**
A 10% volume drop triggers a 41–46% fall in normalised EPS, per the company's own operating-leverage structure. Combined with Iveco interest, EPS could go deeply negative. Monitor: monthly SIAM wholesale data; diesel-price movements at the pump.

**5. Post-Iveco NCD maturities refinanced at 100+ basis points above existing weighted average cost (~7.3–7.5%).**
This is the first tangible sign that post-deal leverage has damaged TMCV's credit standing. A rating-agency move to Negative outlook is the likely companion. Monitor: September and December 2026 NCD refinancing terms; CRISIL/ICRA rating watch announcements.

**What would raise confidence instead:** (a) TMF merger is defeated at the AGM — signals institutional shareholders are protecting minority holders; (b) Iveco confirmed equity-funded or abandoned — removes the binary; (c) Q1 FY27 margin holds ≥12% — confirms the commodity headwind is contained. If all three conditions are met, re-evaluate a starter position at ₹340–360, with a maximum 3–4% portfolio weight given cyclicality.

---

## 11. Second-Best Bet

**Ashok Leyland (NSE: AL)** — India's second-largest CV maker and TMCV's primary competitor in medium and heavy trucks.

Ashok Leyland benefits from the same India CV freight cycle and infrastructure-spend demand without the Iveco binary risk. Its finance subsidiary carries material debt, but the CV business itself is clean and has a longer track record of standalone public-company operation. If the core thesis is "India CV demand recovery," Ashok Leyland is a less event-risk-burdened way to express it. If Iveco closes on full-bridge-debt terms, Ashok Leyland becomes the clear pure-play while TMCV becomes a leveraged industrial-integration story.

---

## 12. What We Would Need to Get More Confident

**Single highest-value data request:** The Iveco acquisition financing structure — specifically the equity-to-debt split in the final deal financing. This one data point determines whether the bear case is ₹300–346 (partial equity) or ₹66 (fully debt-funded), a difference of approximately ₹280 per share. Everything else is secondary.

Other gaps that matter but can wait:
- Financial covenant thresholds for all active financing facilities — would convert "covenant headroom: Not assessable" to a precise number.
- Post-Q4 FY26 analyst estimate revisions to FY27 consensus (revision history unavailable from the Capital IQ export).
- TMF Holdings FVTPL portfolio composition and likely FY27 trajectory — critical for estimating FY27 reported PAT.
- Iveco EBITDA margin and revenue profile — needed to model post-deal consolidated earnings contribution.

---

## 13. Bottom Line

- **What it does.** TMCV makes trucks, buses, and commercial vehicles. India is 88% of revenue. It is India's largest CV maker by market share and the only pure-play listed CV company since demerging from Tata Motors in November 2025.

- **Why it may go up.** The underlying business is genuinely good — 72% ROCE, ₹12,438 crore free cash flow, net-cash balance sheet today, 35.7% market share, gross margins 820 basis points above the nearest rival. If the Iveco deal is blocked or equity-funded, the stock should re-rate from 12.5× to ~14.9× EBITDA, producing a 15–43% return.

- **Why it may go down.** Iveco closes on full bridge-debt terms and intrinsic equity value falls to ~₹66 per share — an 82% decline. Even without Iveco, commodity cost pressure in Q1 FY27 is already confirmed as "significantly higher" than Q4's 100 basis-point impact, H1 FY27 earnings will likely disappoint the stale consensus, and the TMF Holdings merger could permanently embed NBFC losses in the listed entity.

- **What data supports the thesis.** FY26 audited results (Ind AS, confirmed by SEBI LODR, May 2026) confirm 12.3% Underlying EBITDA margin, ₹12,438 crore FCF, net cash ₹8,231 crore, and auto ROCE 72.3%. These are high-quality primary-filing numbers.

- **What data is missing.** The single most important: whether Iveco is financed with equity or debt. Also missing: post-Q4 consensus revisions, covenant thresholds, and Iveco's own financial profile.

- **Buy now or wait.** Wait. The next 60–90 days contain the three events (AGM June 29, Iveco financing disclosure, Q1 FY27 results) that should be observable before any capital is at risk. Waiting costs nothing. Buying before these resolve could cost 19–81% of capital in the downside scenarios.

- **The one thing to watch next.** The 2nd AGM vote outcome on June 29, 2026 — specifically whether institutional holders defeat the TMF Holdings amalgamation. A defeat signals the governance environment is more protective of minority holders than current evidence shows; a pass confirms it is not, and the governance discount becomes structural.

---

## Glossary

**EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation)** — the operating profit before non-cash charges (depreciation, amortisation) and before financing costs; a common measure of recurring business profitability. TMCV's FY26 Underlying EBITDA was ₹10,314 crore on an underlying (adjusted) basis.

**Underlying EBITDA / Underlying EPS / Normalised PAT** — the company's own adjusted figures that strip out non-recurring or non-cash items such as the FVTPL loss, to show what the core business earned. Not the same as GAAP (statutory accounting) numbers.

**ROCE (return on capital employed)** — the profit the business earns on each ₹100 of capital deployed; TMCV's auto-segment ROCE was 72.3% in FY26, meaning the CV business earned ₹72 for every ₹100 invested in it.

**FCF (free cash flow)** — cash generated from operations minus capital spending on property, plant, and equipment; the cash the business actually produces that can be used for dividends, debt repayment, or acquisitions. TMCV's FY26 FCF was ₹12,438 crore (partly inflated by a ₹4,088 crore contract-liability build; normalised FCF is approximately ₹10,000–11,000 crore).

**Net debt** — total borrowings minus cash and liquid investments; a negative net-debt number means a net-cash position. TMCV had net cash of ₹8,231 crore before the Iveco deal.

**EV (enterprise value)** — the total cost to buy the whole business: market cap plus net debt (or minus net cash); the price a buyer would pay to own the business outright. TMCV's EV at the current price is ₹1,28,498 crore.

**EV/EBITDA** — enterprise value divided by EBITDA; how many years of operating profit an investor is paying for the whole business; a common multiple for capital-intensive businesses like vehicle manufacturers. TMCV trades at 12.68× vs a six-month average of ~14.9×.

**EPS (earnings per share)** — profit divided by the number of shares outstanding; the per-share profit attributable to shareholders. TMCV's FY26 normalised EPS was ₹15.91; the consensus forecast for FY27 (stale, April 2026) was ₹19.17.

**PAT (profit after tax)** — the statutory net profit after all charges including tax; TMCV's FY26 reported PAT was ₹3,030 crore, heavily depressed by the FVTPL loss.

**FVTPL (fair value through profit or loss)** — an accounting treatment for certain financial investments (here, the TMF Holdings NBFC investment portfolio) where gains and losses are recognised in the income statement each period, even if no cash changes hands. The ₹2,418 crore FVTPL loss in FY26 reduced reported PAT by 80% but was a non-cash charge.

**NBFC (non-banking financial company)** — a financial institution that lends or invests but does not hold a banking licence; TMF Holdings is a Tata-group NBFC that the promoter wants to merge into TMCV.

**Reverse-DCF** — working backwards from today's stock price to find what growth rate the market must be assuming; if that implied growth rate looks too pessimistic, it suggests the stock may be underpriced. At ₹369.15, the implied FCF growth rate is ~1.7% per year.

**WACC (weighted average cost of capital)** — the blended rate the business must earn to satisfy both its lenders and its shareholders; if ROCE exceeds WACC, the business is creating value.

**Margin of safety** — the gap between what something is worth (fair value) and what you pay; the larger the gap, the more protection against being wrong. At ₹369.15, the margin of safety to the bear-case fair value of ₹300–346 is only 6–19% — thin.

**Basis point (bps)** — one-hundredth of one percentage point (0.01%); used for small changes in margins, interest rates, or spreads. 100 basis points = 1 percentage point.

**NCD (non-convertible debenture)** — a bond issued by a company that cannot be converted into equity; TMCV has ₹4,817 crore of outstanding NCDs that mature in 2026–2027.

**SOTP (sum-of-the-parts)** — a valuation method that values each piece of a business separately and adds them up; used here to value the CV business and the Tata Capital stake independently before combining.

**ROIC (return on invested capital)** — the profit the business earns on each ₹100 of money put into it (similar to ROCE but typically applied to the total invested base including goodwill); used here in the context of the Iveco deal, where management has disclosed no ROIC target.

**TSR (total shareholder return)** — the total gain for a shareholder including share price change plus dividends received; a return-based metric that management bonuses here do not explicitly reward.

**RPT (related-party transaction)** — a commercial deal between the company and a connected party (parent, subsidiary, affiliate, or director); TMCV's RPT outflows were 8.2% of revenue in FY26, above the 5% threshold that triggers closer scrutiny.

**WAC (weighted average cost)** — here, the blended interest rate across all outstanding debt; TMCV's current WAC is approximately 7.3–7.5%.

**SIAM** — Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers; publishes monthly wholesale vehicle-dispatch data, the primary source for CV industry volume tracking.

**SEBI LODR** — Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) regulations; the rules under which listed Indian companies file quarterly results and material disclosures to the stock exchange (NSE/BSE).

**IiAS** — Institutional Investor Advisory Services; a proxy-advisory firm whose vote recommendations matter for special resolutions requiring 75% approval, because institutional shareholders often follow them.


---

The EstimatesReport.xls and Company Comparable Analysis.xls failures are the most material — forward estimates are partially recoverable from the Key Stats tab and the earnings transcript, while peer comps are partially visible in the Credit Health Panel screenshot.

---

## 1A. Jurisdiction & Reporting Regime

| Item | Detected Value | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Primary listing country / exchange | India — National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) and BSE | Ticker NSEI:TMCV; SEBI Listing Regulations cited throughout filings; letters addressed to BSE and NSE [Annual Report filing letter, Jun-06-2026] |
| Filing regime | India / SEBI-LODR (Reg 33 for quarterly results, Reg 34 for annual report) | SEBI (LODR) Regulations 2015, Reg 33 and Reg 52 citations in quarterly-filing cover letters [Q3 FY26 filing, Jan-29-2026; Q4 FY26 filing, May-13-2026] |
| Reporting standard | Ind AS (Indian Accounting Standards) | "Consolidated Audited Financial Results" per Ind AS; equity-accounted investees terminology; "Items that will not be reclassified to profit or loss" OCI format [Q4 FY26 preliminary results, May-13-2026] |
| Reporting currency (and scale) | INR (Indian Rupees), reported in crores (₹ in crores) in primary filings; Capital IQ exports in INR millions | Q4/FY26 results statement header: "(₹ in crores)"; Capital IQ Key Stats tab: "In Millions of the reported currency … Currency: INR" |
| Fiscal-year end | March 31 (Indian fiscal year) | "Year ended March 31, 2026" [Q4 FY26 preliminary results, May-13-2026]; FY26 annual report dated Jun-06-2026 |

**Downstream agents must cite the Indian-regime local equivalents:** SEBI LODR quarterly results (not "10-Q"), Integrated Annual Report (not "10-K"), AGM Notice / Board's Report (not "DEF 14A"), SAST/PIT/shareholding-pattern filings (not "Form 4/13D"). Do NOT mark any US form missing — no US forms are expected for an Indian-listed entity.

## 6. Sufficiency Verdict

- **Verdict:** Partial
- **Reason:** Core financial data (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow, current price, share count, forward estimates, segment data) are fully available and support at least four valuation methods; however, the peer comps workbook (Company Comparable Analysis.xls) failed extraction, leaving structured peer multiples inaccessible from the pool and capping relative valuation precision.
- **Methods that can run:** own-history multiples, intrinsic DCF (FCFF), reverse-DCF, peer relative valuation (lower confidence — from screenshot and Ashok Leyland transcript), SOTP (collapses to consolidated single-segment read)
- **Active partial-data caps:**
  - No peer comps structured extract: overall usefulness max 70 for agent 03 (relative valuation)
  - Full broker-level consensus detail (EstimatesReport.xls) not readable: forward estimates are means only — confidence cap does not formally apply (means are available) but downstream agents should note the broker-count and revision-history gap
- **Critical missing items:**

## valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md

# Price & Capital Structure — TMCV (Tata Motors Limited, formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited)

**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores, except per-share items in ₹). Fiscal year ends March 31. No FX conversion is required — all figures are in INR throughout.

**Entity note:** TMCV (NSEI: TMCV) is the demerged commercial-vehicles entity of the old Tata Motors Group. It received the CV business effective October 1, 2025 (appointed date July 1, 2025) under a Composite Scheme of Arrangement approved by the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT). The company was incorporated June 23, 2024; FY26 (April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026) is its first full twelve-month fiscal year on a consolidated basis.

---

## 1. Current Price

| Field | Value | Source | As-of Date |
|---|---|---|---|
| Current price | ₹ 369.15 | S&P Capital IQ Financials export, Key Stats tab — "Current Capitalization" section, Share Price row | 2026-06-07 (file downloaded 2026-06-07; price is the last-close quote as shown on export date) |
| Currency | INR | S&P Capital IQ export | 2026-06-07 |
| Price basis | Last close / as-of download date | S&P Capital IQ Financials export, Key Stats tab | 2026-06-07 |

The Capital IQ export was downloaded on 2026-06-07 (file modification date confirmed). The "Current Capitalization" block on the Key Stats tab reflects the price at the time of export. The price is drawn from the data pool and carries the Capital IQ label as source (source tier 5 per the hierarchy). No IBKR screenshot with a live NSE quote is in the data pool; the Capital IQ price is the best available pool source.

---

## 2. Share Count

| Field | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Basic shares outstanding (as-of March 31, 2026) | 3,681,724,845 | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results (year ended Mar 31, 2026), filed May 13, 2026, Balance Sheet Supplemental: "Total Shares Out. on Balance Sheet Date" = 3,681.724845 mn; and confirmed by S&P Capital IQ Balance Sheet tab |
| Diluted weighted-average shares (FY26 full year) | 3,681,652,490 | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, May 13, 2026, Income Statement: Weighted Avg. Diluted Shares Out. = 3,681.65249 mn |
| Options / RSUs disclosed | ₹ 110 mn stock-based compensation expense in FY26; count not separately disclosed in available data | SEBI LODR results filing, Note to Income Statement (S&P Capital IQ Supplemental tab, "Stock Based Comp. Exp." = ₹ 110 mn INR) |
| Convertibles / potential shares | None disclosed in available data; no convertible debt outstanding per balance sheet | SEBI LODR Balance Sheet Mar 31, 2026: borrowings are standard term loans / commercial paper; no convertible instruments identified |
| **Fully diluted shares (TSM + if-converted)** | **~3,682,331,373** (Capital IQ basic count used as proxy; TSM dilution from stock-based compensation is immaterial — see note below) | S&P Capital IQ Financials export, Key Stats tab, Shares Out. = 3,682.331373 mn; SEBI LODR filing confirms basic count 3,681.724845 mn |
| Share count used for market cap | 3,682,331,373 | S&P Capital IQ Key Stats "Shares Out." — most recent as-of-download count, marginally higher than the balance sheet date count (3,681.724845 mn), consistent with shares issued up to the export date |
| Share count used for per-share fair value | 3,681,724,845 (basic shares at March 31, 2026) as the base; TSM dilution not calculable — see limitation note | SEBI LODR Balance Sheet Supplemental, Mar 31, 2026 |

**Share count note:** The basic and diluted weighted-average shares are nearly identical (gap of ~606,900 shares, or 0.02% of total), confirming that stock-based compensation dilution is immaterial at the current level of outstanding options/RSUs. The filing reports basic EPS = diluted EPS = ₹ 8.23 for FY26, confirming zero material dilution. The treasury-stock method cannot be precisely applied because the option strikes and vesting schedules are not disclosed in the available data pool — this is a minor limitation given the immaterial dilution. The Capital IQ export uses a "basic" dilution flag, and its "Shares Out." of 3,682.331373 mn is used for market cap as it represents the most current issued-and-outstanding count.

**Gap between basic and fully diluted:** Immaterial (~0.02%). No adjustment required. Per-share fair value calculations in downstream agents should use 3,681,724,845 shares (balance sheet date count) and note this as a minor limitation.

---

## 3. Market Capitalization

`Market cap = Shares outstanding × Current price`

`= 3,682,331,373 shares × ₹ 369.15 per share`

`= ₹ 1,359,332,626,342 (INR)`

`= ₹ 1,35,933 crores (₹ 1,35,933 cr)`

**Cross-check:** S&P Capital IQ Key Stats reports Market Capitalization = 1,359,332.626342 INR mn = ₹ 1,35,933 cr. Computed figure matches exactly, confirming price × shares arithmetic is consistent.

---

## 4. Enterprise Value Bridge

The EV bridge uses the Capital IQ convention (consistent with its reported EV), which includes lease liabilities within total debt and includes short-term investments within cash. Both conventions are presented; the Capital IQ convention is primary because it reconciles precisely to the Capital IQ-stated EV.

**Primary bridge (Capital IQ convention, consistent with reported EV of ₹ 1,28,498 cr):**

| Component | Amount (₹ cr) | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Market capitalization | 1,35,933 | S&P Capital IQ Key Stats, Current Capitalization, downloaded 2026-06-07; confirmed by shares × price arithmetic above |
| + Total debt (financial borrowings + lease liabilities) | 5,615 | S&P Capital IQ Balance Sheet (as at Mar 31, 2026): Total Debt = 56,150 INR mn = ₹ 5,615 cr. Cross-checked to SEBI LODR Balance Sheet: non-current borrowings ₹ 1,344 cr + current borrowings ₹ 3,473 cr + non-current lease liabilities ₹ 603 cr + current lease liabilities ₹ 195 cr = ₹ 4,817 + ₹ 798 = ₹ 5,615 cr — exact match |
| + Minority / non-controlling interest | 0 | S&P Capital IQ Key Stats: Total Minority Interest = "–" (nil); SEBI LODR Balance Sheet: no minority interest line (TMCV has no non-controlling interest in its consolidated accounts as at Mar 31, 2026) |
| + Preferred equity | 0 | S&P Capital IQ Key Stats: Pref. Equity = "–" (nil); SEBI LODR Balance Sheet: no preference share capital |
| − Cash & equivalents + short-term investments | (13,050) | S&P Capital IQ Key Stats: Cash & Short Term Investments = 130,500 INR mn = ₹ 13,050 cr. Cross-checked to SEBI LODR Balance Sheet: cash & cash equivalents ₹ 6,899 cr + bank balances other than above ₹ 877 cr + current financial investments ₹ 5,274 cr = ₹ 13,050 cr — exact match |
| − Equity-method investments | Not deducted (see note) | See adjustment note below |
| **= Enterprise value (EV)** | **1,28,498** | S&P Capital IQ Key Stats: TEV = 1,284,982.626342 INR mn = ₹ 1,28,498 cr. Bridge arithmetic: 1,35,933 + 5,615 − 13,050 = ₹ 1,28,498 cr — exact match |

**Adjustments NOT made and why:**

1. **Equity-method investments (₹ 1,462 cr):** The balance sheet shows "Investment in equity accounted investees" of ₹ 1,462 cr (mainly TMF Holdings subsidiary stakes and some JV interests). Capital IQ does not deduct these from EV in its standard bridge, and the company's primary segment (Tata Commercial Vehicles) is treated as the operating entity; the investees are ancillary. This item is flagged but not deducted from EV in the primary anchor bridge. Downstream agents running a sum-of-the-parts analysis may choose to deduct this if they treat these investments separately.

2. **Non-current financial investments (₹ 6,547 cr):** These are financial investments held at fair value, primarily by a subsidiary (the same subsidiary that recorded a ₹ 2,418 cr FVTPL loss in FY26 per filing Note 8). Capital IQ includes these within short-term investments in the cash netting. Since Capital IQ has already incorporated them in the ₹ 13,050 cr cash & ST investments figure — and this reconciles exactly to the stated EV — no further adjustment is made. If only operational cash is used (₹ 6,899 cr cash + ₹ 877 cr bank balances = ₹ 7,776 cr), EV rises to ₹ 1,33,772 cr.

3. **Operating lease liabilities (included):** Lease liabilities (₹ 798 cr total) are already included in the Capital IQ "Total Debt" figure. This is the standard adjusted-EV convention. Downstream agents should be aware that the EV includes right-of-use asset liabilities per this convention.

4. **Pension / post-employment obligations:** The company has gratuity and post-retirement obligations. The filing notes a ₹ 389 cr exceptional item for the statutory impact of new Labour Codes on gratuity and long-term compensated absences. A precise under-funded pension amount is not separately disclosed in the available data pool. This adjustment is not made; it is flagged as a minor potential addition to EV if separately valued.

5. **Iveco acquisition (pending):** The company announced on July 30, 2025 a voluntary tender offer for Iveco Group N.V. at an approximate consideration of ₹ 41,691 cr (€3.8 billion). As at March 31, 2026 this transaction has not closed (regulatory approvals pending; expected to close in Q2 FY27). It is not reflected in the balance sheet or the EV bridge. This is a material pending event that downstream agents must note — if the acquisition closes, EV will increase materially by the acquisition consideration plus any debt assumed.

---

## 5. Net Debt & Leverage Snapshot

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Total financial borrowings (excl. leases) | ₹ 4,817 cr | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Balance Sheet, Mar 31, 2026: non-current borrowings ₹ 1,344 cr + current borrowings ₹ 3,473 cr |
| Total lease liabilities | ₹ 798 cr | SEBI LODR Balance Sheet Mar 31, 2026: non-current leases ₹ 603 cr + current leases ₹ 195 cr |
| Total debt (financial borrowings + leases) | ₹ 5,615 cr | Sum of above; cross-checked to S&P Capital IQ Balance Sheet total debt 56,150 INR mn |
| Cash & cash equivalents (narrow) | ₹ 6,899 cr | SEBI LODR Balance Sheet Mar 31, 2026: Cash and cash equivalents |
| Cash + bank balances + current investments (broad) | ₹ 13,050 cr | SEBI LODR Balance Sheet: cash ₹ 6,899 cr + bank balances ₹ 877 cr + current financial investments ₹ 5,274 cr |
| **Net debt (basic: financial borrowings − cash only)** | **Net cash ₹ 2,082 cr** | ₹ 4,817 cr borrowings − ₹ 6,899 cr cash = net cash ₹ 2,082 cr; cross-module confirmation: earnings/01_historical-financials.md Note F |
| **Net debt (broad: total debt incl. leases − cash + ST investments)** | **Net cash ₹ 7,435 cr** | ₹ 5,615 cr total debt − ₹ 13,050 cr cash & ST investments = net cash ₹ 7,435 cr |
| **Net cash — company-defined** | **₹ 13,713 cr** | FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.219: company-defined net cash includes lease liabilities and all liquid investments including long-tenor Tata Capital instruments; cross-module confirmation: earnings/01_historical-financials.md Note G |
| Net debt / FY26 Underlying EBITDA | Negative (net cash position) | No ratio applicable; company is net cash positive under all three definitions |
| Net debt / FY26 Reported EBITDA (₹ 9,965 cr per CIQ) | Negative | S&P Capital IQ Financials: EBITDA FY26 = 99,650 INR mn = ₹ 9,965 cr; any net-debt/EBITDA ratio is negative (net cash) |
| Debt equity ratio (as per filing) | 0.38× | SEBI LODR Results filing, May 13, 2026, Note 2(a): Total Debt/Equity = 0.38× at March 31, 2026 vs 0.87× at March 31, 2025 |

**Net debt definition note (CLAUDE.md §15):** The module default is net debt = total debt − cash. The company defines "net cash" differently (includes all liquid investments and deducts lease liabilities). Three values are shown above; the basic definition (financial borrowings minus cash only, = net cash ₹ 2,082 cr) is used as the anchor, consistent with CLAUDE.md §15. The company-defined net cash of ₹ 13,713 cr will be noted as an alternative in downstream agents for reference.

| Metric | Per Share (₹) | Source |
|---|---:|---|

| Tangible book value per share | ₹ 25.28 | Tangible book value = ₹ 9,306 cr (S&P Capital IQ Balance Sheet Supplemental: Tangible Book Value = 93,060 INR mn) / 3,681.72 mn shares = ₹ 25.28. Cross-check: CIQ states Tangible Book Value/Share = ₹ 25.276 — matches. | Current price / book value | 10.67× | ₹ 369.15 / ₹ 34.58. Cross-check: CIQ P/BV = 10.673× — matches |

## 7. Anchor Summary (canonical numbers for downstream agents)

The numbers below are the single authoritative set for all downstream valuation agents. Every downstream agent must use these verbatim and note any divergence from their own extracted figures explicitly.

**Anchor Block (copy-forward):**

- **Price:** ₹ 369.15 (as of 2026-06-07, last close per S&P Capital IQ export; data pool source, not web-sourced, not indicative)
- **Currency:** INR (Indian Rupees). Reporting standard: Ind AS. No FX conversion required.
- **Shares (market cap):** 3,682,331,373 (3,682.33 million) — S&P Capital IQ Key Stats, downloaded 2026-06-07
- **Shares (per-share fair value):** 3,681,724,845 (3,681.72 million) — SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, March 31, 2026. Dilution from stock-based compensation is immaterial (basic EPS = diluted EPS per filing); TSM adjustment not material.
- **Market cap:** ₹ 1,35,933 crores (₹ 1,359,333 million)
- **Net debt (anchor — basic):** Net cash ₹ 2,082 crores (total financial borrowings ₹ 4,817 cr minus cash ₹ 6,899 cr). Company-defined net cash of ₹ 13,713 cr is the management metric; broad net cash including ST investments = ₹ 7,435 cr.
- **EV:** ₹ 1,28,498 crores (using Capital IQ convention: market cap + total debt incl. leases ₹ 5,615 cr − cash & ST investments ₹ 13,050 cr)
- **Key caveats:**
  1. The Iveco Group N.V. tender offer (≈ ₹ 41,691 cr consideration) is pending regulatory approval as at March 31, 2026 and is not reflected in this balance sheet. If it closes in Q2 FY27 as announced, EV will increase substantially.
  2. Non-current financial investments of ₹ 6,547 cr (held at fair value by a subsidiary, which recorded a ₹ 2,418 cr FVTPL loss in FY26) are netted into Capital IQ's cash figure. Downstream agents running a SOTP or adjusted-EV bridge may wish to separate these.
  3. Equity-method investments of ₹ 1,462 cr are not deducted from EV in the primary anchor bridge. SOTP agents should assess these separately.
  4. No dilutive instruments other than stock-based compensation are outstanding. The TSM dilution is immaterial.
  5. Price is drawn from the Capital IQ export (pool source, tier 5). It is the last close as at the export download date (2026-06-07). No real-time exchange quote was available in the pool.

---

*Citations format: [Source, Period, Section/Tab/Page]*

All figures sourced from: SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results (year ended March 31, 2026), filed May 13, 2026 — p.1 (Income Statement), p.2 (Balance Sheet), p.3 (Cash Flow), p.5 (Notes); S&P Capital IQ Financials export "Tata Motors Limited NSEI TMCV Financials.xls", downloaded 2026-06-07, tabs: Key Stats, Balance Sheet, Income Statement; cross-module: analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/01_historical-financials.md, Notes F and G.

## valuation/02_multiples-own-history.md

# Multiples — Own History — TMCV (Tata Motors Limited, Commercial Vehicles)

**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores, except per-share items in ₹). Fiscal year ends March 31. All anchor numbers carried from `01_price-and-capital-structure.md` verbatim.

**Critical entity note:** TMCV was listed on the NSE in November 2025 following the demerger of the Tata Motors commercial vehicles business. FY26 (April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026) is its first full twelve-month fiscal year as a standalone listed entity. The S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet carries only three quarterly data points: the quarter ending December 31, 2025 (NTM multiples only — no LTM), the quarter ending March 31, 2026 (first quarter with LTM multiples), and the current period through June 5, 2026. This means the standalone multiple history spans roughly six months.

---

## 1. Current Multiples

All numbers use the anchor from `01`: Price ₹ 369.15 (S&P Capital IQ export, downloaded 2026-06-07); Market cap ₹ 1,35,933 cr; EV ₹ 1,28,498 cr; shares 3,682.33 mn (market cap) / 3,681.72 mn (per-share fair value). Reporting currency: INR (Ind AS). Multiples are computed against CIQ Total Revenue (includes other income), CIQ-reported EBITDA, and CIQ-reported EBIT for EV-based ratios; per-share multiples use shares at March 31, 2026.

TMCV is an operating commercial-vehicle manufacturer. Per the Business-Type Method Map, the appropriate multiples are EV-based (EV/EBITDA, EV/EBIT, EV/Sales) plus P/E and P/Book. EV multiples are the primary anchor; P/E on reported earnings is distorted by exceptional items and a ₹ 2,418 cr non-cash FVTPL investment loss, so P/Normalised EPS is the more useful earnings multiple.

| Multiple | Basis | Metric Value (₹ cr) | Current Multiple | Source |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| EV / EBITDA | LTM (FY26, reported) | ₹ 9,965 cr | **12.68×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, Close column, Q ending Jun-05-2026; EV ₹ 1,28,498 cr / EBITDA ₹ 9,965 cr |
| EV / EBIT | LTM (FY26, reported) | ₹ 8,020 cr | **15.69×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, Close Jun-05-2026; cross-check: 128,498 / 8,020 = 16.02× — minor rounding vs CIQ due to filing vs CIQ reclassification |
| EV / Sales | LTM (FY26, total revenue) | ₹ 84,979 cr | **1.51×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, Close Jun-05-2026; TEV ÷ Total Revenue (CIQ definition) |
| P / E (reported) | LTM (FY26, Ind AS diluted) | EPS ₹ 8.23 | **44.85×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, P/LTM EPS Close Jun-05-2026; distorted by ₹ 1,428 cr exceptional losses and ₹ 2,418 cr FVTPL loss — not a primary valuation anchor |
| P / E (normalised) | LTM (FY26, S&P normalised) | EPS ₹ 15.91 | **25.56×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, P/LTM Normalised EPS Close Jun-05-2026; strips exceptionals and FVTPL; primary earnings multiple |
| P / Book | LTM (Mar 31, 2026 BV) | BV/share ₹ 34.58 | **10.67×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, P/BV Close Jun-05-2026; cross-check: 369.15 / 34.58 = 10.68× — matches |
| P / Tangible Book | LTM (Mar 31, 2026 TBV) | TBV/share ₹ 25.28 | **14.60×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, P/Tang BV Close Jun-05-2026; cross-check: 369.15 / 25.28 = 14.60× — matches |
| P / FCF (company-defined FCF yield) | LTM (FY26, co-defined FCF) | FCF ₹ 12,438 cr | P/FCF **10.93×** / FCF yield **9.2%** | FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.219: FCF ₹ 12,438 cr. P/FCF = 1,35,933 / 12,438 = 10.93×; FCF yield = 12,438 / 1,35,933 = 9.15% |
| Dividend yield | FY26 (declared ₹ 4/share) | Dividend ₹ 4.00 | **1.08%** | FQ4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript, May 13, 2026: final dividend ₹ 4/share recommended; 4.00 / 369.15 = 1.08% |
| EV / EBITDA | NTM (FY27E consensus) | EBITDA ₹ 10,279 cr (est.) | **12.50×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, TEV/NTM EBITDA Close Jun-05-2026; CIQ FY27E EBITDA ₹ 10,279 cr (Key Stats, ₹ 1,02,793 mn) |
| EV / EBIT | NTM (FY27E consensus) | EBIT ₹ 8,427 cr (est.) | **15.25×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, TEV/NTM EBIT Close Jun-05-2026 |
| EV / Sales | NTM (FY27E consensus) | Revenue ₹ 88,136 cr (est.) | **1.46×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, TEV/NTM Total Revenues Close Jun-05-2026; consensus revenue ₹ 8,81,357 mn per earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md |
| P / NTM EPS | NTM (FY27E, CIQ normalised) | EPS ₹ 18.12 (CIQ) | **20.37×** | S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet, P/NTM EPS Close Jun-05-2026; CIQ uses diluted EPS excl. extras ₹ 18.12 (Key Stats row 33). Using street consensus EPS ₹ 19.17 from earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md: 369.15 / 19.17 = 19.26× |

**EBITDA definition note:** CIQ reports EBITDA of ₹ 9,965 cr (₹ 99,650 mn). This is the reported (not underlying) EBITDA. The company-disclosed Underlying EBITDA is ₹ 10,314 cr (FY26 Integrated Annual Report, MD&A p.215), which excludes the ₹ 2,418 cr FVTPL investment loss, MTM hedges, and exceptional items. Reported EBITDA is used here to match CIQ multiples data and ensure internal consistency; downstream agents may wish to compute EV/Underlying EBITDA = 128,498 / 10,314 = **12.46×** as an additional data point. Similarly, reported EBIT (CIQ: ₹ 8,020 cr) is used; Underlying EBIT = ₹ 8,538 cr gives EV/Underlying EBIT = **15.05×**.

---

## 2. Historical Multiple Bands

**Partial-data note — own history limited to approximately 6 months:** TMCV was listed in November 2025. The S&P Capital IQ Multiples sheet (source: S&P Capital IQ Financials export, downloaded 2026-06-07) contains three quarterly data points for this entity as a standalone listed company. No prior standalone listed history exists. The Historical Capitalization tab in the same export shows "No Data Available." FY25 multiples in the Key Stats tab are calculated at today's current price against the re-presented FY25 (12-month) financials — these are "what would the multiple have been at today's price?" cross-sections, not observed market prices during FY25 (the entity was not listed in FY25). Multi-year standalone multiple bands are therefore not available in the traditional sense. The table below presents the full set of observable data with appropriate labelling.

| Multiple | Min (observed range) | Mean (of available data points) | Median (of available data points) | Max (observed range) | Current (Jun-05-2026) | Data Points Available | Note |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| EV / EBITDA (LTM) | 12.68× | ~14.9× | ~15.3× | 17.06× | **12.68×** | 2 close-period values; 1 FY25 re-presented | Current = observed range low; mean/median derived from 3 reference points |
| EV / EBIT (LTM) | 15.69× | ~19.5× | ~20.2× | 22.14× | **15.69×** | 2 close-period values; 1 FY25 re-presented | Current = observed range low |
| EV / Sales (LTM) | 1.51× | ~1.67× | ~1.63× | 1.92× | **1.51×** | 2 close-period values; 1 FY25 re-presented | Current = observed range low |
| P / E (normalised, LTM) | 25.56× | ~29.2× | ~27.5× | 34.0× | **25.56×** | 2 quarterly avg values (Mar-2026 and Jun-2026); FY25 not available on norm basis | Current = observed range low |

**Source for all rows:** S&P Capital IQ Financials export, Multiples sheet (downloaded 2026-06-07). The "average" row in the CIQ Multiples sheet represents the intra-quarter average of daily close multiples. The "High" and "Low" rows represent the intra-quarter intraday extremes. The "Close" row is the last value at each quarter-end.

**How the mean and median were calculated:** For EV/EBITDA: three reference points are (a) FY25 re-presented multiple at today's price = 15.26× (Key Stats tab, TEV/EBITDA using FY25 EBITDA of ₹ 82,520 mn = ₹ 8,252 cr: EV 1,28,498 / 8,252 = 15.57×; CIQ Key Stats states 15.26× — used directly), (b) Q Mar-31-2026 intra-quarter average = 15.54× (Multiples sheet, Average row, column 3), (c) Q Jun-05-2026 intra-period average = 13.84× (Multiples sheet, Average row, column 4). Mean = (15.26 + 15.54 + 13.84) / 3 = **14.88×**. Median = 15.26×. The "min" and "max" are taken from the lowest intraday low across all quarters (Jun-2026 Low = 12.68×) and the highest intraday high (Mar-2026 High = 17.06×). These intra-quarter ranges reflect daily price movements, not separate economic regimes.

**What this history means:** Because the entity has only one full fiscal year of listed history, the bands above are better read as a de-rating trajectory over six months, not as a stable mean-reversion anchor. The stock opened above ₹ 400 (implied by Q Dec-2025 NTM multiples) and has moved steadily to its current lows. Whether the stock's own six-month history constitutes a meaningful reversion target is a calibration judgment, not a data certainty.

---

## 3. Re-Rating / De-Rating Read

On all three primary multiples, the current price is at or near the low end of the observable range since listing. EV/EBITDA has fallen from approximately 15.3–15.5× (Q Mar-2026 intra-quarter average) to 12.68× today — a compression of roughly 17% relative to the six-month average. P/Normalised EPS has moved from an average of ~30.8× during Q Mar-2026 to 25.6× today, a compression of about 17%.

The most likely driver is not a deterioration in underlying earnings — FY26 delivered the highest-ever revenue and margins, with Q4 FY26 beating consensus on both revenue and EPS. The de-rating appears driven by three specific concerns that emerged after the FY26 results: (a) management's explicit refusal to guide to "mid-teens" EBITDA margins for FY27 (correcting to "teens only"), implying a margin plateau; (b) "significantly higher" commodity cost impact flagged for Q1 FY27, with only partial pass-through via a 2% April price increase; and (c) unresolved Iveco acquisition financing (≈ ₹ 41,691 cr consideration / €3.8 billion), which could add material leverage to a balance sheet that only recently turned net-cash positive. [Source: FQ4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript, May 13, 2026, CFO G.V. Ramanan and CEO Girish Wagh Q&A; earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md.]

The stock is not in a period of multiple expansion — it is in a period of multiple contraction driven by forward-year uncertainty layered on top of a strong trailing year. This is a distinct pattern from a simple "cyclical" de-rating; the core business is doing well, but the market is discounting event risk (Iveco) and margin uncertainty.

---

## 4. Implied Value from Reversion

**Important caveat:** With only six months of standalone listed history, mean-reversion analysis carries much lower reliability than for an entity with 3–5 years of trading history. The three reference points used to construct the mean/median are: one is the FY25 re-presented cross-section at today's price (not an actual trading period), and the other two are intra-quarter averages from recent months. Treating this mean as the "warranted" multiple assumes the stock should trade where it traded in early 2026 — which itself reflected a newly-listed entity's pricing in an uncertain environment, not a long-run equilibrium.

The bridge from implied EV to implied equity value uses the CIQ net cash convention: Cash & ST Investments ₹ 13,050 cr minus Total Debt ₹ 5,615 cr = net cash ₹ 7,435 cr (broad) = ₹ 74,350 mn. Shares used: 3,681.72 mn (balance sheet date, March 31, 2026, SEBI LODR filing).

Formula: Implied Equity Value per share = (Multiple × Metric + Net Cash in mn INR) / Shares in mn

| Multiple | Reversion Target | Metric (LTM) | Implied EV (₹ cr) | Bridge to Equity | Implied Price/Share (₹) | vs Current ₹ 369.15 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|
| EV / EBITDA — mean reversion (14.88×) | 14.88× | ₹ 9,965 cr | ₹ 1,48,256 cr | + ₹ 7,435 cr net cash − / shares 3,681.72 mn | **₹ 423** | +15% |
| EV / EBITDA — median reversion (15.26×) | 15.26× | ₹ 9,965 cr | ₹ 1,52,046 cr | same bridge | **₹ 433** | +17% |
| EV / EBIT — mean reversion (19.5×) | 19.50× | ₹ 8,020 cr | ₹ 1,56,390 cr | same bridge | **₹ 446** | +21% |
| EV / EBIT — median reversion (20.2×) | 20.16× | ₹ 8,020 cr | ₹ 1,61,683 cr | same bridge | **₹ 461** | +25% |
| P / Normalised EPS — mean reversion (29.2×) | 29.20× | EPS ₹ 15.91 | — (equity direct) | n/a | **₹ 465** | +26% |
| P / Normalised EPS — median reversion (27.5×) | 27.55× | EPS ₹ 15.91 | — (equity direct) | n/a | **₹ 438** | +19% |

**Implied value range:** The three multiples at mean/median reversion produce a range of approximately **₹ 423–₹ 465** per share — roughly 15–26% above the current price of ₹ 369.15. The midpoint of this range is approximately ₹ 444.

**Reversion assumption:** These implied values assume the warranted multiple is unchanged from the six-month average. Whether that is justified depends on whether the drivers of the recent de-rating (Iveco event risk, commodity cost absorption, margin plateau guidance) resolve or persist. If Iveco financing is confirmed as dilutive (equity raise or high-cost bridge converting to permanent debt), the multiple the market assigns to the remaining business will not revert to its six-month average — it will reset to a new, lower level reflecting a more leveraged entity. If commodity headwinds normalise and the "teens" EBITDA guidance proves conservative (as the business-model evidence of 11 consecutive double-digit quarters suggests is possible), a reversion to the six-month mean is more plausible. Neither outcome is certain.

---

## 5. Own-History Read

The stock is at the low end of every multiple it has traded at since listing, driven not by deteriorating trailing performance but by forward uncertainty. Reverting to the six-month mean on EV/EBITDA implies a price of roughly ₹ 423–433 per share, or about 15–17% above today's level.

The single biggest caveat is that six months of data is not a reliable reversion anchor. More importantly, the Iveco acquisition (₹ 41,691 cr consideration, expected to close Q2 FY27) was not in the balance sheet at March 31, 2026. If this deal closes with substantial debt, the company moves from net-cash positive to meaningfully leveraged, and the EV-based multiples that looked reasonable against a clean balance sheet will need to be recalibrated against a very different capital structure. The implied-value range above is only valid for a TMCV that remains net-cash positive post-Iveco — a condition that depends entirely on the financing mix, which management had not disclosed as of the Q4 FY26 results date.

The governance module flagged the TMF Holdings merger as a borderline RF-OWN-004 alignment conflict (Amber, not formally triggered). Tata Sons is not a government entity or an extractive parent in the classic sense, but the board-approved decision to absorb NBFC liabilities and a ₹ 2,418 cr FVTPL investment drag into the listed CV entity is a concrete example of intra-group restructuring that transfers costs to minority holders. This does not make reversion to the six-month mean impossible, but it does mean the market may persistently apply a discount to the "clean" multiple, and that discount may widen if the TMF merger proceeds on unfavourable terms.

---

*Citations:*
- *S&P Capital IQ Financials export, "Tata Motors Limited NSEI TMCV Financials.xls," downloaded 2026-06-07 — tabs: Multiples (quarterly H/L/Avg/Close), Key Stats (valuation multiples vs fiscal periods), Income Statement.*
- *SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, year ended March 31, 2026, filed May 13, 2026 — Balance Sheet, Income Statement.*
- *FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.215 (Underlying EBITDA/EBIT), p.219 (FCF, Net Cash).*
- *FQ4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript (S&P Capital IQ), May 13, 2026.*
- *Upstream: analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md (anchor numbers — verbatim); analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/01_historical-financials.md; analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md; analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/management-governance/04_ownership-and-insider-behavior.md (RF-OWN-004 borderline amber flag).*
