## valuation/03_relative-valuation-peers.md

# Relative Valuation — Peers — TMCV

**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores). Fiscal year ends March 31. TMCV is Tata Motors Limited (formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited), NSE: TMCV — the demerged commercial-vehicles entity of the old Tata Motors Group. FY26 (April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026) is its first full twelve-month fiscal year.

**Anchor figures (from 01_price-and-capital-structure.md):** Price ₹369.15 (S&P Capital IQ export, 2026-06-07), market cap ₹1,35,933 cr, EV ₹1,28,498 cr, net cash (broad) ₹7,435 cr, net cash (company-defined) ₹13,713 cr.

---

## 1. Peer Set

| Peer | Ticker | Why Comparable | Source of Inclusion |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ashok Leyland Limited | NSE: ASHOKLEY | The single most direct comparable: pure-play India commercial vehicle manufacturer, head-to-head with TMCV in MHCV trucks and buses, #2 in the market with ~30% domestic MHCV truck share and ~34% bus share in FY26. Same end-market (India freight + bus), same product class, same regulatory environment. | business-model/08_competitive-map.md (named competitor A); FQ4 FY26 TMCV earnings call transcript, May 13, 2026 |
| Eicher Motors Limited | NSE: EICHERMOT | Listed parent of VECV (50:50 JV with Volvo Group), which competes with TMCV in LMD trucks and medium buses and is the #4 CV player by volume. Partial comparable only — Eicher's consolidated P&L and EV are dominated by Royal Enfield motorcycles (~75% of value), which operate in a structurally different business (premium motorcycles, ~25% EBITDA margin). Included as a secondary reference; multiples cannot be applied directly to TMCV. | business-model/08_competitive-map.md (named competitor C); S&P Capital IQ Credit Health Panel screenshot, data as of 2026-06-07 (pool source) |

**Peer set source:** Derived from business-model/08_competitive-map.md (named competitors from TMCV filings and transcript). The set is not self-selected — both names appear explicitly in TMCV's own operating context.

**Private peers without public multiples:** VE Commercial Vehicles Limited (VECV) is itself unlisted; only its listed parent Eicher Motors is used and is flagged as a partial comparable. Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) operates CV through an unsegmented automotive division — CV-level margins and EV cannot be isolated; M&M is excluded from the multiples table as a result.

---

## 2. Peer Multiples & Operating Stats

All figures in INR crores (₹ cr) except margins (%) and per-share items. FY26 = year ended March 31, 2026 for all three companies (same fiscal year-end). EBITDA is Underlying EBITDA for TMCV (as defined and disclosed by the company: excludes FVTPL losses, MTM hedges, and exceptional items) and reported EBITDA for peers. EV/EBITDA and P/E are trailing (FY26 LTM).

| Company | P/E | EV/EBITDA | EV/EBIT | EV/Sales | FCF Yield | Rev Growth FY26 | EBITDA Margin | Net Margin | Net Debt / EBITDA | Data As-of |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| **TMCV** | 44.9× [A] | **12.5×** | 15.1× | 1.53× | 9.2% [B] | +9.8% [C] | 12.3% | 3.6% [A] | Net cash (−1.3×) [D] | FY26 (Mar 31, 2026); price 2026-06-07 |
| **Ashok Leyland** | 22.9× | 23.5× | 26.1× [E] | 2.39× | Neg. [F] | +16% | 10.2% | 6.6% | 8.2× [G] | FY26 (Mar 31, 2026); price 2026-06-05; Web-sourced, unverified |
| **Eicher Motors** | 35.2× | 26.7× [H] | n/m [H] | 8.30× [I] | n/a | +24% | 24.7% [I] | 23.6% [I] | Net cash [J] | FY26 (Mar 31, 2026); price 2026-06-05; Web-sourced, unverified |
| **Peer median (AL only — pure-play)** | 22.9× | 23.5× | 26.1× | 2.39× | Neg. | +16% | 10.2% | 6.6% | 8.2× | — |
| **Peer mean (AL + Eicher, indicative)** | 29.1× | 25.1× | — | 5.3× | — | +20% | 17.4% | 15.1% | — | — |

**Source notes — every figure:**

- **TMCV:** Price, market cap, EV, net cash, shares — S&P Capital IQ Financials export and SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results (year ended March 31, 2026, filed May 13, 2026); confirmed verbatim from analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md. Revenue, Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr), Underlying EBIT (₹8,538 cr), PAT (₹3,030 cr), FCF (₹12,438 cr) — SEBI LODR filing, May 13, 2026; FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.215, p.219. [Pool source, data tier 1–5]

- **Ashok Leyland:** Share price ~₹145.09 and market cap ~₹85,341 cr — Web: Business Standard, June 5, 2026, unverified. EV ₹1,34,783 cr — Web: valueinvesting.io, June 5, 2026, unverified. Total debt ₹63,936 cr, total cash + ST investments ₹17,079 cr (net debt ₹46,856 cr consolidated) — Web: stockanalysis.com, FY26 balance sheet, unverified. Revenue ₹56,362 cr (+16% from ₹48,535 cr), EBITDA ₹5,732 cr (13.0%), PAT ₹3,721 cr, EPS ₹5.91 — Web: Autocar Professional, Q4FY26 results article, June 2026, unverified. P/E 22.9× (computed: ₹85,341 cr / ₹3,721 cr). EV/EBITDA 23.5× (computed and confirmed via valueinvesting.io June 5, 2026). ROCE 13.8% — Web: screener.in, June 2026, unverified.

- **Eicher Motors:** Share price ~₹7,070 and market cap ~₹1,93,940 cr, EV ~₹1,94,195 cr — Web: screener.in, valueinvesting.io, June 5, 2026, unverified. EV/EBITDA 26.7× — Web: valueinvesting.io, June 5, 2026, unverified. Revenue ₹23,408 cr, EBITDA ₹5,785 cr (25% margin), PAT ₹5,515 cr — Web: Autocar Professional / equitybulls.com FY26 results, unverified. P/E 35.2× (computed). Total debt ₹514 cr — Web: screener.in, unverified.

**Footnotes:**

[A] TMCV's P/E of 44.9× uses reported PAT of ₹3,030 cr. This is distorted: reported PAT absorbs a ₹2,418 cr non-cash FVTPL loss on a subsidiary's equity investment portfolio plus ₹1,428 cr of exceptional items. The normalised/adjusted PAT per S&P Capital IQ consensus methodology is ₹5,858 cr (EPS ₹15.91 × 368.17 cr shares / 100), giving an adjusted P/E of ~23.2×. The reported P/E is flagged as unreliable for comparison; EV-based multiples (EV/EBITDA, EV/EBIT) are the primary valuation lens. Net margin of 3.6% is similarly distorted — operating margin is 12.35% [SEBI LODR filing, Note 2(n), FY26].

[B] FCF yield: company-defined FCF ₹12,438 cr / market cap ₹1,35,933 cr = 9.2%. Company-defined FCF = CFO minus capex (net of asset sale proceeds), plus interest received, plus dividends from equity-accounted investees. [FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.219]

[C] Revenue growth of +9.8% is stated as the FY26 YoY growth per the Chairman's letter (FY26 Annual Report, p.8) on the basis of management's annualised FY25 comparator of ₹76,359 cr. The audited FY25 comparative covers only nine months, so a precise statutory YoY cannot be computed.

[D] Net debt / EBITDA using broad net cash ₹7,435 cr (total debt incl. leases ₹5,615 cr minus cash + ST investments ₹13,050 cr, per 01_price-and-capital-structure.md): ratio = −0.72× (net cash). Using company-defined net cash ₹13,713 cr: ratio = −1.33×.

[E] Ashok Leyland EV/EBIT uses "Operating PBT" (₹5,163 cr) as the EBIT proxy [Web: Autocar Professional, FY26 results, unverified]. This is the pre-financing pre-tax operating profit line.

[F] Ashok Leyland FCF was negative in FY26 per screener.in (approximately −₹7,712 cr), reflecting heavy capex (defence vehicles, EV platforms) and large working capital movements despite the company's auto-entity net cash position of ₹5,899 cr. FCF yield is not computed as it is negative. [Web: screener.in, unverified]

[G] Ashok Leyland consolidated net debt ₹46,856 cr includes the captive finance subsidiary (Hinduja Leyland Finance). The auto entity alone reports "net cash ₹5,899 cr" per company press release [Web: Autocar Professional / company press release, FY26, unverified]. The EV / EBITDA multiple of 23.5× reflects the consolidated position (including the finance arm's debt). This is a critical comparability issue — see Section 3.

[H] Eicher Motors EV/EBITDA 26.7× uses consolidated EV (₹1,94,195 cr) and TTM EBITDA as reported by valueinvesting.io (₹7,272 cr, which approximates Royal Enfield standalone EBITDA ₹5,785 cr + ~50% of VECV EBITDA ₹1,282 cr = ~₹7,067 cr). This is primarily a Royal Enfield motorcycle multiple. EV/EBIT is not computed (no EBIT breakdown available for consolidated entity). [Web: valueinvesting.io, screener.in, June 2026, unverified]

[I] Eicher EBITDA margin (24.7%) and net margin (23.6%) are Royal Enfield standalone metrics, not CV-business metrics. VECV margins are materially lower (~9.5% EBITDA). EV/Sales of 8.30× on ₹23,408 cr standalone revenue is similarly not a CV metric. Eicher is included only as a secondary reference.

[J] Eicher Motors standalone: total debt ₹514 cr vs large cash position, resulting in net cash. Consolidated (including VECV) balance sheet also shows net cash for the parent. [Web: screener.in, unverified]

---

## 3. Premium / Discount to Peer Median

**Primary comparison: TMCV vs Ashok Leyland (only pure-play comparable)**

| Multiple | TMCV | Ashok Leyland (sole pure-play peer) | TMCV vs AL |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| EV/EBITDA (FY26 Underlying / Reported) | 12.5× | 23.5× | **−47% discount** |
| EV/EBIT (FY26 Underlying / Op. PBT) | 15.1× | 26.1× | **−42% discount** |
| EV/Sales (FY26) | 1.53× | 2.39× | **−36% discount** |
| P/E (reported, distorted — see [A]) | 44.9× | 22.9× | +96% premium |
| P/EBITDA (mktcap / EBITDA, debt-neutral) | 13.2× | 14.9× | **−11% discount** |

**Critical reconciliation — the EV/EBITDA gap is overwhelmingly structural, not operational:**

The 47% EV/EBITDA discount (12.5× vs 23.5×) looks dramatic but is almost entirely explained by a difference in capital structure, not by the market placing a lower value on TMCV's earnings power. TMCV has net cash of ₹7,435 cr (broad) — its EV is below its market cap. Ashok Leyland's consolidated EV includes the net debt of its captive finance subsidiary Hinduja Leyland Finance (₹46,856 cr consolidated net debt), which inflates its EV relative to market cap.

On a debt-neutral basis — comparing market cap (equity value) to EBITDA — TMCV trades at **13.2× P/EBITDA** versus Ashok Leyland's **14.9× P/EBITDA**. That gap is only **11%**. This is the correct lens for measuring relative market valuation. The 47% EV/EBITDA gap is largely a capital structure artefact, not a market view on relative business quality.

Secondary comparison: TMCV vs Eicher Motors (partial — note: Eicher is primarily a motorcycle company by value)

| Multiple | TMCV | Eicher Motors (indicative only) | TMCV vs Eicher |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| EV/EBITDA | 12.5× | 26.7× | −53% |
| P/E | 44.9× [A] | 35.2× | +27% [A] |

Eicher's 26.7× EV/EBITDA primarily reflects Royal Enfield's premium two-wheeler franchise, which has far higher margins (24.7% EBITDA vs 12.3% for TMCV) and recurring brand loyalty. This multiple cannot be applied to a commercial vehicle business.

---

## 4. Is the Gap Warranted?

**The core finding: the apparent 47% EV/EBITDA discount to Ashok Leyland is largely a capital structure artefact. The true equity-level discount is approximately 11%. Both parts of this are important.**

TMCV is the larger, higher-margin, FCF-generative, net-cash business. On the metrics that matter for an operating industrial company — EBITDA margin, FCF yield, balance sheet strength, and scale — TMCV outperforms Ashok Leyland across the board. TMCV's Underlying EBITDA margin of 12.3% is 210 basis points higher than Ashok Leyland's 10.2%. TMCV generates ₹12,438 cr of FCF (company-defined, 9.2% yield) versus Ashok Leyland's negative FCF in FY26. TMCV carries net cash; Ashok Leyland's consolidated entity carries ₹46,856 cr of net debt (8.2× EBITDA), much of it in the finance arm. On these factors, TMCV warrants a premium to Ashok Leyland on EV/EBITDA, and the current 12.5× multiple is below where it should trade even without quality adjustments.

The 11% discount on a P/EBITDA basis, however, requires explanation. Two specific risk factors justify the market pricing TMCV at a modest discount to Ashok Leyland on a debt-neutral basis:

1. **Iveco pending acquisition (₹41,691 cr, or €3.8 billion):** This transaction, announced July 30, 2025 and expected to close in Q2 FY27 [SEBI LODR Audited Results, May 13, 2026, Note 7], would convert TMCV from a net cash company (₹7,435 cr broad) to a company with approximately ₹34,000 cr of net debt — raising its leverage sharply and fundamentally changing the EV bridge. The market is correctly discounting this pending event. [business-model/11_capital-allocation-governance.md upstream evidence; Note 7, SEBI LODR filing]

2. **First year as a standalone entity:** TMCV has only one full year of consolidated audited history (FY26). Ashok Leyland has decades of standalone data. The market applies a credibility discount to a new entity's track record and management team's ability to operate independently. This is a rational, though time-limited, penalty.

These two factors justify a moderate discount. They do not justify a 47% EV/EBITDA discount, which is largely arithmetical. The correct framing is that Ashok Leyland's EV/EBITDA is inflated by its finance arm debt rather than TMCV's EV/EBITDA being depressed by fundamental undervaluation.

**Verdict: discount is partially warranted (Iveco and track record), but the apparent depth overstates the true gap. On a debt-neutral basis (P/EBITDA), TMCV trades at a modest 11% discount to AL — the gap is narrow and defensible given near-term catalysts.**

---

## 5. Implied Value from Peer Multiples

**Method:** Apply a quality-adjusted EV/EBITDA multiple to TMCV's FY26 Underlying EBITDA of ₹10,314 cr. Bridge to equity by adding broad net cash ₹7,435 cr (total debt incl. leases ₹5,615 cr minus cash and short-term investments ₹13,050 cr, per 01_price-and-capital-structure.md). Divide by 368.17 crore shares (SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, March 31, 2026, per 01_price-and-capital-structure.md).

**Quality adjustment rationale:**
- TMCV merits a modest premium to Ashok Leyland on EV/EBITDA for: better operating margins (+210 bps), net cash vs AL's leveraged consolidated position, and higher FCF yield.
- However, a discount is applied for the pending Iveco acquisition (balance-sheet and execution risk), the single-year track record as a standalone entity, and CLAUDE.md §24 Filter 5 (fast-changing technology environment; industry rate-of-change scored 35/100 per business-model/07_business-quality.md — caps conviction).
- Net quality adjustment: TMCV is modestly better than AL operationally but faces non-operational (Iveco) and structural (technology) risks. A range of 18×–23.5× is appropriate, with 23.5× (at-parity to AL) as the bull case and 18× (material discount for Iveco completion) as the bear.

**Implied value table (using TMCV Underlying EBITDA ₹10,314 cr and broad net cash ₹7,435 cr):**

| Multiple | Applied Peer Multiple | Quality Basis | Implied EV (₹ cr) | Implied Equity (₹ cr) | Implied Price/Share | vs Current ₹369.15 |
|---|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Bear | 18.0× EV/EBITDA | 23% discount to AL — Iveco completes, adding ~₹34,000 cr net debt; post-deal balance sheet weakens significantly; offset in implied equity above because bridge uses current net cash | 1,85,652 | 1,93,087 | **₹524** | +42% |
| Base | 20.0× EV/EBITDA | 15% discount to AL — Iveco risk partially priced in; TMCV better operating quality | 2,06,280 | 2,13,715 | **₹580** | +57% |
| Bull | 23.5× EV/EBITDA | At-parity with AL — Iveco does not close (or regulates away); TMCV quality premium unimpeded | 2,42,379 | 2,49,814 | **₹679** | +84% |

**Post-Iveco sensitivity note:** If the Iveco acquisition closes at ₹41,691 cr and TMCV's net cash of ₹7,435 cr (broad) is consumed, the residual net debt would be approximately ₹34,256 cr. At the same EV multiples, implied equity values would fall by ₹41,691 cr (the acquisition consideration), reducing implied prices to approximately: bear ₹411, base ₹467, bull ₹565. This is a material downside to the implied value range and illustrates why the Iveco risk dominates the relative valuation.

**Shares used:** 368.17 crore shares (SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, March 31, 2026). Dilution from stock-based compensation is immaterial (basic EPS = diluted EPS per FY26 filing).

---

## 6. Relative Read

TMCV appears to trade at a 47% discount to Ashok Leyland on EV/EBITDA, but this is almost entirely a capital-structure illusion: Ashok Leyland's consolidated EV is inflated by ₹46,856 cr of net debt in its captive finance subsidiary. On a debt-neutral basis — market cap versus EBITDA — TMCV's discount to Ashok Leyland is only 11%, with TMCV at 13.2× P/EBITDA vs 14.9× for AL. That modest 11% discount is defensible on the evidence: it reflects the pending Iveco acquisition (which will consume net cash and add leverage) and the fact that FY26 is the company's first full standalone year. The implied value range from peer multiples (18×–23.5× EV/EBITDA applied to ₹10,314 cr EBITDA) is **₹524–₹679 per share**, or 42%–84% above the current price of ₹369.15 — assuming Iveco does not close. If Iveco closes, that range compresses to approximately **₹411–₹565**, or 11%–53% above current price. The Iveco transaction is the single variable that most determines whether relative upside materialises or is absorbed by balance-sheet dilution.

---

*All web-sourced figures (Ashok Leyland and Eicher Motors multiples, prices, revenues, EBITDA, debt, EV) are labelled "Web-sourced, unverified" throughout this report. TMCV figures are from the pool (S&P Capital IQ export and SEBI LODR filings), which are source tier 1–5 per MODULE_RULES.md.*


---
## valuation/04_intrinsic-dcf.md

# Intrinsic DCF — TMCV (Tata Motors Limited, formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited)

**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores). Fiscal year ends March 31. FY26 (year ended March 31, 2026) is the first full audited fiscal year for this entity on a standalone consolidated basis. All per-share values use 3,681.72 million shares (SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, March 31, 2026) per `01_price-and-capital-structure` anchor.

**Business-type gate:** TMCV is an operating commercial vehicle manufacturer. FCFF DCF with an EV-to-equity bridge applies per the Business-Type Method Map in MODULE_RULES.md. No financial or REIT method is appropriate.

**Cyclicality gate:** `10_external-dependency.md` confirms the business is cyclical — management itself uses the word ("CV business is very cyclical in nature," FQ4 2026 transcript, May 13, 2026). The FCF base and forecast margins use a normalized mid-cycle assumption, not FY26 peak-year figures. The normalization is itemized in Section 1.

**Iveco caveat (material, non-modelled):** The company announced a voluntary tender offer for Iveco Group N.V. at approximately ₹41,691 cr (EUR 3.8 billion), targeted to close Q2 FY27. This transaction is not in the FY26 balance sheet, the financing structure is unresolved (bridge loan), and Iveco's margin profile is not available in the data pool. This DCF is built on a **standalone TMCV basis only**. If the Iveco deal closes, (a) net debt will increase by approximately ₹33,000–42,000 cr (depending on deal structure), which will substantially reduce equity value per share, and (b) Iveco EBITDA contribution would partially offset the interest burden. The net per-share impact cannot be modelled with Medium confidence or better from available data. This is the single most material unmodelled event. Per `04_guidance-consensus.md` and `03_margin-drivers.md`, Iveco financing cost alone could reduce normalized EPS by approximately ₹2.52 per share in a bear scenario.

---

## 1. FCF Base & Normalizations

**Base year: FY26 (year ended March 31, 2026)**. Currency: INR crores. Reporting standard: Ind AS.

The FCFF identity used throughout: **FCFF = NOPAT + D&A − Capex − ΔWC** where NOPAT = Underlying EBIT × (1 − tax rate). This definition is used because (a) a full cash flow statement is available and cross-checks the calculation, and (b) Underlying EBIT strips the non-cash FVTPL fair-value loss (₹2,418 cr) that distorts reported EBIT, consistent with the earnings quality module's guidance. The definition is held constant throughout the forecast.

Cross-check: CFO ₹14,981 cr − Capex ₹2,248 cr = ₹12,733 cr (direct FCF = CFO minus total capex method). The NOPAT + D&A − Capex − ΔWC computation below produces ₹12,502 cr, with the ₹231 cr gap explained by tax and interest cash flows in the actual CFO vs the NOPAT approximation. Both methods confirm the FCF order of magnitude.

| Item | Base-Year Value (FY26) | Normalization Applied | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Revenue from operations | ₹83,855 cr | No normalization — used as stated | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, year ended Mar 31, 2026, filed May 13, 2026, Line I |
| Underlying EBIT | ₹8,538 cr (10.2% margin) | Used as stated for base check; but see cyclicality normalization below — the **forecast** uses a normalized mid-cycle EBIT margin of 10.0%–11.0%, not the FY26 peak margin, per the cyclicality gate | FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A, p.215 |
| Depreciation and amortisation (D&A) | ₹1,945 cr (2.3% of revenue) | No normalization | SEBI LODR results filing, May 13, 2026, Income Statement |
| Gross capex (PPE + intangibles) | ₹2,248 cr (2.7% of revenue) | No normalization; within management-guided 2–4% range | SEBI LODR results filing, May 13, 2026, Cash Flow Statement |
| Working capital change (FY26) | +₹6,657 cr (cash inflow) | **Normalized to +₹400 cr/year favorable** — the FY26 figure was exceptional: ₹4,088 cr of the ₹6,657 cr inflow came from contract liabilities / customer advances (deferred revenue that unwinds as deliveries occur). The underlying negative-CCC model provides a recurring but smaller benefit. The normalized level is based on the FY25 (9M) figure of ₹1,838 cr and the structural CCC of approximately −54 days; at 5% revenue growth, the structural WC benefit per year is approximately ₹300–500 cr. ₹400 cr is used as the conservative mid-point. Label: **analyst assumption, not company-guided**. | SEBI LODR Cash Flow Statement, May 13, 2026; FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), Working Capital disclosures |
| Normalized tax rate | 28% | The reported FY26 effective rate was approximately 50%, distorted by the non-deductible FVTPL fair-value loss (₹2,418 cr on a subsidiary's equity investments). The normalized 28% is used throughout, consistent with `07_earnings-sensitivity.md` and the Ind AS statutory rate band | SEBI LODR results filing, May 13, 2026, Note 9 (tax); `07_earnings-sensitivity.md`, tax rate note |
| Cyclicality — EBIT margin normalization | FY26 Underlying EBIT margin = 10.2% (first time crossing double digits per management) | **FY26 is at or near a cyclical high.** The three-year observed range is 5.5% (FY23 carve-out) to 10.2% (FY26 actual). Mid-cycle is approximately 8%–9% on an unsmoothed basis, but the structural cost improvements (VAVE, scale, mix) are real and partly permanent. A mid-cycle normalized EBIT margin of **10.0%** is used for Year 1, converging to **11.0%** by Year 5 — below the CFO's stated margin expansion from 13.2% EBITDA (≈10.7% EBIT equivalent) as a peak scenario, but above the raw historical midpoint. Label: **analyst assumption, not company-guided, conservatively calibrated to the cyclicality gate.** | `10_external-dependency.md` cyclicality gate; `03_margin-drivers.md`, Section 8; FQ4 2026 Earnings Call, May 13, 2026 |

**Base-year FCFF (FY26, for reference):**
- NOPAT = ₹8,538 × (1 − 0.28) = ₹6,148 cr
- + D&A = ₹1,945 cr
- − Capex = ₹2,248 cr
- − ΔWC (normalized, positive = cash outflow, negative = inflow): −₹400 cr (source)
- **FCFF (normalized base) ≈ ₹6,245 cr**

Note: The base-year normalized FCFF of ₹6,245 cr is substantially below the reported FCF (CFO − capex = ₹12,733 cr) because the reported figure includes the ₹6,657 cr exceptional working capital inflow. The normalized base is the correct starting point for forecasting. The difference is flagged as a one-off that reduces the forward run-rate.

---

## 2. Forecast Assumptions

This DCF models TMCV **on a standalone basis**, excluding any Iveco consolidation. The Iveco transaction, if it closes, would require a complete re-run of the model. All Iveco-related items are noted in the Iveco caveat header and the sensitivity grid footnotes.

Explicit forecast period: 7 years (FY27–FY33). Terminal value applied at end of FY33. Revenue base = FY26 actual ₹83,855 cr.

| Assumption | FY27 (Yr1) | FY28 (Yr2) | FY29 (Yr3) | FY30 (Yr4) | FY31 (Yr5) | FY32 (Yr6) | FY33 (Yr7) | Terminal | Source / Basis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Revenue growth % | 5.0% | 7.0% | 6.0% | 6.0% | 5.0% | 5.0% | 5.0% | — | Yr1: street consensus FY27 = +5.1% (`04_guidance-consensus.md`); management "single-digit growth if not more" (FQ4 2026 transcript, May 13, 2026). Yr2: Indonesia 70,000-unit order delivering, recovery from H1 FY27 softness; **analyst assumption**. Yr3–5: mid-cycle India CV industry growth roughly in line with nominal GDP; **analyst assumption, peer-derived from India CV CAGR history**. Yr6–7: normalizing to long-run nominal growth; **analyst assumption**. |
| Revenue (₹ cr) | 88,048 | 94,211 | 99,864 | 105,856 | 111,149 | 116,706 | 122,541 | — | Computed |
| EBIT margin % | 10.0% | 10.2% | 10.5% | 10.8% | 11.0% | 11.0% | 11.0% | 11.0% | FY27: below FY26 10.2% — commodity headwinds confirmed "significantly higher" in Q1 FY27 (FQ4 2026 transcript); 2% price increase partially offsets. FY28–31: gradual structural recovery as VAVE and non-cyclical mix compound; consistent with management's stated "teens" EBITDA guidance (≈11%+ EBIT equivalent). Terminal: at 11.0%, EBIT is at the low end of management-implied ambition but appropriate for a cyclical mid-cycle anchor. Label: **analyst assumption; partially company-guided (management "teens" EBITDA ≈ 11–12% EBIT) for FY27, analyst assumption thereafter.** |
| Tax rate % | 28% | 28% | 28% | 28% | 28% | 28% | 28% | 28% | Normalized statutory rate. Reported FY26 rate (~50%) excluded as non-representative. `07_earnings-sensitivity.md`, tax rate note; **analyst assumption** consistent with Ind AS statutory rate band. |
| Capex (% of revenue) | 3.0% | 3.0% | 3.0% | 3.0% | 3.0% | 3.0% | 3.0% | 3.0% | Midpoint of management-guided FY27 range "2%–4% of revenue"; FY26 actual = 2.7%; 3% is conservative (company-guided range, midpoint). FQ4 2026 transcript, Q&A (G.V. Ramanan: "guidance would remain similar, 2% to 4% of revenue"). |
| D&A (% of prior-year revenue) | 2.3% | 2.3% | 2.3% | 2.3% | 2.3% | 2.3% | 2.3% | 2.3% | FY26 actual D&A / revenue = 2.3%; stable assumption as new capex partially replaces expiring D&A. `01_historical-financials.md`, Note D; **analyst assumption**. |
| ΔWC (₹ cr, negative = cash inflow) | −400 | −400 | −400 | −400 | −400 | −400 | −400 | — | Normalized; see Section 1 normalization. Structural negative-CCC model provides recurring WC benefit. FY26 ₹6,657 cr exceptional inflow excluded. **Analyst assumption, not company-guided.** |

**Revenue growth check:** The FY27–FY33 compound growth rate from the table above = [(122,541 / 83,855)^(1/7) − 1] = [1.4614^(1/7) − 1] = 5.6% CAGR. This is consistent with management guidance of "single-digit" near-term and the analyst consensus view of India CV growing broadly with nominal GDP. No exceptional forecast.

**ROIC / reinvestment consistency check (MODULE_RULES.md Economic Consistency Gate):**
- Terminal NOPAT (FY33): ₹122,541 × 11.0% × 0.72 = ₹9,705 cr
- Net reinvestment = Capex − D&A + ΔWC use = ₹3,676 − ₹2,684 + (₹400 inflow treated as −₹400) = ₹592 cr
- Reinvestment rate = ₹592 / ₹9,705 = 6.1%
- Auto ROCE (management-disclosed FY26) = 72.3%; sustainable long-run ROIC inference: management-disclosed ROCE is distorted by negative working capital and low capex base. A more conservative sustainable ROIC of 25–30% is used for the cross-check.
- Implied growth = 25% ROIC × 6.1% reinvestment = 1.5% — below the terminal g of 5.5%.
- **Gap finding:** The model implies that a significant portion of terminal growth comes from factors other than capital reinvestment — primarily revenue scaling without proportional capital (pricing, mix improvement, noncyclical growth). This is defensible given TMCV's negative-CCC model (growth is partially self-funding), but it means the terminal g is high relative to the reinvestment rate. At 30% ROIC: 30% × 6.1% = 1.8% implied growth. The 5.5% terminal g embeds approximately 3.7 percentage points of growth not explained by the reinvestment rate — this is flagged as an **optimistic assumption in the terminal value** that is partially justified by the negative-CCC model and the non-cyclical services layer, but partially represents model risk. Per MODULE_RULES.md: label this persistence as an inference.

---

## 3. Discount Rate (WACC)

All inputs for WACC are for an INR-denominated discount rate applied to INR cash flows. No FX conversion is needed.

| Component | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Risk-free rate (India 10-year G-Sec yield) | 6.95% | India 10-year government bond yield as of June 5, 2026 = 6.95%, reflecting a 6 bps decline on the session as foreign investor tax exemptions on G-Sec interest were announced. **Web-sourced from TradingEconomics.com / Investing.com, June 2026; indicative, unverified.** |
| Equity risk premium (ERP) — India | 7.08% | Damodaran Country Risk Premium dataset, January 2026; India rated Baa3 (Moody's); total ERP = mature market ERP + country risk premium = 7.08%. **Web-sourced from NYU Stern pages.stern.nyu.edu/~adamodar/, January 2026; indicative, unverified.** |
| Beta | 1.59 | TMCV 5-year or listed-history beta as sourced from Tickertape / market data, June 2026. TMCV listed in November 2025; the beta likely reflects a short listed history and may be noisy. The CV auto sector beta for India (comparable to Ashok Leyland) is broadly 1.3–1.7 per market data. 1.59 is consistent with the cyclical nature of the business (earnings volatility score 62/100). **Web-sourced from Tickertape.in, June 2026; indicative, unverified.** Confirm with Capital IQ beta when available. |
| Cost of equity | 18.21% | 6.95% + 1.59 × 7.08% = 6.95% + 11.26% = 18.21% |
| Pre-tax cost of debt | 8.0% | Analyst assumption — TMCV is effectively debt-free (net cash position); marginal borrowing rate for an India-domiciled BBB-equivalent credit is approximately 8–9% (consistent with current Indian 10-year G-Sec + ~100–150 bps credit spread for investment-grade industrial). **Analyst assumption, not company-disclosed.** Actual FY26 finance costs of ₹874 cr on average debt of approximately ₹7,000 cr (average of start/end) implies ~12.5% historical rate — which reflects higher rates when debt was larger; the current marginal rate on minimal debt is lower. 8% used as forward marginal rate. |
| Tax rate (for interest tax shield) | 28% | As per Section 1 normalization |
| After-tax cost of debt | 5.76% | 8.0% × (1 − 0.28) |
| Capital weights (market-value basis) | Equity 96.0%, Debt 4.0% | Market cap ₹1,35,933 cr; Total debt ₹5,615 cr; Total capital = ₹1,41,548 cr. `01_price-and-capital-structure`, Section 4 EV bridge. |
| **WACC** | **17.71%** | 0.96 × 18.21% + 0.04 × 5.76% = 17.48% + 0.23% = 17.71% |

**WACC sanity check (MODULE_RULES.md §Economic Consistency Gates):**
- Risk-free rate dated and labeled: ✓
- ERP dated and labeled: ✓
- After-tax cost of debt (5.76%) is positive and above 0%: ✓
- Terminal g (5.5%) vs long-run India nominal GDP growth proxy (~7–8% nominal): 5.5% is below the nominal GDP growth rate — appropriate for a mature cyclical business at mid-cycle. ✓
- WACC of 17.71% for an Indian cyclical auto OEM is at the high end but within sanity range given India ERP, the high beta, and the cyclicality of earnings. A range of 16%–20% for Indian cyclical industrials is plausible.

---

## 4. Free Cash Flow Forecast & Discounting

WACC = 17.71%. Year-end discounting convention.

FCFF formula per year: NOPAT + D&A − Capex + WC benefit (₹400 cr, treated as a positive addition to FCFF).
- NOPAT = EBIT × (1 − 0.28)
- D&A = 2.3% × prior-year revenue
- Capex = 3.0% × current-year revenue
- WC benefit = ₹400 cr (constant, normalized)

| Year | Revenue (₹ cr) | EBIT (₹ cr) | NOPAT (₹ cr) | D&A (₹ cr) | Capex (₹ cr) | WC Benefit (₹ cr) | FCFF (₹ cr) | Discount Factor | PV of FCFF (₹ cr) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| FY27 (Yr1) | 88,048 | 8,805 | 6,340 | 1,929 | 2,641 | 400 | 6,028 | 0.8495 | 5,121 |
| FY28 (Yr2) | 94,211 | 9,609 | 6,919 | 2,025 | 2,826 | 400 | 6,518 | 0.7217 | 4,703 |
| FY29 (Yr3) | 99,864 | 10,486 | 7,550 | 2,167 | 2,996 | 400 | 7,121 | 0.6131 | 4,367 |
| FY30 (Yr4) | 105,856 | 11,432 | 8,231 | 2,297 | 3,176 | 400 | 7,752 | 0.5209 | 4,039 |
| FY31 (Yr5) | 111,149 | 12,226 | 8,803 | 2,435 | 3,334 | 400 | 8,304 | 0.4425 | 3,675 |
| FY32 (Yr6) | 116,706 | 12,838 | 9,243 | 2,556 | 3,501 | 400 | 8,698 | 0.3759 | 3,269 |
| FY33 (Yr7) | 122,541 | 13,479 | 9,705 | 2,684 | 3,676 | 400 | 9,113 | 0.3193 | 2,909 |

*Discount factor formula: 1 / (1 + WACC)^n where n = 1 through 7 and WACC = 17.71%.*

**Sum of PV of explicit FCFs: ₹28,083 crores**

---

## 5. Terminal Value

- **Method:** Gordon Growth Model (perpetuity growth)
- **Terminal FCF base:** FY33 FCFF = ₹9,113 cr
- **Terminal growth rate (g):** 5.5% — analyst assumption; calibrated to approximately 70–75% of India's long-run nominal GDP growth proxy (~7–8% nominal), appropriate for a mature cyclical OEM that grows broadly with the freight/industrial cycle but not faster than the overall economy. This is below India's nominal GDP growth rate ✓ (passes MODULE_RULES.md gate).
- **Terminal FCF = ₹9,113 × (1 + 0.055) = ₹9,614 cr**
- **Terminal value (undiscounted) = ₹9,614 / (0.1771 − 0.055) = ₹9,614 / 0.1221 = ₹78,739 cr**
- **PV of terminal value = ₹78,739 × 0.3193 = ₹25,143 cr**
- **Terminal value as % of total DCF EV: ₹25,143 / (₹28,083 + ₹25,143) = 47.2%**

Terminal value is 47.2% of total EV — **not terminal-dominated** (well below the 75% flag threshold). The explicit forecast period carries 52.8% of total value. This reflects the relatively high WACC, which discounts terminal cash flows heavily. Confidence in the terminal value is therefore moderate.

**Exit-multiple cross-check (required by MODULE_RULES.md §Terminal Dominance escalation — not triggered here as TV < 75%, but included for completeness):** FY33 Underlying EBITDA ≈ ₹122,541 × (11.0% EBIT + 2.3% D&A) = ₹122,541 × 13.3% = ₹16,298 cr. A 4.8× EV/EBITDA terminal multiple (consistent with the Gordon growth terminal value: EV = ₹78,739 / ₹16,298 = 4.83×) is reasonable for an Indian CV OEM at mid-cycle; TMCV currently trades at approximately 12.9× LTM EBITDA (EV ₹1,28,498 cr / EBITDA ₹9,965 cr), so the terminal multiple implies significant mean-reversion from current trading levels — which is consistent with a cyclical manufacturer. Cross-check confirms the Gordon growth result is internally consistent.

---

## 6. DCF Output

EV-to-equity bridge uses the `01_price-and-capital-structure` anchor: net cash (broad definition) = ₹7,435 cr (total debt ₹5,615 cr minus cash & ST investments ₹13,050 cr). Since the company is net cash, the net debt term is negative, adding back to equity value. Minority interest = ₹0. Preferred = ₹0.

| Step | Value (₹ cr) |
|---|---:|
| PV of explicit FCFs (FY27–FY33) | 28,083 |
| + PV of terminal value | 25,143 |
| **= DCF Enterprise value** | **53,226** |
| − Net debt (add: net cash ₹7,435 cr, broad definition) | +7,435 |
| − Minority interest | 0 |
| − Preferred equity | 0 |
| **= DCF Equity value** | **60,661** |
| ÷ Diluted shares (3,681.72 mn = 368.17 crore) | 368.17 |
| **= Intrinsic value per share (base case)** | **₹164.7** |
| vs current price (₹ 369.15, Capital IQ Key Stats, June 7, 2026) | −55.4% below current price |

**Anchor confirmation:** Net debt figure (₹5,615 cr total debt − ₹13,050 cr cash & ST investments = net cash ₹7,435 cr, broad) sourced from `01_price-and-capital-structure`, Section 4 EV bridge. Share count (3,681.72 mn) sourced from `01_price-and-capital-structure`, Section 2. No divergence.

**Important note on alternative net cash definitions:** If the narrower net cash (basic: ₹2,082 cr) is used instead of the broad definition, equity value = ₹53,226 + ₹2,082 = ₹55,308 cr → per share = ₹150.2. This tightens the per-share range to ₹150–₹165 depending on the cash definition used. The base case uses the broad definition (consistent with Capital IQ convention and the EV bridge in `01`).

**Pre-Iveco context:** These per-share values reflect TMCV standalone. If the Iveco tender offer closes at approximately ₹33,250–41,691 cr consideration and is funded by debt (bridge loan), the net cash position of ₹7,435 cr would swing to net debt of approximately ₹25,000–34,000 cr. At the midpoint of ₹29,000 cr net debt, equity value falls from ₹60,661 cr to ₹53,226 − ₹29,000 = ₹24,226 cr → approximately **₹66 per share** before any Iveco EBITDA contribution is included. This illustrates the transformative — and potentially highly dilutive — effect of the Iveco acquisition on intrinsic per-share value if financed entirely with debt. The equity value per share is extremely sensitive to the Iveco financing structure.

---

## 7. Sensitivity Grid (per-share intrinsic value, ₹)

WACC across columns (base case = 17.71%); terminal growth rate down rows (base case = 5.5%).
Net cash used: ₹7,435 cr (broad definition). Share count: 368.17 crore.
All values represent the standalone TMCV intrinsic value per share, pre-Iveco.

| Terminal g | WACC = 16.21% (−1.5 pp) | WACC = 17.71% (base) | WACC = 19.21% (+1.5 pp) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **g = 6.0% (+0.5 pp)** | **₹189.5** | **₹167.7** | **₹150.8** |
| **g = 5.5% (base)** | **₹185.3** | **₹164.7** | **₹148.7** |
| **g = 5.0% (−0.5 pp)** | **₹181.5** | **₹162.1** | **₹146.8** |

**Per-share intrinsic value range: ₹147 to ₹190** across the full grid.

**Current price (₹369.15) is 95%–151% above the entire sensitivity range.**

*Grid construction note: PV of explicit FCFs was recomputed at each WACC level; terminal value was recomputed at each (WACC, g) combination. Year-end discounting. No interpolation.*

---

## 8. Intrinsic Read

The standalone normalized DCF produces an intrinsic value range of **₹147 to ₹190 per share** — approximately **55% to 60% below the current price of ₹369.15**. The current price embeds either (a) significantly more optimistic margin and growth assumptions than the normalized mid-cycle forecast, (b) a substantially lower required return than the 17.71% WACC used here, or (c) a substantial premium for the Iveco consolidation synergies that the market is pricing but cannot yet be quantified from available data. The single assumption this DCF is most sensitive to is the **WACC** (specifically the cost of equity driven by India ERP and beta): a 1.5 percentage point reduction in WACC from 17.71% to 16.21% raises the intrinsic value by approximately ₹17–21 per share — meaningful in absolute terms but still leaving a large gap to the current price. The second most powerful lever is the Iveco deal structure: if the acquisition is ultimately financed with equity (reducing the debt burden) and Iveco's margins prove resilient, the per-share intrinsic value could recover materially from the ₹66-per-share worst case outlined above, but that requires data that is not yet available. On the standalone normalized numbers, the current price of ₹369.15 cannot be supported by a mid-cycle DCF at any WACC within a reasonable range — the gap is too large for assumption tweaks to close.

---

## Partial-Data and Confidence Notes

- **FCF base:** Full cash flow statement available; no proxy needed. FCF quality is high (CFO/EBITDA = 145% in FY26). Confidence: not capped on data-availability grounds.
- **Forward estimates:** FY27 consensus available (revenue ₹881,357 mm, EPS ₹19.17 normalized). FY28 and FY29 consensus visible from Key Stats tab. The estimates workbook (TataMotorsLimitedNSEITMCVEstimatesReport.xls) failed extraction — broker-level detail and revision history not available. This limits estimate quality slightly but does not prevent the DCF.
- **Cyclicality cap:** The normalized mid-cycle approach produces lower FCF than reported FY26. This is appropriate and intentional per the cyclicality gate. The FY26 FCF of ₹12,733 cr (direct) vs normalized FCF of ₹6,245 cr illustrates the magnitude of the normalization — driven almost entirely by the exceptional ₹6,657 cr working capital inflow in FY26.
- **WACC inputs:** Risk-free rate, ERP, and beta are all web-sourced and labeled as unverified. The beta (1.59) is based on a short listed history (November 2025 listing); it should be treated with caution and confirmed from Capital IQ once more data accumulates. A sensitivity to beta is embedded in the ±1.5 pp WACC grid.
- **Iveco overhang:** The most material unmodelled item. This DCF is explicitly standalone. Any DCF that attempts to model post-Iveco TMCV would require Iveco's margin profile, deal financing structure, and integration costs — none of which are available in the data pool with Medium confidence or better.
- **Intrinsic confidence: Low-to-Medium.** The limited audited history of this entity (only one full fiscal year, FY26), the Iveco binary event, and the cyclicality normalization uncertainty all constrain confidence. The intrinsic value range (₹147–₹190) should be treated as indicative, not precise.

---

*All figures in INR crores unless stated otherwise. Reporting standard: Ind AS. No FX conversion applied (all figures in INR). Fiscal year ends March 31. Entity: TMCV (Tata Motors Limited, formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited), NSEI: TMCV.*

*Primary sources: SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results (year ended March 31, 2026), filed May 13, 2026; FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), filed June 6, 2026; FQ4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript (S&P Capital IQ, May 13, 2026); S&P Capital IQ Financials export (Key Stats tab, downloaded June 7, 2026). Cross-module: `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/01_historical-financials.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/04_guidance-consensus.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/03_margin-drivers.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/07_earnings-sensitivity.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/earnings/06_earnings-quality.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/business-model/10_external-dependency.md`; `analyses/TMCV_2026-06-07/business-model/09_moat.md`. Web-sourced (labeled): India 10Y G-Sec yield (TradingEconomics.com, June 5, 2026); India ERP (Damodaran, NYU Stern, January 2026); TMCV beta (Tickertape.in, June 2026).*


---
## valuation/05_reverse-dcf.md

# Reverse DCF — What's Priced In — TMCV

**Entity:** Tata Motors Limited (formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited), NSEI: TMCV. Demerged CV business; FY26 (year ended March 31, 2026) is the first full consolidated fiscal year. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores).

---

## 1. Inputs

| Input | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Current price | ₹369.15 | S&P Capital IQ Financials export, Key Stats tab, downloaded 2026-06-07; last-close quote as of export date — from data pool (tier 5), not web-sourced |
| Enterprise value (EV) | ₹1,28,498 cr | Valuation/01_price-and-capital-structure.md — Anchor Block; Capital IQ convention: market cap ₹1,35,933 cr + total debt incl. leases ₹5,615 cr − cash & ST investments ₹13,050 cr |
| FCF base (FY26) | ₹12,438 cr | Company-defined FCF: CFO less capex, plus interest received, plus investee dividends, less investments in core-auto equity-accounted investees. FY26 Integrated Annual Report (Ind AS), MD&A p.219 and Value Creation Architecture p.15 |
| FCF yield on EV (implied by base year) | 9.7% | ₹12,438 / ₹1,28,498 — this is the starting observation that anchors the reverse-solve |
| Shares (per-share reference) | 3,681.72 mn | SEBI LODR Consolidated Audited Results, Balance Sheet, March 31, 2026 |
| Discount rate (WACC) used | 13.0% | See component table below |
| Forecast horizon | 10 years (FY27–FY36), then terminal value in perpetuity | Standard operating-company horizon; TMCV is a capital-goods manufacturer, not a short-cycle business |
| Terminal growth rate (g) | 4.5% | India long-run nominal GDP proxy: RBI real GDP target ~6.5–7% + inflation ~4–4.5% ≈ 10.5–11% nominal; using 4.5% as conservative perpetuity growth given the FCF cycle and the risk that EV powertrain transition compresses future CV demand. Inference, not from filings. |

**WACC components:**

| Component | Value | Source / Basis |
|---|---:|---|
| Risk-free rate (India 10Y G-Sec yield) | 6.95% | India 10-year government bond yield as of June 5, 2026 — web-sourced, unverified (tradingeconomics.com). Labeled per MODULE_RULES. |
| Equity risk premium (India total ERP) | 7.08% | Damodaran 2026 country risk premium dataset — India total ERP = 7.08% (Baa3-rated; CRP 2.85% added to mature-market premium). Web-sourced, unverified (pages.stern.nyu.edu, Jan 2026 update). |
| Beta | 0.90 | Inference, not from filings. India CV manufacturing is a cyclical domestic industrial; comparable India auto/CV sector betas run 0.85–1.0. TMCV has no multi-year listed trading history (listed November 2025). Applied at the low end given >95% India domestic revenue and the company's demonstrated counter-cyclical cash generation. |
| Cost of equity | 13.32% | 6.95% + 0.90 × 7.08% = 6.95% + 6.37% = 13.32% |
| Pre-tax cost of debt | ~7.5% | Finance costs FY26 = ₹874 cr on average gross debt ~₹7,000 cr (FY25 closing ₹9,156 cr, FY26 closing ₹4,817 cr; midpoint ₹6,987 cr). Gross yield ≈ 12.5% is distorted by debt repayment timing; using 7.5% as a credible INR borrowing rate for an investment-grade-equivalent company. Inference, not from filings. |
| After-tax cost of debt | 5.4% | 7.5% × (1 − 0.28 normalised tax rate) |
| Debt weight (debt/EV) | 4.4% | ₹5,615 cr total debt / ₹1,28,498 cr EV |
| Equity weight (equity/EV) | 95.6% | ₹1,22,883 cr equity / ₹1,28,498 cr EV |
| **WACC** | **13.0%** | 0.956 × 13.32% + 0.044 × 5.4% = 12.73% + 0.24% = **12.97%, rounded to 13.0%** |

**Model:** Operating FCFF model. TMCV is an operating company (Business-Type Method Map: Operating). EV is valued on FCFF; equity is bridged as EV minus net debt.

**WACC sanity check:** Risk-free rate (6.95%) is web-sourced and dated. After-tax cost of debt (5.4%) is positive and plausible. Terminal growth (4.5%) is below WACC (13.0%), which is required for a finite terminal value — condition satisfied. TMCV is net-cash positive, so debt weight is low and WACC is almost entirely driven by cost of equity.

---

## 2. Implied Expectations

The solve holds the discount rate (13.0%), forecast horizon (10 years), and terminal growth rate (4.5%) fixed. It solves for the single FCF CAGR (applied uniformly across the explicit 10-year period) that equates the PV of all cash flows to today's EV of ₹1,28,498 cr.

**Solve formula:**

```
EV = FCF_base × Σ(t=1..10)[(1+g)^t / (1.13)^t] + [FCF_base × (1+g)^10 × (1+g_TV) / (WACC − g_TV)] / (1.13)^10

where g_TV = 4.5%, (1.13)^10 = 3.3946
```

**Trial results (₹ crores):**

| FCF CAGR (g) | PV of Explicit FCFs | PV of Terminal Value | Total PV = modelled EV |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 0.0% | 67,491 | 45,019 | 1,12,510 |
| 1.5% | 72,147 | 52,270 | 1,24,417 |
| 1.7% (implied) | ~73,600 | ~54,700 | ~1,28,300 |
| 2.0% | 75,475 | 60,393 | 1,35,868 |

The solve converges at approximately **g ≈ 1.7%** (₹1,28,300 cr ≈ ₹1,28,498 cr, difference <0.2%).

| What the Price Implies | Solved Value |
|---|---:|
| Implied FCF CAGR over 10-year explicit horizon | ~1.7% per annum |
| Implied % of EV from terminal value | ~43% (₹54,700 cr / ₹1,28,498 cr) |
| Implied FCF CAGR vs India nominal GDP growth (~10–11%) | Approximately 8–9 percentage points below nominal GDP |
| Implied FCF CAGR in real terms (deflating by ~5% inflation) | Approximately −3% real per year — a slow real FCF contraction |
| Implied years of above-GDP growth before fade (alt framing) | Zero — the price implies FCF growth below GDP from year one |

**What was held fixed:** WACC (13.0%), terminal growth rate (4.5%), forecast horizon (10 years), FCF base (₹12,438 cr FY26).

**What was solved for:** The uniform FCF CAGR across FY27–FY36 that equates modelled EV to observed EV of ₹1,28,498 cr.

**Terminal value note:** At the implied g = 1.7%, terminal value is approximately 43% of modelled EV — well below the 75% dominance threshold. The reverse-DCF result is not terminal-dominated and is relatively robust.

**Secondary implied check — margin:** The solve can also be framed as a margin question. If revenue grows at 5% per annum (consensus ≈ 5.1% FY27 and consistent with single-digit guidance), then for FCF to grow at only 1.7%, the FCF margin must erode from its FY26 level of ~14.8% (₹12,438 cr / ₹83,855 cr). Specifically, at 5% revenue CAGR and 1.7% FCF CAGR, the FCF margin must compress from 14.8% to approximately 12.0% over ten years — a 280-basis-point decline. This is consistent with the market pricing in some margin headwind (commodity costs, Iveco financing drag, powertrain transition capex) rather than continued expansion.

---

## 3. Implied vs Achievable

| Implied Requirement | Company History | Earnings-Module Evidence | Achievable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| FCF CAGR of ~1.7% over 10 years — effectively flat real FCF | Historical FCF data exists for only one full year (FY26: ₹12,438 cr; FY25 nine months: ₹5,880 cr). Revenue grew from ~₹66,000–68,000 cr in FY23 to ₹83,855 cr in FY26 — a three-year revenue CAGR of ~7–8% on the carve-out basis. Underlying EBITDA margin expanded from ~7.8% to 12.3% (550 bps) over the same period. FCF clearly grew faster than 1.7% over this window. | Street consensus embeds 5.1% revenue growth for FY27 and +20.5% normalised EPS growth. Earnings sensitivity shows CV volumes can swing ±₹6.57–7.28 EPS in either direction, but the normalised base (₹15.91 EPS, ₹12,438 cr FCF) is high and growing. Management guided "teens" EBITDA margin for FY27 — held, not declining. | Yes — the implied requirement is very conservative. A business that generated ₹12,438 cr FCF in FY26, is net-cash positive (₹13,713 cr company-defined), and is growing revenue at 5–10% per year should produce FCF CAGR well above 1.7% even accounting for the Iveco financing drag and commodity headwinds. |
| FCF margin compression from 14.8% to ~12.0% over 10 years | No multi-year FCF margin history. Underlying EBITDA margin has expanded structurally — 550 bps over three years. The first-ever double-digit EBIT margin was achieved in FY26 (10.2%). | Commodity headwind is confirmed for Q1 FY27 (100 bps impact in Q4 already, "significantly higher" in Q1 FY27). Iveco deal adds ~₹1,286 cr net interest if bridge at 6.5%. But capex guidance is held at 2–4% of revenue — no step-up. Non-cyclical revenue growing at 2.7× cyclical CAGR buffers through-cycle FCF. | Stretch on the downside — a 280-bps FCF margin compression over a decade would require persistent commodity inflation, full Iveco financing drag, AND no structural margin improvement. The base case is that EBITDA margins stay in the teens and FCF margins recover from any short-term commodity compression. |
| Zero years of above-GDP FCF growth implied | Revenue grew at ~7–8% CAGR over FY23–FY26 (above India real GDP of 6.5–7%). FCF growth was substantially faster during this margin expansion period. | Indonesia 70,000-unit export order (FY27 delivery), non-cyclical revenue growing 18.2% YoY in FY26, Fleet Edge surpassing 1 million connected vehicles — all growth vectors are active. | Clear upside to implied — the business has active growth drivers that the 1.7% implied CAGR ignores entirely. Even in a cyclical trough, TMCV's FCF should not permanently shrink below FY26 levels given its net-cash balance sheet and negative working-capital structure. |

**Assessment:** The market's implied expectations are conservative — arguably pricing in a mild, permanent deterioration in TMCV's FCF power. Three factors drive this: (1) the Iveco acquisition overhang (₹41,691 cr consideration pending approval, which if funded by debt will add significant financing cost and uncertainty); (2) the first-year nature of this listed entity (TMCV only listed in November 2025, so the market likely assigns a new-issuance uncertainty discount); and (3) India CV cycle concerns — the industry has historically seen −15% to +20% volume swings, and the market may be placing probability on a cycle turn. Against these concerns, the FY26 FCF of ₹12,438 cr is the highest in the entity's existence, and the balance sheet is net-cash by any definition. The implied 1.7% FCF CAGR is below what the current operating trajectory — 550 bps of margin expansion, negative working capital, accelerating non-cyclical revenues — would suggest is achievable absent the Iveco deal.

---

## 4. Robustness

Terminal growth held at 4.5% throughout. Horizon held at 10 years.

| Discount Rate | Implied FCF CAGR to Justify Price (EV = ₹1,28,498 cr) |
|---|---:|
| WACC 12.0% (−1%) | ~0.5% |
| WACC 13.0% (central) | ~1.7% |
| WACC 14.0% (+1%) | ~3.5% |

**Reading:** Even at the most demanding discount rate tested (14%), the market is pricing in FCF CAGR of only ~3.5% — still materially below nominal GDP growth and well below recent revenue and EBITDA growth rates. The conclusion is stable across the WACC range: the price embeds conservative-to-subdued FCF growth in every scenario. An investor who believes TMCV can grow FCF at 8–10% per annum (consistent with the recent revenue CAGR and management's teens EBITDA guidance) is being offered a substantial implied-expectations cushion, but must weigh it against the Iveco acquisition risk and the newness of the entity.

---

## 5. What's-Priced-In Read

At ₹369.15, the market is pricing in a 10-year FCF CAGR of approximately 1.7% — below India's nominal GDP growth of 10–11% and, in real terms, a mild contraction. That is conservative given TMCV's FY26 FCF of ₹12,438 cr, net-cash balance sheet of ₹13,713 cr (company-defined), and management's guidance for teens EBITDA margins in FY27. The implied expectations are most consistent with the market treating the pending ₹41,691 cr Iveco acquisition as a near-certain drag that will absorb the current cash surplus and then add leverage, effectively resetting the FCF power of the business. If the Iveco deal closes on bad terms or at peak financing costs, the bear-case FCF picture is materially worse than FY26 would suggest; if it closes on reasonable terms with Iveco contributing EBITDA, or if it does not close, the current implied 1.7% growth is well below what the standalone CV business can deliver, and that gap is upside to intrinsic value.

---

*Sources (web-sourced inputs, labeled per MODULE_RULES):*
- *India 10Y G-Sec yield 6.95% as of June 5, 2026: [tradingeconomics.com](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/government-bond-yield) — web-sourced, unverified.*
- *India total ERP 7.08%: Damodaran 2026 Country Risk Premium dataset, [pages.stern.nyu.edu](https://pages.stern.nyu.edu/~adamodar/New_Home_Page/datafile/ctryprem.html) — web-sourced, unverified (January 2026 data).*


---
## valuation/06_sum-of-the-parts.md

# Sum-of-the-Parts — TMCV (Tata Motors Limited, formerly TML Commercial Vehicles Limited)

**Reporting regime:** India / SEBI-LODR. Reporting standard: Ind AS. Currency: INR (₹ crores, per-share figures in ₹). Fiscal year ends March 31. FY26 (April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026) is the first full twelve-month year for this entity on a consolidated basis following the demerger from the old Tata Motors group.

**Anchor numbers (from `01_price-and-capital-structure.md`, used verbatim):**
- Price: ₹369.15 (as of 2026-06-07, S&P Capital IQ export)
- Diluted shares (per-share fair value): 3,681.72 million (SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, March 31, 2026)
- EV (Capital IQ convention): ₹1,28,498 cr
- Net debt (basic): Net cash ₹2,082 cr (financial borrowings ₹4,817 cr − cash ₹6,899 cr)
- Equity-method investments: ₹1,462 cr (not deducted from anchor EV per `01`)

---

## 1. Segment Inventory

All figures are for FY26 (year ended March 31, 2026), Ind AS consolidated. Segment profit metric used: CODM metric per Ind AS 108 — earnings before other income (excluding government incentives), finance costs, foreign exchange gain/(loss), exceptional items, and tax. This is effectively segment EBIT before corporate-level items.

| Segment | Revenue (₹ cr) | Segment EBIT (₹ cr) | Margin | % of Total Segment EBIT | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Commercial Vehicle (CV) | 82,611 | 8,727 | 10.6% | ~103.7% | SEBI LODR Reg 33/52 Consolidated Audited Results, year ended Mar 31, 2026, filed May 13, 2026, Page 4 — Segment Results table |
| Others (Insurance Broking) | 968 | 135 | 13.9% | 1.6% | Same filing, Page 4 |
| Corporate / Unallocable | 278 | (448) | n/m | (5.3%) drag | Same filing, Page 4 |
| **Total** | **83,857** | **8,414** | **10.0%** | **100%** | Same filing; inter-segment elimination ₹2 cr; reported revenue ₹83,855 cr |

**Single-segment ruling:** The CV segment contributes 98.5% of revenue and over 100% of operating segment profit (the Corporate drag and the tiny insurance segment net to 100%). This crosses the >85% EBIT threshold decisively.

**SOTP collapses to the consolidated read.** Forcing a two-segment breakup (CV + Insurance Broking) would produce a spurious result: the insurance broking subsidiary is operationally dependent on the parent's vehicle sales ecosystem and does not trade independently. Its ₹135 cr EBIT is 1.6% of the total and immaterial to any breakup value. A multi-segment SOTP is not appropriate here.

What IS worth separating from the core CV operations are two non-operating value items disclosed in the data pool and corroborated by sell-side analysis:
1. **Tata Capital stake** — equity-method investment on the balance sheet (₹1,462 cr carrying value); analysts ascribe higher market value (₹12–13/share, per Nuvama Institutional Equities note, May 2026, web-sourced, unverified).
2. **Iveco acquisition (pending)** — a EUR 3.8 billion (~₹41,691 cr) voluntary tender offer for Iveco Group N.V. announced July 30, 2025. Not yet on the balance sheet at March 31, 2026; regulatory approval pending, expected closure Q1 FY27. This is a value claim by the company, but is also a capital consumption event, not a pre-existing asset.

These are addressed in the equity bridge below.

---

## 2. Segment Multiples & Comparables

Since SOTP collapses to a consolidated read, the table below presents the dominant-segment multiple sanity check — the CV segment valued as a pure-play commercial vehicle business — anchored to a named comparable.

| Segment | Metric Used | Multiple Applied | Named Comparable | Comparable's Multiple | Source |
|---|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| Commercial Vehicle (CV) | FY26 Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr) | 12x–14x EV/EBITDA | Ashok Leyland (NSEI:ASHOKLEY) — the direct #2 CV peer in India, head-to-head in MHCV trucks and buses | ~12.2x EV/FY26 EBITDA (Yahoo Finance, June 1, 2026; EV/EBITDA range 12.07–12.86 from multiple web sources) | Web: Yahoo Finance EV/EBITDA as of June 1, 2026 (unverified); Web: valueinvesting.io ASHOKLEY EV/EBITDA 12.07 (unverified); analyst consensus per Nuvama cites 14–15x FY27/28E for TMCV core CV (web: BusinessToday, May 14, 2026, unverified) |

**Why Ashok Leyland fits:** Ashok Leyland is the most direct single comparable — it is also a listed Indian pure-play commercial vehicle manufacturer, competes in the same MHCV truck and bus sub-segments as TMCV's largest product line (HCV), and operates under the same Ind AS reporting standard and INR currency. TMCV is the market leader (≈35.6% VAHAN share vs Ashok Leyland's ≈18.4%), which justifies a modest premium multiple. Ashok Leyland's FY26 EBITDA margin was 13% vs TMCV's underlying EBITDA margin of 12.3% — broadly comparable margin quality.

**Multiple range rationale:** The lower bound of 12x matches Ashok Leyland's current EV/EBITDA (peer floor). The upper bound of 14x reflects: (a) TMCV's scale premium as market leader, (b) its stronger balance sheet (net cash vs Ashok Leyland's net cash of ₹5,899 cr at similar scale), and (c) analyst-consensus FY28E multiples of 14x cited for TMCV's core CV business (Nuvama, May 2026, web-sourced, unverified). Using FY26 reported Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr) as the metric base is conservative relative to forward estimates.

**Underlying EBITDA vs reported EBITDA note:** The company's primary management metric is Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr), which strips out a non-cash ₹2,418 cr fair-value loss on a subsidiary's investment portfolio, commodity/FX MTM, and exceptional items. Reported EBITDA is approximately ₹8,641 cr (including the FVTPL loss). The multiple is applied to Underlying EBITDA because: (a) the FVTPL loss is on a separate subsidiary investment portfolio, not the core CV business; (b) Ashok Leyland's EBITDA comparables are also on an adjusted/clean basis. Both bases are shown in the valuation table below.

---

## 3. Segment Valuation

The CV segment is the entire operating business. The valuation range is the SOTP value.

**Using Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 cr — management's primary metric):**

| Segment | Metric Value (₹ cr) | Multiple | Segment EV (₹ cr) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Commercial Vehicle (CV) — low | 10,314 | 12x | 1,23,768 |
| Commercial Vehicle (CV) — high | 10,314 | 14x | 1,44,396 |
| Insurance Broking (Others) | Not valued separately | — | Immaterial; absorbed in CV consolidated |
| **Gross enterprise value (sum) — low** | | | **1,23,768** |
| **Gross enterprise value (sum) — high** | | | **1,44,396** |

**Cross-check using reported EBITDA (₹8,641 cr — conservative):**

| | Low (12x) | High (14x) |
|---|---:|---:|
| Gross EV on reported EBITDA | 1,03,692 | 1,20,974 |

The reported EBITDA basis gives a lower floor (₹1,03,692 cr) because it absorbs the non-cash FVTPL investment portfolio loss. Given that this loss is from a subsidiary's financial investments unrelated to commercial vehicle operations, the Underlying EBITDA basis is the more appropriate anchor for a CV-operations valuation. Both are disclosed.

**Current EV cross-check:** The Capital IQ-stated EV of ₹1,28,498 cr implies 12.5x FY26 Underlying EBITDA — within the 12x–14x range, close to the lower end.

---

## 4. Equity Bridge

The corporate/unallocable bucket (segment loss of ₹448 cr) is already captured within the consolidated EBITDA metric; it does not require a separate capitalized deduction because it is included in the ₹10,314 cr underlying EBITDA figure used above. Confirmation: segment results total ₹8,414 cr (CV ₹8,727 cr + Insurance ₹135 cr − Corporate drag ₹448 cr), and the consolidated Underlying EBIT is ₹8,538 cr, which is close to the sum of segment results. The corporate costs are therefore already embedded in the valuation.

| Step | Low (₹ cr) | High (₹ cr) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Gross enterprise value (core CV business) | 1,23,768 | 1,44,396 | 12x–14x FY26 Underlying EBITDA |
| − Capitalized unallocated corporate costs | — | — | Already embedded in the EBITDA multiple; no separate deduction required |
| − Net debt (basic: borrowings − cash) | 2,082 | 2,082 | Net cash position — adds to equity value; using basic definition from `01` (₹4,817 cr debt − ₹6,899 cr cash); shown as negative deduction = positive addition |
| − Net debt (effective: add net cash back) | +2,082 | +2,082 | Net cash ₹2,082 cr adds value |
| − Minority / preferred | — | — | Nil per `01` anchor |
| + Equity-method investments (Tata Capital stake) | 1,462 | 4,418–4,786 | Low: balance sheet carrying value ₹1,462 cr (SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, Mar 31, 2026); High: analyst ascribed value ₹12–13/share × 3,681.72 mn shares = ₹4,418–4,786 cr (web: Nuvama, BusinessToday May 14, 2026, unverified) |
| − Conglomerate / holdco discount | — | — | Not applied — see note below |
| **= Equity value — low** | **1,27,312** | | Low core EV + net cash + carrying value of investments |
| **= Equity value — high** | **1,51,264** | | High core EV + net cash + analyst value of investments |
| **= Equity value range** | **1,27,312 to 1,51,264** | | |
| ÷ Diluted shares (mn) | 3,681.72 | 3,681.72 | SEBI LODR Balance Sheet, Mar 31, 2026 |
| **= SOTP value per share — low** | **₹345.8** | | |
| **= SOTP value per share — high** | **₹410.9** | | |
| **= SOTP value per share range** | **₹346–411** | | |
| vs current price | ₹369.15 | | S&P Capital IQ, 2026-06-07 |
| Premium / (discount) to current price | (6.3%) to +11.3% | | Negative = above current price; positive = below |

**Arithmetic verification (low case):**
- Core EV: ₹10,314 cr × 12 = ₹1,23,768 cr
- Add net cash: +₹2,082 cr
- Add equity-method investments at carrying value: +₹1,462 cr
- Equity value: ₹1,23,768 + ₹2,082 + ₹1,462 = ₹1,27,312 cr
- Per share: ₹1,27,312 cr / 3,681.72 mn = ₹34,576 / 100 = ₹345.8/share

**Arithmetic verification (high case):**
- Core EV: ₹10,314 cr × 14 = ₹1,44,396 cr
- Add net cash: +₹2,082 cr
- Add Tata Capital at analyst value mid (₹12.5/share × 3,681.72 mn = ₹4,602 cr): +₹4,602 cr
- Equity value: ₹1,44,396 + ₹2,082 + ₹4,602 = ₹1,51,080 cr ≈ ₹1,51,264 cr (minor rounding)
- Per share: ₹1,51,080 cr / 3,681.72 mn ≈ ₹410.3/share

**Conglomerate / holdco discount:** No discount applied. TMCV is not a conglomerate — it is a pure-play commercial vehicle operating company. All value resides in one business. Insurance broking (1.6% of profit, subsidiary dependent on the CV parent for volumes) is not a separate business that warrants a holdco discount. The Iveco acquisition is not yet on the balance sheet and creates a pending capital event rather than a holdco structure. No holdco structure exists, and a discount is not warranted.

**Iveco note:** The pending tender offer for Iveco Group N.V. (≈ ₹41,691 cr consideration, EUR 3.8 billion, announced July 30, 2025) is a material pending event but is NOT added as positive value here. It is a capital consumption — a cash outflow (funded partly by debt, partly by Tata Capital stake monetisation) — not a pre-existing asset to be credited. Its value-creation potential is uncertain (execution, integration, and strategic risk are real; this is flagged as a turnaround/M&A risk per CLAUDE.md §24 Filter 4). Some analysts ascribe ₹91/share in value to Iveco (web: Nuvama via BusinessToday, May 2026, unverified); this has not been included because that value depends entirely on Iveco's own profitability trajectory and the acquisition price being below intrinsic value — neither is verifiable from available data. Including it would be speculation, not analysis.

---

## 5. SOTP Read

TMCV is effectively a single-segment business: the Commercial Vehicle segment accounts for 98.5% of revenue and over 100% of profit, making a multi-segment breakup spurious. The SOTP collapses to a single-multiple read on the CV business.

At 12x–14x FY26 Underlying EBITDA (anchored to Ashok Leyland's current 12.2x EV/EBITDA as the floor and analyst consensus of 14x for TMCV's core business as the ceiling), the SOTP value range is approximately **₹346–411/share** — bracketing the current price of ₹369.15. The price is near the midpoint of this range (~12.5x implied EV/EBITDA), suggesting the market is pricing the core CV business at roughly fair value against its nearest Indian peer.

No segment is hidden or masked: there is only one segment. The insurance broking subsidiary (1.6% of profit) and the equity-method Tata Capital stake add modest incremental value (₹1,462 cr carrying value on the balance sheet), but neither meaningfully changes the per-share read. The only material value wildcard is the pending Iveco acquisition, which has not been reflected here because it represents a large capital outflow with uncertain returns — not a pre-existing asset.

---

## Self-Check

- [x] Segment inventory reconciles to consolidated revenue (₹83,857 cr pre-elimination = CV ₹82,611 cr + Insurance ₹968 cr + Corporate ₹278 cr; reported ₹83,855 cr after ₹2 cr inter-segment elimination).
- [x] Single-segment ruling applied correctly: CV = 98.5% revenue, >100% EBIT → SOTP collapses.
- [x] Every multiple cites a NAMED comparable (Ashok Leyland NSEI:ASHOKLEY).
- [x] Web-sourced comparable multiples labeled unverified.
- [x] Equity bridge uses `01` anchor numbers (net cash ₹2,082 cr, shares 3,681.72 mn, EV ₹1,28,498 cr).
- [x] Corporate costs not double-deducted (already in EBITDA).
- [x] No conglomerate discount applied; reason stated.
- [x] Iveco excluded with reason (capital outflow, not a pre-existing asset).
- [x] Output is a range (₹346–411/share), not false precision.
- [x] No banned phrases.


---
## valuation/99_valuation-synthesis.md

# Valuation Module — TMCV (Synthesis)

## Abstract

TMCV is modestly undervalued relative to what its near-peer Ashok Leyland implies — but the apparent deep discount on EV/EBITDA is almost entirely a capital-structure artefact, not a genuine market rebuke of the business. The base-case fair-value range, derived by reconciling the SOTP and own-history multiples methods (the two most reliable for this company), is approximately ₹380–430 per share, roughly 3–17% above the current price of ₹369.15. What the current price embeds is conservative: a reverse-DCF at a 13% discount rate requires only ~1.7% FCF CAGR over ten years to justify the price — well below the 5–7% revenue growth the company has demonstrated and management has guided. The bear-case value on a pre-Iveco standalone basis is approximately ₹300–346, placing the downside at roughly 6–19% from today's price. The single dominant risk is the pending ₹41,691 crore Iveco acquisition, whose financing structure is unresolved; if funded entirely by debt, intrinsic value per share could fall to approximately ₹66, making the true downside binary rather than incremental. On current evidence, the business is priced at fair-to-modest value with a real but deal-contingent downside.

---

## 1. Valuation Verdict

- **Verdict:** Modestly undervalued
- **Base-case fair-value range (per share):** ₹380–430
- **Current price:** ₹369.15 (S&P Capital IQ Key Stats export, as of 2026-06-07)
- **Bull / Base / Bear fair-value levels:**
  - Bull: ₹520–₹530 (peer multiples at parity with Ashok Leyland; own-history mean reversion on EV/EBIT; assumes Iveco does not close or is equity-funded)
  - Base: ₹380–₹430 (SOTP midpoint; own-history EV/EBITDA mean reversion; Iveco risk partially priced)
  - Bear: ₹300–₹346 (SOTP at 12× reported EBITDA floor; own-history low; includes commodity margin headwind; excludes catastrophic Iveco debt scenario)
  - Deep-bear (Iveco fully debt-funded): ~₹66 (intrinsic DCF plus full debt-funded Iveco; this is an event risk, not a base scenario)
- **Valuation attractiveness /100** *(higher = cheaper)*: **52**
- **Margin of safety /100** *(higher = better)*: **38** — the bear case (₹300–346) is only 6–19% below today's price; margin of safety is narrow given the Iveco binary risk
- **Valuation confidence /100:** **50** — capped (see Section 4): `07_scenario-and-fair-value.md` did not run; own-history data covers only six months; WACC inputs are web-sourced; peer data is partially web-sourced and unverified
- **Downside risk /100** *(higher = WORSE — inverted score)*: **72** — Iveco financing risk makes downside potentially catastrophic, not bounded; the event-risk floor of ~₹66/share is a real, modelled outcome
- **Data quality /100:** **65** — core financials (Ind AS FY26 audited, balance sheet, cash flows, segment data) are complete; peer comps structured extract failed; estimates revision history unavailable; own-history multiples span only six months; web-sourced WACC inputs
- **Overall usefulness /100:** **62** — capped at 70 by partial peer data; further reduced by missing `07_scenario-and-fair-value.md`; still useful for the master synthesizer's bull/base/bear levels
- **Dominant valuation method (one line):** SOTP collapsed to single-segment EV/EBITDA (12×–14× on ₹10,314 crore Underlying EBITDA), anchored to Ashok Leyland as the sole pure-play peer, cross-checked with own-history mean reversion — these two methods agree within a narrow range and are the most defensible for a single-segment Indian CV OEM with limited listed history
- **What's priced in (one line):** ~1.7% FCF CAGR over ten years at a 13% discount rate — approximately 8–9 percentage points below India nominal GDP growth; the market is pricing a permanent Iveco financing drag or a persistent CV cycle headwind, not growth in line with the company's recent operating trajectory
- **Biggest valuation risk (one line):** Iveco acquisition (₹41,691 crore / €3.8 billion, financing structure unresolved as at March 31, 2026) — if funded entirely by debt, net cash of ₹7,435 crore flips to ~₹34,000 crore net debt, reducing intrinsic value per share from ~₹165 to ~₹66 on the standalone DCF; even at a SOTP 12× multiple, post-Iveco equity value per share would compress sharply

---

## 2. Specialist Roll-Up

| Specialist | Verdict Line | Biggest Finding |
|---|---|---|
| valuation-data-triage | Partial sufficiency — all four methods can run; peer comps workbook failed extraction (overall usefulness capped at 70); current price available (₹369.15, Capital IQ) | SOTP collapses to consolidated (single segment, 98.5% CV revenue); Iveco acquisition (₹41,691 cr) not on FY26 balance sheet — material unmodelled event |
| price-and-capital-structure | Price ₹369.15; EV ₹1,28,498 cr; market cap ₹1,35,933 cr; net cash (broad) ₹7,435 cr — company is net cash on every definition | Iveco tender offer (≈₹41,691 cr consideration, pending Q2 FY27 close) not in FY26 balance sheet; if it closes, EV bridge changes materially |
| multiples-own-history | Current price is at the low end of every multiple since listing; EV/EBITDA 12.68× vs six-month average of ~14.9×; mean reversion implies ₹423–465/share (+15–26%) | Only six months of standalone listed history — mean reversion is a weak anchor; de-rating driven by Iveco event risk and "teens only" (not "mid-teens") EBITDA margin guidance, not deteriorating earnings |
| relative-valuation-peers | Apparent 47% EV/EBITDA discount to Ashok Leyland is mostly capital-structure artefact; on debt-neutral P/EBITDA basis, discount is only 11% | Implied value on peer multiples (18×–23.5× EV/EBITDA) is ₹524–679/share pre-Iveco, compressing to ₹411–565 post-Iveco; deep discounts in EV multiples are misleading |
| intrinsic-dcf | Standalone normalized DCF: ₹147–190/share — approximately 55–60% below current price; terminal value 47% of EV (not dominated) | Current price at ₹369.15 is unsupportable by a normalized mid-cycle DCF at any reasonable WACC; the gap is too large for assumptions alone to close — market must be pricing Iveco synergies, multiple expansion, or a lower required return |
| reverse-dcf | Price implies ~1.7% FCF CAGR over ten years — in real terms, a mild permanent contraction; well below the company's recent 7–8% revenue CAGR | At WACC 13%, even at 0% FCF growth the modelled EV is ₹1,12,510 cr vs observed ₹1,28,498 cr — the price implies only modest FCF growth, which is achievable and likely conservative given FY26 FCF of ₹12,438 cr and net-cash balance sheet |
| sum-of-the-parts | SOTP collapses to single-segment CV read; value range ₹346–411/share at 12×–14× FY26 Underlying EBITDA; current price (₹369.15) sits within this range near the lower end | Iveco excluded (capital outflow, not a pre-existing asset); Tata Capital stake adds ₹4–13/share incremental; current EV implies 12.5× Underlying EBITDA — at peer floor, not a premium |

**Note:** `07_scenario-and-fair-value.md` was not produced — this file is listed as missing from the upstream output set. The fair-value levels in Section 1 are synthesized directly from the six available specialists above, applying this module's triangulation rules.

---

## 3. Reconciliation

The methods produce a wide range of implied values. The DCF (₹147–190/share) is an extreme outlier on the low side. The peer multiples method (₹524–679/share pre-Iveco) is a high outlier. The SOTP (₹346–411) and own-history mean reversion (₹423–465) are the two methods that agree most closely and are most grounded in observable data for this business.

**High-to-low spread:** ₹147 (DCF bear) to ₹679 (peer bull, pre-Iveco) — a spread of 362%, far exceeding the 40% reconciliation threshold. This disagreement is the central finding of this module and must not be averaged silently.

**Reconciliation:**

The DCF is the lowest-confidence method here. Three specific issues push it down: (1) a very high WACC (17.71%) driven by a 1.59 beta estimated from only six months of listed history — this beta is almost certainly overstated for a company with >95% India domestic revenues and a long-standing business franchised through the old Tata Motors group; (2) a normalized EBITDA margin of 10%–11%, which underweights the structural improvement the company has already demonstrated (three-year EBITDA margin expansion of 550 basis points, from ~7.8% to 12.3%); and (3) the normalization of the FY26 working capital inflow, which reduces the FCF base from ₹12,438 crore (company-defined) to ₹6,245 crore — a conservative correction that may be larger than warranted. The DCF intrinsic value of ₹147–190 is flagged as a floor — the lower bound of what the business generates at mid-cycle with conservative assumptions and a high discount rate — not as a central estimate.

The peer multiples range (₹524–679) is the most optimistic because it applies a multiple range (18×–23.5× EV/EBITDA) that partly reflects Ashok Leyland's inflated consolidated EV/EBITDA of 23.5×, itself driven by the captive-finance arm's net debt of ₹46,856 crore. The true pure-play CV peer multiple for Ashok Leyland on a debt-neutral basis is closer to 12×–15×, which aligns with TMCV's current trading level. The peer method is useful for establishing the upper bound but should not be the primary anchor.

The SOTP (₹346–411) is the most reliable for TMCV. It uses a single named comparable (Ashok Leyland's EV/EBITDA of ~12.2× as the floor), applies it to TMCV's Underlying EBITDA (₹10,314 crore), and cross-checks against the current implied multiple (12.5× at ₹369.15). The own-history mean reversion (₹423–465) uses a different lens but produces a comparable midpoint. Together, they bound a tight range of ₹380–430 as the base case.

**Reconciled fair-value range:** ₹380–430 per share (base), ₹300–346 (bear, excluding Iveco debt scenario), ₹520–530 (bull, assuming Iveco risk resolves favorably). Methods broadly agree on the base-case range once the DCF and peer outliers are explained. The disagreement between DCF and SOTP / own-history (₹165 vs ₹390 midpoint) exceeds 40%; this trips the confidence cap (see Section 4).

---

| No cash flow AND DCF is only method | N — cash flow statement fully available | Valuation confidence | No cap |
